great for short girls! These are SO flattering for short girls. Bought the Medium Short in both Thin and Thick materials (I am almost 4’11”). TRUE short pants are hard to come by that don’t look bad - these are so flattering. The waist is tighter, which I like. If you are looking for a more loose fit in waist, size up. I like the Thin-Black pair most. I think that the Thick- will wrinkle more in the lower stomach area. Both are extremely comfortable. PSA: The THIN and THICK materials have different button-fastenings. Thick has a true button on the front. Thin has a fasten-hook on the inside. The true-button on the thick material may need you to sew on the button better.

Stylish Khaki Trousers These wide-leg high-waisted pants are a fun addition to my wardrobe. They can be dressed up or down, and they're both stylish and comfortable. I love the khaki color for a moody, neutral look—perfectly paired with black shoes, a black top, and a black belt. The fabric is thick but lightweight, feeling high-quality without being heavy. The generously sized front pockets and pleats give my narrow waist and hips a curvier appearance, which I appreciate. I'm 5'2"and the length is just right for me. I can wear them with heels or flats, and I like that they touch the ground when I wear flats.

Great fit and very comfortable I was looking for more work clothes that are fall colors and love the wide leg trouser look and decided to order these pants. I am 5’ 10” and weigh 145 lb and I ordered the medium long. These are the right fit and length, and are very flattering. The material is not too thick nor too thin and I expect to be able to wear these year round. There are four deep pockets that will easily hold my large phone and other essentials. Overall, these trousers are very flattering and the sizing guide was very accurate. The material is very comfortable and these pants are not too thick nor too thin. They are great trousers for work.

Baggy but good for work I like these pants a lot, however they are baggier than I thoughy they would be but I like it. If you’re not into baggy pants then don’t get these. I like the material and feel of them and there’s some stretch to them (95% Polyester, 5% Spandex), and I love the color. I’m tall (5’9”) so I thought about ordering the medium-tall size, but something told me these would be long enough in the regular size so I went with that and they are perfect, hitting right below my ankles. These pants are so cute with a fitted top and heels, but I wouldn’t wear anything that’s flowy or baggy on top with these. Overall I really like them and I will definitely get wear out of them. They seem well made and are comfortable.

Super comfy and warm! These sweats are exactly as described. The material is a slick feel on the outside that I am not familiar with but they are definitely fleeced lined on he inside giving that warm feeling. I wore them with an oversized sweatshirt and sneakers and even though I was in a baggy look; I felt super cute and comfortable. My inseam is a 33. For reference. I am in the process of losing prednisone weight but still deal with an arthritic inflammatory disease. I’m 5’8 and 188#;. Longer legs with a shorter torso, a perfect fit is large or size US 12. Weight is mostly in tummy area now; 13# left to go. I still go one size up for extra looseness in clothing but it’s almost too big now to do so. Shoe size is about a 7 wide when inflamed. 7 medium when normal. US shoe size 7-8 is about normal depending on shoe. FYI: online purchases I try to keep rating simple. If I was in a local store and could hold and view it; 5 star would buy and take it home, one star would put it back on shelf and pick something else.#OnlyJesusSaves.

Petite friendly! Comfy and flattering! Are you really short and hate having to hem pants? Are you slim thicc or carry your weight on your lower half? These are for you!! I’m 5’0” and fluctuate between 110-115. I ordered these in 29” XS and they fit like a GLOVE. No weird baggy crotch and THE perfect length to wear with my gym shoes without dragging. Fits my waist AND my large rear end… I think my butt looks cute in these but since I picked a light color, I can see dimples… I imagine this would not be an issue with darker shades. To be totally fair, I have not yet worn these extensively so I will update later if need be but I had to hop on here and let the really short ladies know to order these!!! I DREAD having to take stuff to the tailor and always pray I can find a pair that fits in the waist and in length… Hope this helps! **Updated original review to add pic of the back of the pants. Still loving these pants! I wash with detergent (no fabric softener) and hang them to dry!

Oh my goodness… First of all, the important part is I am 5’10” & 120lbs (in those photos), I bought a medium, tall, the 33” inseam, in white, khaki and black. And I am only wearing flats. Posting several pics to see the fit, plus for proof its not possible to take a bad pic in Europe. Anyway, I bought these to wear on our three week trip. I wanted something I could beat up but still look dressy & fabulous. Since we had never been to Europe and only brought a backpack each, I was really nervous about my clothes. But let me tell you, I would buy these pants all over again. I honestly felt like I one upped all the younger, high fashion girls in Milan and Capri. Also, my husband had me vacuum seal my clothes in my backpack. So they were (neatly) crammed into a super tight bag for three weeks. They went through six flights, unknown bus and trains and multiple ABNBs. Every time I pulled them out, they were wrinkle free. They are so soft, I actually slept in them, several times. They STILL do not have stretched out knees. They cleaned up super easy and air dried every where we went (cuz they don’t have dryers in Europe). I wore the WHITE in the RAIN on the streets of ROME (i feel really spoiled saying that) and figured that would be the ultimate test. I honestly figured I would be throwing them away when we got back. After 19,000 steps in the rain, the bottoms where they hit my tennis shoe heel had some discoloration after I washed them. But there has been no shrinkage and I still wear them. I wanted a loose, classy, palazzo look and the medium, tall did exactly that. I bought the small, tall and it was too tight on my bottom for what I wanted, so I gave them to my daughter who is about five pounds smaller. But the smalls did fit. The medium doesn’t feel like they are going to fall off, I felt confident in them everywhere we went. And the hips don’t look like they aren’t cut for a girl my size. I’ve purchased much more expensive pants and didn’t get 1/2 of what I am getting out of these. My only wish is they made them with a sewn in front seam and a non-yoga unwrapped waste for a more professional look. If they did, I’d probably buy the company.

Fits my waist AND my large rear end… I think my butt looks cute in these but since I picked a light color, I can see dimples… I imagine this would not be an issue with darker shades. To be totally fair, I have not yet worn these extensively so I will update later if need be but I had to hop on here and let the really short ladies know to order these!!! I DREAD having to take stuff to the tailor and always pray I can find a pair that fits in the waist and in length… Hope this helps! **Updated original review to add pic of the back of the pants. Still loving these pants! I wash with detergent (no fabric softener) and hang them to dry!

Oh my goodness… First of all, the important part is I am 5’10” & 120lbs (in those photos), I bought a medium, tall, the 33” inseam, in white, khaki and black. And I am only wearing flats. Posting several pics to see the fit, plus for proof its not possible to take a bad pic in Europe. Anyway, I bought these to wear on our three week trip. I wanted something I could beat up but still look dressy & fabulous. Since we had never been to Europe and only brought a backpack each, I was really nervous about my clothes. But let me tell you, I would buy these pants all over again. I honestly felt like I one upped all the younger, high fashion girls in Milan and Capri. Also, my husband had me vacuum seal my clothes in my backpack. So they were (neatly) crammed into a super tight bag for three weeks. They went through six flights, unknown bus and trains and multiple ABNBs. Every time I pulled them out, they were wrinkle free. They are so soft, I actually slept in them, several times. They STILL do not have stretched out knees. They cleaned up super easy and air dried every where we went (cuz they don’t have dryers in Europe). I wore the WHITE in the RAIN on the streets of ROME (i feel really spoiled saying that) and figured that would be the ultimate test. I honestly figured I would be throwing them away when we got back. After 19,000 steps in the rain, the bottoms where they hit my tennis shoe heel had some discoloration after I washed them. But there has been no shrinkage and I still wear them. I wanted a loose, classy, palazzo look and the medium, tall did exactly that. I bought the small, tall and it was too tight on my bottom for what I wanted, so I gave them to my daughter who is about five pounds smaller. But the smalls did fit. The medium doesn’t feel like they are going to fall off, I felt confident in them everywhere we went. And the hips don’t look like they aren’t cut for a girl my size. I’ve purchased much more expensive pants and didn’t get 1/2 of what I am getting out of these. My only wish is they made them with a sewn in front seam and a non-yoga unwrapped waste for a more professional look. If they did, I’d probably buy the company.

The G4Free AiryBlend Wide Leg Sweatpants are well made and very soft and comfortable. I really like the fabric, it has a nice drape to it that makes the fit very flattering and it doesn’t show every little bump and bulge. The fabric has a very slight shine that elevates the look of these pants. All the seams are neatly finished both inside and out. These sweatpants are a step up from your average sweatpants and to me, they seem more refined and slightly dressier. You could wear these pants anywhere. They are not just for the gym. There is a cinch cord at the waist that works so much better than the usual ties. The cinch cord allows you to quickly and easily adjust the size of the waist and it holds firmly in place. The pants are just the right fit, not too loose or too baggy. I ordered the 31" inseam and the pair I received is actually a 32" inseam. I like that these come in regular and tall sizes because we all know that one size does not fit all.For reference, I am 5’9” and I weigh 195 lbs. I normally wear an XL and I ordered these in an XL. These pants fit me well but the waist does seem a little smaller and tighter than my usual XL sweatpant's waist. But they are still comfortable enough to wear all day. The length for me was perfect and they don’t require any hemming. These sweatpants are well made, look great and feel great.

I love the length I probably could have done the medium though.

Good Quality, Well-Made, Will Keep You Warm & Stylish! At 69, and after a long illness that left me with no meat on my bones, I'm freezing when it's below 75°F. I got these fleeced-lined pants to survive Vegas winters - yes, it gets cold here too! These arrived faster than my need for an extra blanket, well-packaged and looking like they could take on snowstorm (or at least a stiff desert breeze).First impression: the pants are excellent quality, with strong stitching and soft fleece lining that'll keep you toasty through autumn and winter. The straight leg design is a dream - comfortable, stylish, and not too tight (although I could've gone for a snugger fit with all the weight lost - working on getting that back, thiugh!).Two back pockets? Fake, but hey, they make my backside look great! Two deep side pockets? Real and big enough for my phone, keys, and maybe even a snack or two. These pants can go from grocery run to fancy dinner without missing a beat, making me look trendy and pulled together, no matter what the occasion.At 5'4" and feeling more like a shrimp than ever (hovering around 100lbs), this 31" insem is perfect - I've got long legs, and these pants know how to show them off. All in all, they're worth every penny for the warmth, comfort, and versatility they bring. Highly recommended for anyone - no matter your size or how cold you get when the thermostat dips below "mild."

So comfy.. and works for any height! For starters, I’m super excited the 33 inch inseam is actually long enough for tall girls! I am 5‘10“ tall and almost never find pants on Amazon that go to the ground. These are really comfortable a nice medium weight and really soft lining, which also makes them hide lumps and bumps better than most lounge pants. There are seams and pockets, almost make them look like a slack, but personally, I think the material gives them away as an athletic style pants instead. I plan to wear mine when I am working remote in case I stand up on a video call for some reason. For reference, I ordered a size medium in the 33” inseam and they fit perfectly. I am 5’10”, as I mentioned previously, 167lbs, 29” waist, 40” hips and typically wear either a medium or large or around a size 8 or 29/30 in pants.

Leisure Wear Only Got these royal blue leggings to wear to watch Football on Sundays, but they’re just a miss. They are soft. However, they are thin enough to show off cellulite and could be see through depending on your undergarments and how stretched the fabric is. The size Small has too much extra fabric in the crotch area for my 122lb body = camel toe if I put my legs together. This is immediately out of the package, before they get stretched out, so it would only get worse. This is really minor, but the pockets are already starting to flip out away from the body, right out of the package. I’ve bought other $20ish leggings from Amazon that have been great but they didn’t have the color I wanted. Could keep these for lounging around the house in or wearing as long johns, but I know I’d get annoyed with the inevitable sagging. So, I’ll be returning them.

The PERFECT yoga pants! These are THE perfect yoga pants. I own 7 pair (2 being the Capri pants). From home to gym, these pants are great! No see-through, no fade, perfect fit, love the pockets. I love how it hugs my tummy. I believe it says line dry, but I have no issues putting them in the dryer on low heat. I recently lost 50 lbs in 4 months going from 170 lbs to 120 lbs. I’m 5’4” in height. When I was 170 lbs size Large fit best. I’m currently in a size Medium at 120 lbs, but I can tell that they are getting slightly big and I may need to size down to Small. Hope this helps! You’re making a great purchase!

Soft, great fit, good coverage & form-fitting pockets They look great on me and are really soft. I’m 5’4 and I range between 115-120 lbs. I didn’t have high expectations. They fit perfect, but are slightly on the smaller side, so before the wash — they seem perfect, but I am worried about them getting slightly smaller after the wash/dryer. They would probably still fit, but this is to keep in mind for anyone who may be the heavier side. My favorite part besides the softness / comfort is how the pockets stay in position whether you just had a lot of stuff in them or not, which is great if you don’t want that heavy pocket look for when you don’t have anything in the pockets. I’m definitely going to consider ordering more black pairs & their other colors! I haven’t tried working out in them, I just planned on wearing them around the house or to do errands outside, but they’re nice enough to wear with an outfit if you wanted to.

Super high waist, buttery soft For $10 a pair in a three pack, I’m not complaining too loudly. Pros: For a pale blue, you can barely see my black with lemon panty when I squat. But not when I’m standing which is surprising. They are so soft!! The fit is incredibly generous- I’m 5’6, just shy of 180 and have the L/XL. I could still fit another 30 pounds in here easily. No muffin top when I fold the waistband down. Good length. I have longer legs and these still hit mid-calf, which was the goal. Cons: These will snag very easily on uneven or rough surfaces. These do not stay up while running etc, but maybe just because they are not snug on me. Pilling is a guarantee. These are really made for walking to the store, working from home, being cute under an oversized sweater or hoodie. These are NOT suitable for working out. The waist comes to my bra. That’s not high waisted, that’s a catsuit! But the band folds over nicely which is how I wear them.

Too wide in hips and waist My daughter and I were debating whether to order these in my daughter’s regular size small or go down a size and order x-small. She is 5’5” 115 lbs and wears size small in jeans. According to the included chart these numbers put her in size small. However, her hip and waist measurements put her in size x-small according to the chart. Ultimately we decided to get size small because we have a couple of other pairs of jeans from this brand and size small in those fits her great. Unfortunately, these pants are too big for her in the waist and the hips. In the pictures I took the frontal view looks great but make sure to look at the back view picture too, so you can see how my daughter tightened them with a shoe lace to make them wearable. The fabric content according to the tag is 65% cotton 20% polyester 15% spandex. I’m not a fabric specialist by any means but 15% spandex? I have never seen jeans with such a high spandex content. And wouldn’t that make them very, very stretchy? These jeans however are not very stretchy. They are definitely not all cotton because they are soft for denim and have some give but I would not describe them as stretchy denim. All in all, the style is trendy, the workmanship is good, but size wise I wish they were running more true to the standard size small jeans.

Really Baggy I almost thought these pants were the wrong size. The waist in comparison to the legs is ridiculous. I wear a lot of straight leg and boyfriend jeans but these are a new level. I'm sure someone would like them but they aren't for me. I'm 5'1 and have an inseam of 25 inches. They are definitely not meant for petite girls; even with heels, they drag on the floor.

Ugh so cute and comfortable Photos don't do them justice- it's all in the movement! These pants have a heavy drape and flow beautifully like a midi skirt, while keeping my inner thighs covered for the added feeling of security pants provide, and to prevent chub rub! I ordered these in size large and they fit me perfectly. For reference, I am 5'5", and I have a 31" waist, and 45" hips. Very pear shaped with thick backside and legs. It's next to impossible to find pants or bottoms in general that fit and flatter me, and I hate showing off my legs, even in the heat of summer. These pants are the perfect solution! They're so freakin' breezy and comfortable to wear, and I love the flat waistband. (It's so hard to find a nice-fitting waistband without a drawstring!) I have machine washed these and hung to dry, with a tiny bit of a fluff up in the dryer once dry, and they didn't shrink at all. There were a few loose threads that I need to cut and fix, but I'm just so happy to have found comfy and beautiful summer pants, that I don't even care about small details like perfect quality 😂😂. I'm going to buy these in every colour, and become "that girl". (You know, "that girl who always wears those loose pants"?) Summer is going to be so much more bearable now. Yay.

No longer as good I originally bought a pair of these pants in dark gray in 11/2020. The quality was good, they’ve held up to washing, and the material was on the thicker side and a soft fleece type. I tried ordering a new pair in 10/2022 in light gray, but the pair I received seemed so much thinner than my old pair, and seemed to have a cheap/flat sherpa-esque lining (but not like the products they have that are actually advertised as sherpa—I have no complaints about Gihuo sweatshirts for example! So I suspect the sweatpants that are advertised to have sherpa lining are all right, those are just too hot for my legs). I thought maybe it was a fluke, some companies like this accidentally mail the wrong model when they have a lot of varieties. I tried ordering these pants again in 01/2023 in dark gray, to compare to the pair I already had, and brown. They definitely are not the same anymore. The new pair was much thinner in material and didn’t feel as well-made as before. The new pair also had that flat cheap fur-like lining (think like, if you got “warm” leggings from Five Below). Even the string ties seemed a flimsier material. The brown pair mysteriously seemed somewhat better made, and slightly better feeling on the inside (less fur-like, but still not as thick material as the old dark gray pair I have). I’m pretty disappointed honestly, and wish I just got multiple pairs earlier. I would not recommend these pants as they are now. Gihuo’s sherpa-lined sweatshirts are great, so those pants that are also sherpa-lined may be a better alternative, but I just wanted house pants for the winter and I know those will absolutely roast my legs. (Side note: I ordered these in Medium, but personally, I think Small would’ve fit better if I were keeping them. 5’2” 135 lbs, hips are around 39”)

Great Fit and Extremely Warm! Love these Sherpa Lined Pants! Bought these Light Grey Sherpa Lined Pants for my 79 year old Mother who has Neuropathy in her legs,Her legs stay cold all the time so I got these hoping that they would help. My Mother absolutely loves her new Sherpa Lined Pants and says that they definitely have helped keep her legs warm especially when she is out walking her little Dog. I was concerned with what size to buy her with all the conflicting reviews about the sizing but I decided to buy her a size small and they fit her well but are a little long but since they have the elastic at the bottom of the pants it doesn't seem to be a problem for her. For information purposes on her height and weight, she is 5'0 tall and weighs 112 lbs and the size small fits her good. I was so happy to see that these pants fit her so well and are keeping her warm this Winter.

Warm! Just what I wanted for this frigid winter. Thick and REALLY well made. Zippered pockets! I ordered a large (5’6” and about 150, usually wear size 8 US) so I had room to layer leggings under. They fit perfectly. Great length, too.

Bought these for a concert. I ordered my usual size small (148# 28" waist, big thighs lol) They are very difficult to put on, but once you do they look great. As someone else suggested they go on better if you pull them up like pantyhose. They are smoothing and hide any flaws :) Once you get them on they are stretchy but have great compression. The light felt lining feels nice. They are not at all see through and seem very durable. They do seem to run a tad smaller than regular leggings so you may want to size up. I'm keeping the smalls because they suck me in more Lol. They make this almost 50 year old lady feel sexy!

They feel expensive and are perfection! I ordered these pants in black last year and loved them so much. Came across these in this merlot color and thought I would give them a try. Absolutely love them just as much as the black ones. They are controlling yet stretchy. When I wear them to work I would always catch on to the dishwasher (I bartend) and would fling me back. Never tears scratches or rips. They have a good material in the inside where it’s not leathery but more like a cloth material. It’s not a liner but just how the material on the other side is. You can flip them on the waist if u don’t want high waisted. These come up to my waist around my belly button. I’m 5’7” 135 pounds and these are a small. Update: love them so much had to add the brown pair to my collection. I have the black red navy blue and brow. I bartend in these and always get caught in corners and slingshot back with no scratches or tears. So impressed and get a ton of compliments. As stretchy as they are the compression is impressive. My co workers are all different sizes and shapes and love theirs just as much! Worth every single penny. 🤎🤎🤎🤎🤎🤎 Another update; bought them in green too. Love them! They’re the most comfortable brand of “leather” leggings. My only complaint would be the waist area gets sweaty but no other places. Im more than ok with that. So many compliments. I hear people spend near $100 for the spanx brand. I haven’t bought them before and have no reason since these work perfectly for me.

Terrible product packaging They packaged this item like it was garbage. Balled up in a plastic sack. I started to request an immediate refund, but yge event I purchased them for was tonight so I still tried them on. They will work.....as a previous reviewer said , they are short. I am 5'2 150lbs and purchased a small. I probably could have worn a medium comfortable. They should be less expensive considering how the vendor ships them......

Nice quality, very warm. These are fleece lined, they are very warm, very thick and nice quality. They are tight, good control too, similar to spanx. The only downside was waist band was the pleather on the inside too so it’s very hot on your skin and sticks to your. I also found that they slid down a bit after walking for a while, but even my nice yoga pants do that, so I’ll let it go. Overall a really great purchase. I almost bought the$70 ones, glad I didn’t. I’m bigger in the legs. 5’4, 165lbs, thick thighs.

I have them with a tee and tennis shoes but I’m sure the black ones would look really cute with a crop top. They suck you in tight like that famous brand that’s $100 per pair. Get em if u like em!

Too pleather-y looking for me.... I really wanted to love these leggings, the reviews were hit or miss but the price was right so I just went for it. And unfortunately I made the wrong decision bc they just weren’t doin it for my 5’3 135lbs body... I ordered a medium & they were def too long (but most pants r at my height😕) and too tight in all the wrong places! Plus I noticed that the sheen/shine intensified too much while stretched against my body, so that nice matte leather look, turns into cheap plastic pleather real quick! I ended up returning them & the seller was nice enough to give me a full refund which I didn’t think twice about & I put it toward the so called, “Holy Grail” of faux leather leggings... SPANX! Of course I paid about $85+tax for them but I learned long ago that u def get what u pay for & those extra $$ are usually put toward making your ass look phenomenal! Which is always something I’m willing to pay extra for😉 lol!

Other than having to size up, awesome pants! I'm 5'5" and 125 lbs. I ordered the black color first and then returned them for the brown, so I'll start with the black. I was able to get them on, but they were really uncomfortable. Yes, they had a horrible smell. Is that a reason to give a bad review? No. The smell will dissipate in a few days or you can just wash them. It was almost gone by the second day I had them open. I personally don't feel you should listen to reviews that are negative because of a temporary smell. Okay, now for the brown pants. I returned the black and decided to order the brown in a size large. I LOVE them. They're expensive looking and soft on the inside (see pic)! They sit very comfortably on my waist aside from a teeny tiny bit of extra room in the back, but not enough for me to care. They're a tad bit squeaky between the thighs, but again, not enough for me to care. I feel like these are going to go with everything I wear this winter and I can't wait! And for whatever reason, the brown did NOT have a horrible smell :)

Fits great, very comfortable I love these jeans. I’ve tried on so many pairs lately and these fit perfectly. I’m 5 foot 10 and weigh 170 pounds and bought an 8 long.

Way better than I had expected Usually I’ll buy some fashion nova jeans in a 15 for $30 and they’ll show up a little tight but stretch out in a few hours. Few months in they’ll tear at the thigh anti-gap lol. A coworker convinced me to get jeans at Target in July. Got 3 pairs of “Levi’s” in a 14 (because they’re stretchy) for like $40/ea. Literally wearing these things to work every day because that’s the only thing I can do through this apocalypse. Some how a pair of these garbage pants ripped in a month. A MONTH. So. I’m on amazon and I see these here bad boys on sale for $9 and I figure I should honestly just buy six pairs and call it a night. Just in case though I upped it to a size 16. If they run small, they’ll fit. If they run regular, I’ll wear a belt. Well guess what. They run regular. The delivery lady Just dropped them off and I pulled one on and these bad boys are so comfortable, but I ordered them big lol. Anyway, I’ll wear a belt to work. I got two pairs but I came back to buy two more, but the sale is over, sad day, but not that sad. They seem way sturdier than the ones on fashion nova AND these pants not only have 4 1/2 Real Pockets but I can also fit my entire hand in the front ones. Heck, I can fit a couple TV remotes in the front pockets. I’m definitely coming back, and even though the $9 sale is over, full price is still a steal compared to Target’s “Levi’s” For reference I’m about 5’5 and 197lbs this week but I’m kindof dense in the bones. A lot of my weight is butt, a lot of it is thighs...these jeans don’t quite do the butt justice but that’s definitely on me because I purposely ordered them too big haha if I figure out how to update this review, I’ll edit to add pictures when I buy the right size

Dont Think This Style's Good for Petites/Shorts, Otherwise Nice Im 5'5" a little over 35.5" in waist, 45" hips, 24" diameter thighs and 16" diameter legs/calves, no thigh gap. What drew me to these pants were that they were high waisted and on a lightning deal for like 20 bucks. I didnt think much about the cut beyond the waist but figured tapered legs would be attractive. I think the deal breaker for me is the style. They look kind of like straight legs on me, and since I have large thighs it's just more fabric than I want in the leg area. I dont like how the fabric bunches behind my knee and though this isn't entirely on the jeans I don't think they're doing my bum any favors. I did go a size larger than i probably should have with the 16, I just hated the idea of the top of the jeans pinching my stomach. I also got the regular instead of the short but I dont think the tapers sharp enough for the extra length to make a difference here, meaning to give shape to my legs. I think the taper's only really attractive if your thighs arent super disproportionate to your legs. Maybe like 2 or 3 inches max wider diameter in the thighs in relation to your calves. At least for the fit I want. I was gonna send these back in exchange for some 14's but I think I'll have to go with one of the other cuts to get the fit I'm looking for. Besides the fit I think these were a good buy for the price. Nice stretch and color. not as thick as some denim blends, a bit thicker than like denim leggings. Comfortable to move around in, liked fit at the waist. Better than most things you'd find between 20 - 30. If I had to recommend based on fabric and comfort vs leg fit I'd recommend.

Fit is great, continues to look NEW Great jeans, I have purchased several times. After washing they still look NEW! At 72 it is difficult to find jeans that flatter but these definitely do! (Yes, that fashion is coming back for jeans) Enjoy your Vanderbilt’s, I know I do!

Decreasing quality I love the Amanda jeans and have worn them for years. I bought the lighter Amanda jeans from this listing two years ago, and the darker Portland Wash jeans last week. The finishing on the new pair is so much more raggedy than the older pair after many washings! The fabric content is slightly different: the lighter pair is 73% Cotton, 25% Polyester, 2% Elastane and the darker is 72% Cotton, 26% Polyester, 2% Elastane. The first is made in Jordan, the second in Kenya. I did buy two other pairs in the two year interim, and I returned them as the “fits at natural waist” was uncomfortably well above my belly button. The pairs I kept both needed to be hemmed, but I’m used to that. I’m 5’4” with a 29” inseam. The Short length is too short at 28”, and the Regular length is too long at 30”. This pair of jeans fit comfortably and the darker color is more dressy, which is what I wanted. I’m glad I have a pair I can keep!

Could be worse for the price so I’m going to keep them

Big waist gap Size 22 Plus - regular length - Black 5'2" - 235lbs - short torso - large booty I've got what I call a " booty shelf". My bum sticks out a lot, and you could almost set stuff on it. So to be fair, a lot of women my height and weight may not have this issue... The waist gap is big with these jeans. No way can I wear them. When they slide down, which they will inevitably do, they'll still show way too much booty/undies, especially if I bend over and/or my shirt rides up. The regular length is long on me, but that's not unusual. Otherwise the fit seems fine in the hips/thighs. I'm returning them, so of course I don't know how much they'll shrink in the wash. These will probably work great for people without a booty shelf. Since I can't wear them I'm giving 4 stars, but for a different shaped body they could easily be 5 star jeans.

These did not, but they were way too loose in the waist & gave me a bit of a pooch in the crotch area. Because of the softness and stretch, these pants would most likely become larger after a wash instead of shrinking. I got my normal size 16S Plus (couldn’t beat $10!), but need the 14S and they aren’t even available at all. Find them in your proper size and you won’t be disappointed 💕

Extremely comfortable but stretchy, stretchy These 22Plus, Gloria Vanderbilt jeans have a huge amount of elastic give which does not allow it be body conforming in the places you want them to be. For example, they do not help you look shapely in the tush, hips and tummy. I am 5'8, 260 pounds with a generous tummy. The jeans do not hide much tummy nor subdue hips and are very loose in the bottom side of the rear, & legs due to the stretch. Without a body shaper on they are a tad loose everywhere. With a shaper on, they are tighter but still comfortable. Yet to me, still not the most flattering look. They fit a tad bit like mom jeans in my opinion. I almost exchanged them for a 20 Plus just to get a more flattering silhouette. But I decided against it as one thing that they definitely are is the most comfortable jeans I ever wore. So, I decided to keep them as I am of the age (56) that comfort trumps style. My first designer jeans at age 14/15 in 1979 were Gloria Vanderbilts (see me in them in 3rd pic) . So buying these were like visiting an old friend that you greatly missed. If nothing else, I am decidely loyal to old friends and our fond memories.

So glad I bought these!!!! I work outside for a small delivery company( starts with an A) and wanted something to keep me warm and dry on rainy days. I am very happy with these pants. I worked 5 days last week, and I wore these for 3 of those days. The temp was 40-44F for a high and 30s at night. I wouldn’t want to wear them if it were any warmer though. But that’s just me, I get heated quickly when I am constantly moving. I work 10 hour days and deliver around 100+ packages each shift. They keep me warm and dry for sure. They are also comfortable. I am 5’5” between 145 and 150 lbs, and I am wearing the medium size. I can’t think of anything negative to say about them. I am most certainly getting me another pair to put into my work rotation.

I am 5'6", 140 lbs and got the medium. They fit perfectly.

Perfect Snow Pants! I love everything about these pants...the style, color, fabric, warmth and how well they are made. I ordered them for Alaska, but I wear these all of the time because they’re so cute.Directions say to hand wash, but I just set the washing machine to COLD delicates and then I hang them to dry.I’m 5’4” 118 pounds. I had originally ordered the xs, but had to exchange for a small. The waist was too tight for me. The small is PERFECT.Update: I was able to use these in Alaska in -25 to -40 while mushing & viewing the Northern Lights. I DID wear thermal underwear AND a synthetic base layer underneath that was also fleece lined. These pants are still great, but for extreme cold, you can’t wear them alone. I have worn them in the teens on up, without any additional layers.Update: Also wore them in Alaska in September 2021, hiking in rainy 30 degree weather. Still love them!Update: As of Nov. 2022 they’re still great! No holes & I still wear them all of the time. 👍🏼👍🏼Update: It’s January 2024 & they’re still going strong!

I have worn them in the teens on up, without any additional layers.Update: Also wore them in Alaska in September 2021, hiking in rainy 30 degree weather. Still love them!Update: As of Nov. 2022 they’re still great! No holes & I still wear them all of the time. 👍🏼👍🏼Update: It’s January 2024 & they’re still going strong!

Great waterproof winter pants! I bought these for our winter Iceland trip. They were completely waterproof and warm, and I wore them every day! For reference, i am 165lbs and wear a size 8-10 pants 5ft 6in. I purchased an XL to wear a layer underneath and they were movable and comfortable. So size up! Highly recommend these for any winter activities.

Great Pants, but runs Really BIG! I'm 5' 2" 110 lbs - ordered a size 2 (XS) in gray and they were really big (more like a large) so I had to return them. Still giving these pants 5 stars because they were well made, looked warm, but not bulky and would have been exactly what I wanted if they were a few sizes smaller. I've found that sizes can vary by color with a lot of things I've ordered from Amazon, so other colors might be sized differently. Photo shows a pair of my size 2 jeans with the pants, for size reference.

I ordered these recently and have worn them out hiking twice so far, in 25-35 F temps. They are VERY warm and I did not need long johns on underneath at those temperatures, though i suspect when it gets below 0 F I will. These are so well made, very comfortable. I am 5 ft 5.5" and 114 lbs and the small fits me well, though snug in the butt area as I have a naturally large butt. The elastic allows for a little wiggle room. The pant legs are slightly tapered but this works well for tucking these pants into boots. I also love the side pockets on the pant legs for little things like compasses to stash. I would highly recommend these! I was even comfortable enough to do a little dance improvisation and posing in these. :)

Do not recommend So I waited to write my review after actually being able to use it in the snow. (Snowboarding) 1. Not warm- I needed 2-3 layers of thermals just to wear this on a December day in Big Bear, CA. I can only imagine if wearing these higher up north. 2. The (butt) pockets have a small velcro closing, which allowed for snow to get in the pants. (Picture attached) I think it it were zipped completely, it would have held the snow out much better. Not to mention, that the lining was super thin and does not keep the snow out if Velcro is undone— which was difficult since I would fall on my butt when trying to snowboard. So I was freezing after about 1hr, on the slopes (I.E: sliding/falling on my butt) and eventually snow got it, and wet my under layers. I don’t personally recommend because the intended use for the product was not met. Otherwise, it’s aesthetically nice. The fitting was good, I’m 5ft and got a size Small, pretty long— could have went smaller if I wanted to be more snug.

Perfect I've struggled for years to find a good pair of ski pants that actually fit a plus size woman and these were it. Super comfortable, waterproof (obviously) and crazy warm! It was in the 30 - 40s on the mountain so I didn't wear a base layer and I was still so warm. For reference I am 5'6", 240, and typically wear a 16-18. Definitely recommend!

Warm and water proof Wore these while snow tubing in CO. I wore them with leggings underneath and they kept me warm and dry the whole time. I’m 5’4, 170lbs and ordered an XL because of mixed reviews but probably could have gotten away with a large. This pair was a little big on the hips and no way of tightening it. Wish they had a Velcro belt or straps to keep them up.

Don’t buy if you’re tall I’m 5’10, 140 pounds and I bought a medium for the length. They fit great at the waist, though I think I could fit a medium. These left plenty of room for another layer. However, they were 4 inches too short. So disappointing. The color is gorgeous!

Runs small These are a great Tripp pants dupe. They are made of cheap fabric, but they look the part. I bought them for a Halloween costume. I ordered size Large. But unfortunately these run small and have zero stretch to the fabric. I usually wear size XL in bottoms, so I knew Large would be a bit snug, but these fit more like size Medium to me and could barely get on. I measured 16” across the waist when laying flat to help anybody else trying to get a size comparison to a current garment they own. The leg length was great, however. They are long enough on me and I’m 5’10”. Just too bad they don’t fit my waist. Have to return.

Very impressive… I love these pants. I am 225lbs and 5’4. I maybe could’ve gone down to a large but the XL works and what you see here. There is elastic in the back. I guess I didn’t notice that.The material is a cotton linen blend. They didn’t have wash instructions. So I checked Google. (Gentle cycle cold or warm, no fabric softener, and air dry. If wrinkled you iron gently with something between the hot iron and the material).I purchased the grey and the pink purple. They had a great price for natural material. I’ve washed them and can’t wait to wear them. Yay!

Very comfortable and has pockets!! Great variety of colors to choose from. True to size, super comfortable. I love the bow tie detail on the waist and pant leg. Always important to me, it has pockets! I'm not a fan of the high waist line on me, so I ended up returning. The back side was even more unflattering than the front view. For reference: I purchased Deep Blue Haz in a size Medium. I'm 5'3, 135LBS

This style doesn’t work for short women Color is beautiful, would have worked nicely for thanksgiving. Unfortunately for me I am only 5 foot 2, so this style just doesn’t not work like I was hoping, and they look nowhere near the photos on me. Overall nice quality pants, with pockets, decent material thickness unless you have a larger behind like me, you can definitely see seams. Anyways, if you’re trying to get this style as the photos it will work beautifully if you’re tall and skinny. Haha

So Comfy I am 5’6 and 165lbs. I got the large. I wear weekly and are holding fine thru washes. No change in size. I don’t have to iron. Hide my lower belly. One of my favorite outfits.

Stretchy, comfortable dress pants! I ordered these pants in large & in the apricot color. I love them!!! They are a great color, stretchy & comfortable! Nice pockets in the front & very cute bow details. I am 5’ 9” & 170 lbs and the large was perfect!!! I would definitely recommend them!

Super cute but hear me out! I NEVER wear things like this because of my stomach pouch but I figured to broaden my horizons with a new outfit. I love it! I’m 164lbs, 5’4, and I bought an XL (fits just right, anything smaller and I wouldn’t have been able to get into it). Absolutely cute with a black shirt, BUT guys, they are super thin. I wore a bodysuit with this and you can clearly see the lines from the shirt. So one picture is stretching my butt out a little, and the other is just me standing relaxed. You can see even more if you squat. I mean… this outfit makes me pretty confident and I like it, even with the thinness to it, so I’m keeping. I even wore it out like this. It’s probably just me that overthinks the “panty lines” because it doesn’t look as good, but what can you do. I feel like that’s every outfit.

Dressy yet casual Bought these in Fuchsia to wear to my office job. Can be dressed up or down as needed. Material feels durable with a little stretch to it. The waistband in back expands or contracts for a more versatile fit. I am 5’9” and almost 150lbs and the medium is a good fit. Runs a little short on tall people, but can be remedied with a higher boot so they won’t look like high water britches.

obsessed! Ok, first off, for reference I am 5’2.5” 195lb and Igot an XL. If you want a looser fit, size up. These pants aren’t unbearably tight but they are fitted. I have hips and a bit of a butt and I had to hop to get the elastic waist over my humps BUT once i did baby these pants had the body bodying ok? Love love love these. The belt is detached which is great because as pictured, I changed mine to a stretchy belt that really helped the high rise of the pants cinch my waist in. The pants are a light material but aren’t sheet. Obviously not particularly expensive feeling but not itchy or irritating. I worked a lengthy shift on a retail job when i got them today and I was comfortable in them all night. I ended up snipping the ties at the ankle; didn’t like that feature. but overall i like them. I believe they’re worth it for the price.

AMAZING for the bigger booty girls with long torsos (: Alright ladies. If you have a larger bum/legs then these are for you. I have always wanted a pair of pants like these but the waist is always too large or the thighs/calves are too tight. However, these pants are literally perfect. I got a medium and I am 138lbs, 5’6”, pear shaped, and have a super long torso and short legs. Most high waisted pants hit me just below my belly button but these hit above! They make my torso look shorter and my legs look longer, really balancing my proportions out more. The stretch is perfect, super comfy but still has structure. And the most important part… I don’t feel uncomfortable having my booty show at my job. I work at an elementary school and often feel as though I need to cover my bum because most tighter pants really show it off lol but these are the perfect material that gives me shape but doesn’t put my whole body on display if that makes sense (they are perfectly work appropriate). Lastly, I didn’t love the bows on the ankles, since there’s already a bow on the belt, so I literally just cut them off and you wouldn’t be able to tell at all - especially from far away. Now they’re super cute cigarette style pants (: If you’re considering these pants, just try them!!! So worth it.

Great quality and overall very cute pants. I am just over 5'7"/140lbs and average build. I ordered the coffee color in size medium. These pants are so cute and nice quality. High waisted and a long stride. They hit at a great crop length. The thickness of material was great for work. Feels like a lot of normal work slacks do. They do have some stretch but holds shape well too. There is enough extra room in the size medium for me that I am going to get another color in a size small to see the difference and will update then. My biggest concern is I do have some hips and since these are only pull on with out any kind of zipper I wonder if I can get the small on.

Perfect and classy and fit short curvy girls! So I really love these pants. Very flattering on hour glass shaped women. I got them for an interview and I think they’re going to be absolutely perfect. I’m 5’1 super curvy and probably 175-185 (it’s been a while since I’ve weighed myself lol thanks quarantine 2020) and I ordered an XL in “dark green”. The dark green looked a little more teal and I was hoping it was so took the chance and was super happy I did. I took a picture outside so you could see color better. Its not like Christmas tree green it’s more like handmaidens tale wife dark green (sorry for the weird reference but ¯\_(ツ)_/¯) When pulled to be high waisted as intended they fit my legs and end at my ankles with just a little extra at the bottom so good for us short girls!! I am wearing the “Bishop” shirt from Target (available as of Jan 2021 online) and it works well for this as a crop shirt with puffy sleeves. Love that these pants have their own little bow/sash/belt! Also these are very comfortable so if you needed to have something you could wear that could survive literally running to meetings these will hold up. Felt nice enough that I’m not worried about them tearing or ripping and are comfortable enough to wear going out for brunch or what have you. Will be ordering more colors!

Good fit, good quality I read various good reviews before ordering, and I'm glad I did. I really enjoy these pants. I am 5'7" and about 185 lbs, normally a size 12 pant, and I ordered an XL. They fit really well. Maybe a bit big in the waist, but I didn't want anything smaller to be tight on my thighs/behind, so I'm really happy with the sizing. The pictures don't really do justice, and I don't love this top with these pants as much (it's a looser fit, and I need something that tucks in better), but I am so pleased with these pants. I haven't washed them yet, so I don't know how they wash, but other reviews have said they wash good. If I had one complaint, it'd be that they're a touch long for me, but I just tied the bottom bow a little tighter so they don't fall down. The length would be perfect with sandals. I also LOVE the pockets. They are big enough to put my phone in (iphone 7) AND to put my hand in at the same time. Overall, the material lays/wears in a flattering way, and I love that. I couldn't be happier with these pants.

Really adorable dress pants! Lots of compliments! Love! For reference: I am 5'5" and 135lbs. These dress pants were seriously so cute and comfy! Nice color, great fabric. I tied the bows on the ankles in the back instead of the front. The pants were the perfect length for me. No complaints at all. I bought them for a wedding. I received lots of compliments on them. I bought the dusty rose color in these dress pants too, to go with the white version of the same top I'm wearing in the picture. Mainly because I couldn't decide which color outfit I wanted to wear with the full intent of sending one set back. I kept both outfits! (Both the pants and top are sold on Amazon.) I will say, the dusty rose pants came in nicer brand name packaging with tags and the gray pair in the picture came in generic packaging but also with tags. Overall, they both were in perfect condition and I absolutely love them! I highly recommend these! I hope this helps! :)

Cute, comfy, fit great Love these! I'm 5'5", 154lbs with a bigger booty and hips. High waisted bottoms can be hit or miss for me as far as fit/being flattering but these hit the mark! I got the Large and they fit great - not tight or constricting at all (great stretch, especially in the waist) but are super comfortable and flattering. The material is nice quality, not too thin and perfect for elevating your look. It's not the softest material but definitely not itchy or anything like that. Plus, it's not nearly as much of a dog hair magnet like some of my other bottoms. I purchased the burgundy color as well!

Super cute and comfy, but… I literally just got these in the mail less than 15 minutes ago. They are very cute and can be worn in different ways. I think they fit great. I’m about 5’7 and 145 lbs and these are size medium. The only problem I’ve found so far is that one of the pockets weren’t sewed together so I’m definitely going to lose a thing a two with these lol

Sleek and easy to maintain These wash up so well, and pack in a suitcase without major wrinkles, great for traveling! I got tons of compliments on the (including from a party bus in downtown Nashville!). Can go out at night with stilettos, with my matching CTAS, or even to the office with a more formal blouse. My favorite detail is the little ties at the front of the ankles. The bows are so cute. I weight 130# 5’5” and Small was perfect.

Easy Care, Resists Wrinkling, Super Comfy Super comfy. True to size. Good stretch. Resists wrinkling (in the photo I had been sitting in them for 6 hours, and as you can see, they don't wrinkle). I am 5'7", 153 lbs and my lower body is proportionately smaller than my upper body (I'm usually a size 6 in pants, but an 8 in tops). I ordered a size MEDIUM (Apricot in color) and they fit perfect. They look more fitted in the front, but there's unused stretch in the back, for comfort. I think these pants are very forgiving and super flattering (even for someone like me who is conscious of their belly). Slight flaw in workmanship on my belt, which was misstitched, but overall, probably the best piece of clothing I've purchased from Amazon.

Super cute definitely a staple item! Well I absolutely love the pants I actually bought another pair only issue I have is sizing. I went with the medium based on the reviews. I’m 5’4 165 with thick thighs. I wear a size 6 -7 jeans. I found the pants fit very nicely in the legs and butt area but I had a lot of extra fabric in the waist and lower stomach. I feel like if I went with a small I wouldn’t of had enough room in the legs and butt and it would of been tight. Hope this helps. Again only reason I removed a star is for the sizing other than that the pants are fantastic I love the material light on a summer day but still thick enough to be dressed up. Definitely squat proof. 😄

Comfortable and fetching Comfortable, versatile, stretchy, and in my opinion flattering 👌 We really must must must post 🍑pics in reviews. I couldn’t find many and took a *lunge* of faith. I am 5’7, 190lbs, and wear 14/16 dress size. I ordered XL and they are just right. I like these. I will order more colors. I can tell from the lightness of the fabric that I will stick to darker colors to avoid cellulite center stage, as that’s not my preference. So many beautiful colors to choose from, and I’ll be ordering the mustard ones next.

Perfection and classy Soooo, to help everyone, I am a size 12/13. I buy size 12 skinny pull on jeans at Old Navy for size reference. I’m 5’4. I weight 183 pounds on the thick side. two different styles fit differently at waist I'm 5'4 and somewhere about 135-140 pounds. I carry more weight in my middle than my legs or hips. I bought the medium based on measurements and found the ruched waist too snug for comfort. I'm returning my medium to try a large instead. The hips weren't overly roomy, so I think I'll be good with the large. Will try to update! I bought these for an international flight hoping they'll be super comfy. EDIT : 5'4, 140lbs, size 6/8. I ordered a medium in the style without bows at the ankles. The waist was just too fitted for me for comfort, so I returned them for a large. I switched to the bows at the ankle style. What I didn't notice in photos, is that the ones with bows are only half ruched at the waist and the ones without have a fully ruched waist. Had I recognized that, I would have stuck with the original style, because going up one size was more like going up two with that variation. (see photos-- yellow is large, green is medium). The legs were too baggy now and I had ample room in the waist Ultimately, I'm returning both. The pants are soft and nice quality, but given what I just explained, neither fits. I bet a large in the bow-less ones would, but my trip is in 7 days and I don't want to order a third pair at this point. If you carry weight in your middle I'd say order your true size in the ones with bows, OR size up one in the ones without. They're cute pants, just not for my shape.

Not because it’s see through, it’s just a thin fabric. For me, trying to stay somewhat on trend with the “paper bag pants” trend, these were a good buy.

SO HAPPY WITH THIS PURCHASE!!❤ I'm 5'5", 118 lbs. Normally, I would go with an XS, but upon reading the measurements on their sizing chart, I decided to go with a S (my waist is about 26"). True to size IF YOU PAY ATTENTION TO THE MEASUREMENTS. These pants are gorgeous! 😍 Great quality, material is light but not see-through (so great!). They don't restrict your movement at all, as they stretch so well. Very stretchy elastic waistband so it adjusts to your own body shape perfectly. They look exactly as they are pictured, so no surprises when purchasing. You can physically remove the belt/ribbon, and switch it for another belt/scarf/ribbon. Personally, I think it looks a little strange without one given that it has 3 very-visible wide belt loops. The pockets are deep enough for a phone, keys, or regular sized wallet. I measured them: they are about 4 1/2" wide, the length from the waistband to the bottom of the pocket is 8", and from the bottom point of the diagonal opening to the bottom of the pocket, you have about 3" of full covered security (nothing should fall out). Overall, I'm very happy with this purchase, and might even buy me some more in different colors...

Love love love I Love these pants they are so comfortable! The look so cute and professional. I am petite and it’s hard for me to pull of the paper bag pants. I feel these look great on my body type. I’m 5”2 and 120lbs athletic body type. My waist is 28 inches and my hips are 38 inches I really loved these. I actually first ordered in black and had to order another one in coffee color. The pants for me sit around the belly button so they are high waisted for me. They are stretchy and firm so they hold shape nicely. I like the fit of them I could of probably sized down even but I feel like medium fits great for this style of pant. However if you wanted the pants be less paper bag-ish you could probably size down for a slimmer look. I think you don’t need to size down tho because even tho they have that paper bag style these ones don’t give the penguin effect like other paper bag pants do sometimes on petite girls and the elastic and tie make it slimming. Stretchy and comfy I'm 5'4" and normally about 152lbs, but I'm 16 weeks pregnant and currently 162lbs so I sized up from my normal size medium to a large. The large fits me great and I feel confident I'll be able to wear these pants as my belly keeps getting bigger (maybe not in the third trimester). They're very stretchy like a jersey type material but very light. I did expect the color to be a bit more brown than pink so mildly disappointed in that but it's fine. Perfect for me as I'm not quite strictly in maternity clothes yet but my normal clothes are getting uncomfortable. I'd definitely wear these not pregnant too of course.

Like the look, hate the quality! I went one size up and ordered large, relying on the reviews that were mentioning that these pants run small. Should’ve bought my normal size, which is medium (US 6) in every single brand! They are true to the size! Large was too big and baggy, especially in the crotch area. The waist was a bit loose too. I exchanged them for medium and they fit perfectly (I’m 5’8”, waist 28, hips 40). Got them in ginger color, which is more like camel in the reality. However, the quality isn’t so great. The material is not soft to the touch, not breathable. Wrinkles easily and can not be ironed since it’s polyester and basically made of plastic and it melts and sticks even on lowest heat. Only steamer works, but takes a looong time. They can be worn for a couple of hours, but I can’t imagine having them on the entire day. If you tend to get hot or live in a hot environment, note that this fabric doesn’t let the air through and traps the moisture. Hope this helps.

Sexy, stylish, comfy! I wanted pants exactly as pictured- comfy, stylish, sexy, stretchy, and light enough not to take up much space in my carry on luggage. These pants check ALL of those boxes. FIT: I am 5'3, 110 pounds, and I bought XS. These fit like a dream (see photos). Fit just above the ankle. Tight enough to be sexy and stylish but loose enough to look classy. QUALITY: I was extremely impressed with the quality of these pants upon receiving the package. Purchasing clothes on Amazon can be a bit of a crap shoot, sometimes they aren't great but these are MORE THAN GREAT! The material is thick and stretchy. The construction is quality. STYLE: These pants are very in, I totally fit in when I wore these to a nice dinner in Europe which was exactly what I bought them for. They styled really well with a slouchy top tucked into them. I chose the dusty blue color and the color is perfectly true to the listing photos

Covers a lot if you get a bigger size. Oh boy, the model must’ve been like a million feet tall, and oh-so-skinny. But anyway, I knew they wouldn’t look like that on me, so I posted pictures. I bought two pair, a S and a M. Im returning the S and posted pictures of me in the M. I’m 5’2.5” and 116lb of after-baby mush. Don’t get me wrong, I worked hard to eat right & lose the fat, but did very little to gain muscle. What does that have to do with these pants? Well, it hides my jiggly, squishy tummy. I can’t say as much about my backside, but they sort of cover the range where the cheeks meet the legs. I’ve got kind of wide hips for my stature, and chicken legs to make them look bigger. So the pants do a good job. They meet my ankles, and will go best with flats (on me). Also, you can totally see the shirt line on the backside of the pants because of the material, and I (for the first time ever) can see why women in the 80’s bought those godforsaken tops that buttoned at the crotch. Ugh, I think I might need to find one now, because I like the pants.

Seriously so comfortable and cute!! I’m 5’7”, 210lbs, a size 14 in jeans, with an apron belly and I ordered an XL. These are PERFECTION. I bought them for a job interview and I’m obsessed. Yes, you should get them!! These pants are soooo comfy and can easily be dressed up or down. There is an elastic band with a decorative belt. No buttons or zippers. I was hesitant to buy these based off my short frame with wider hips and thighs. They have a bit of stretch to the material so they form-fit in some areas (but not in a bad way) and are looser in other areas. For reference I am 5’2 and around 135-140 lbs. I would order a size up if I wanted a looser look but I am content with the fit of a small. Almost looks like a fancy Capri. I roll the pants up about 2 times at the hemline and wear with wedges or heels (short girl proboems) and they hit right above the ankle. Super cute, would recommend, and now I want them in every color!!!! Definitely get some, perfect for summer especially if you don’t like wearing shorts :)

Some Guys Opinion Update: After my wife has now worn both pairs, there is a sizing difference. The pink pants are noticeably bigger and baggier than the black. She stands by her feeling on the pants. And if you are willing to deal with the fit difference, you should have a positive experience with everything else. Ok... So, I bought 2 pairs separately because one color was set to deliver 3 days before the other. Then they packaged and shipped it together anyway, so I was ready to be upset. Instead... They both got here on time and my wife was able to wear them at our daughters college graduation. AND SHE LOVED THEM!!! She loves the way they look, feel and move with you while on. She is ready to buy in other colors. She is 5’11” and bought a Large if that helps anyone with sizing.

Cute pants! Exactly the look I was going for! I also have these in the burnt orange color and they both fit a little different. They are a crepe/dry fit material. Stretchy and lightweight. I am 5’5, 125lbs, carry my height in my legs and they fit as ankle pants. I got size Medium. Only downfall is that you can see my shirt tucked in in the back (a little more sheer than the burnt orange, as expected with a lighter color)

Just freaking get them already! Okay I’ve never worn this style of pants before bc I’ve never found one that flatters my shape so I was super skeptical about ordering, especially from amazon! 100% going to purchase more of these!!! I know. You read/hear it all the time. “I’m so skeptical of clothes from amazon”, but I’m gunna say it anyways. Comfy and stylish! I’m 5’4 and about 140lbs and the XS fits PERFECT. i originally got these pants in a small which is my usual size but they were a bit big and baggy. The XS fits great. Very comfortable and has pockets!! Great variety of colors to choose from. True to size, super comfortable. I love the bow tie detail on the waist and pant leg. Stretchy, comfortable dress pants! I ordered these pants in large & in the apricot color. I love them!!! They are a great color, stretchy & comfortable! Nice pockets in the front & very cute bow details. I am 5’ 9” & 170 lbs and the large was perfect!!! I would definitely recommend them!

Super cute but hear me out! I NEVER wear things like this because of my stomach pouch but I figured to broaden my horizons with a new outfit. I love it! I’m 164lbs, 5’4, and I bought an XL (fits just right, anything smaller and I wouldn’t have been able to get into it). Absolutely cute with a black shirt, BUT guys, they are super thin. I wore a bodysuit with this and you can clearly see the lines from the shirt. So one picture is stretching my butt out a little, and the other is just me standing relaxed. You can see even more if you squat. I mean… this outfit makes me pretty confident and I like it, even with the thinness to it, so I’m keeping. I even wore it out like this. It’s probably just me that overthinks the “panty lines” because it doesn’t look as good, but what can you do. I feel like that’s every outfit.

Dressy yet casual Bought these in Fuchsia to wear to my office job. Can be dressed up or down as needed. Material feels durable with a little stretch to it. The waistband in back expands or contracts for a more versatile fit. I am 5’9” and almost 150lbs and the medium is a good fit. Runs a little short on tall people, but can be remedied with a higher boot so they won’t look like high water britches.

obsessed! Ok, first off, for reference I am 5’2.5” 195lb and Igot an XL. If you want a looser fit, size up. These pants aren’t unbearably tight but they are fitted. I have hips and a bit of a butt and I had to hop to get the elastic waist over my humps BUT once i did baby these pants had the body bodying ok? Love love love these. The belt is detached which is great because as pictured, I changed mine to a stretchy belt that really helped the high rise of the pants cinch my waist in. The pants are a light material but aren’t sheet. Obviously not particularly expensive feeling but not itchy or irritating. I worked a lengthy shift on a retail job when i got them today and I was comfortable in them all night. I ended up snipping the ties at the ankle; didn’t like that feature. but overall i like them. I believe they’re worth it for the price.

AMAZING for the bigger booty girls with long torsos (: Alright ladies. If you have a larger bum/legs then these are for you. I have always wanted a pair of pants like these but the waist is always too large or the thighs/calves are too tight. However, these pants are literally perfect. I got a medium and I am 138lbs, 5’6”, pear shaped, and have a super long torso and short legs. Most high waisted pants hit me just below my belly button but these hit above! They make my torso look shorter and my legs look longer, really balancing my proportions out more. The stretch is perfect, super comfy but still has structure. And the most important part… I don’t feel uncomfortable having my booty show at my job. I work at an elementary school and often feel as though I need to cover my bum because most tighter pants really show it off lol but these are the perfect material that gives me shape but doesn’t put my whole body on display if that makes sense (they are perfectly work appropriate). Lastly, I didn’t love the bows on the ankles, since there’s already a bow on the belt, so I literally just cut them off and you wouldn’t be able to tell at all - especially from far away. Now they’re super cute cigarette style pants (: If you’re considering these pants, just try them!!! So worth it.

Great quality and overall very cute pants. I am just over 5'7"/140lbs and average build. I ordered the coffee color in size medium. These pants are so cute and nice quality. High waisted and a long stride. They hit at a great crop length. The thickness of material was great for work. Feels like a lot of normal work slacks do. They do have some stretch but holds shape well too. There is enough extra room in the size medium for me that I am going to get another color in a size small to see the difference and will update then. My biggest concern is I do have some hips and since these are only pull on with out any kind of zipper I wonder if I can get the small on.

Perfect and classy and fit short curvy girls! So I really love these pants. Very flattering on hour glass shaped women. I got them for an interview and I think they’re going to be absolutely perfect. I’m 5’1 super curvy and probably 175-185 (it’s been a while since I’ve weighed myself lol thanks quarantine 2020) and I ordered an XL in “dark green”. The dark green looked a little more teal and I was hoping it was so took the chance and was super happy I did. I took a picture outside so you could see color better. Its not like Christmas tree green it’s more like handmaidens tale wife dark green (sorry for the weird reference but ¯\_(ツ)_/¯) When pulled to be high waisted as intended they fit my legs and end at my ankles with just a little extra at the bottom so good for us short girls!! I am wearing the “Bishop” shirt from Target (available as of Jan 2021 online) and it works well for this as a crop shirt with puffy sleeves. Love that these pants have their own little bow/sash/belt! Also these are very comfortable so if you needed to have something you could wear that could survive literally running to meetings these will hold up. Felt nice enough that I’m not worried about them tearing or ripping and are comfortable enough to wear going out for brunch or what have you. Will be ordering more colors!

Good fit, good quality I read various good reviews before ordering, and I'm glad I did. I really enjoy these pants. I am 5'7" and about 185 lbs, normally a size 12 pant, and I ordered an XL. They fit really well. Maybe a bit big in the waist, but I didn't want anything smaller to be tight on my thighs/behind, so I'm really happy with the sizing. The pictures don't really do justice, and I don't love this top with these pants as much (it's a looser fit, and I need something that tucks in better), but I am so pleased with these pants. I haven't washed them yet, so I don't know how they wash, but other reviews have said they wash good. If I had one complaint, it'd be that they're a touch long for me, but I just tied the bottom bow a little tighter so they don't fall down. The length would be perfect with sandals. I also LOVE the pockets. They are big enough to put my phone in (iphone 7) AND to put my hand in at the same time. Overall, the material lays/wears in a flattering way, and I love that. I couldn't be happier with these pants.

Really adorable dress pants! Lots of compliments! Love! For reference: I am 5'5" and 135lbs. These dress pants were seriously so cute and comfy! Nice color, great fabric. I tied the bows on the ankles in the back instead of the front. The pants were the perfect length for me. No complaints at all. I bought them for a wedding. I received lots of compliments on them. I bought the dusty rose color in these dress pants too, to go with the white version of the same top I'm wearing in the picture. Mainly because I couldn't decide which color outfit I wanted to wear with the full intent of sending one set back. I kept both outfits! (Both the pants and top are sold on Amazon.) I will say, the dusty rose pants came in nicer brand name packaging with tags and the gray pair in the picture came in generic packaging but also with tags. Overall, they both were in perfect condition and I absolutely love them! I highly recommend these! I hope this helps! :)

Cute, comfy, fit great Love these! I'm 5'5", 154lbs with a bigger booty and hips. High waisted bottoms can be hit or miss for me as far as fit/being flattering but these hit the mark! I got the Large and they fit great - not tight or constricting at all (great stretch, especially in the waist) but are super comfortable and flattering. The material is nice quality, not too thin and perfect for elevating your look. It's not the softest material but definitely not itchy or anything like that. Plus, it's not nearly as much of a dog hair magnet like some of my other bottoms. I purchased the burgundy color as well!

Super cute and comfy, but… I literally just got these in the mail less than 15 minutes ago. They are very cute and can be worn in different ways. I think they fit great. I’m about 5’7 and 145 lbs and these are size medium. The only problem I’ve found so far is that one of the pockets weren’t sewed together so I’m definitely going to lose a thing a two with these lol

Sleek and easy to maintain These wash up so well, and pack in a suitcase without major wrinkles, great for traveling! I got tons of compliments on the (including from a party bus in downtown Nashville!). Can go out at night with stilettos, with my matching CTAS, or even to the office with a more formal blouse. My favorite detail is the little ties at the front of the ankles. The bows are so cute. I weight 130# 5’5” and Small was perfect.

Easy Care, Resists Wrinkling, Super Comfy Super comfy. True to size. Good stretch. Resists wrinkling (in the photo I had been sitting in them for 6 hours, and as you can see, they don't wrinkle). I am 5'7", 153 lbs and my lower body is proportionately smaller than my upper body (I'm usually a size 6 in pants, but an 8 in tops). I ordered a size MEDIUM (Apricot in color) and they fit perfect. They look more fitted in the front, but there's unused stretch in the back, for comfort. I think these pants are very forgiving and super flattering (even for someone like me who is conscious of their belly). Slight flaw in workmanship on my belt, which was misstitched, but overall, probably the best piece of clothing I've purchased from Amazon.

Super cute definitely a staple item! Well I absolutely love the pants I actually bought another pair only issue I have is sizing. I went with the medium based on the reviews. I’m 5’4 165 with thick thighs. I wear a size 6 -7 jeans. I found the pants fit very nicely in the legs and butt area but I had a lot of extra fabric in the waist and lower stomach. I feel like if I went with a small I wouldn’t of had enough room in the legs and butt and it would of been tight. Hope this helps. Again only reason I removed a star is for the sizing other than that the pants are fantastic I love the material light on a summer day but still thick enough to be dressed up. Definitely squat proof. 😄

Perfection and classy Soooo, to help everyone, I am a size 12/13. I buy size 12 skinny pull on jeans at Old Navy for size reference. I’m 5’4. I weight 183 pounds on the thick side. The tie at the ankles can be tied higher to where you like. I purchased an XL based on the reviews. I am I LOVE with this. And I am planning to buy the beige, black and another in green so I can wear the green as casual and keep one for dressy. Here are my measurements to help choose the size for you. I believe if I would have got a large, it would have been too tough to sit without ripping the seams in the behind area. You have to put a tiny effort to get them up your hips. 41 in Belly (around belly button) 39 in waist 46 Buttock 42 thigh to thigh under butt 26 Left and right thigh

Yeah, go head and buy them sis…but my thicc girlies need to size up For reference, I’m 5’5, 180 lbs with a short torso, “shmedium” hips and nice back end. I always struggle to find the perfect pants because my thighs are gargantuan, so I played it safe and ordered a size up (XL) to give the pants a fighting chance to get up my legs. They fit *JUST* right. Not baggy, not clinging to the skin. Despite the waist area not being super stretchy, the pants were comfortable to move around in. If you’re worried about height, my legs are pretty long for my body, and the end of the pants came 2-3 inches above my ankle. A bootie or strappy heel compliments it well. I’ll be buying another pair soon.

I purchased the G Gradual Women’s joggers pants with side zipper pockets stretch tappered athletic joggers in white size medium. They are not see through. First I bought these pants because they have zipper side thigh pockets for my iPhone. I prefer my phone on the side of my thigh when I run! I love the pockets. My actual body measurements are 37 hip, my natural waist is 27, the circumference around my belly button where the pants sit is 31 inches. When I buy shorts I base my purchase decision on the waist. I go for looser so i do not get muffin top. The waist on the medium was a true medium. I compared it to my Under Armor pants size medium. I am 5”1’ tall and 110 pounds. I have super skinny stork-like legs but you’d never know it in these pants. They do not cling or hug me. I wear size 4 in Lulu Lemon speed up shorts and size 4 in jeans. But I do size up to size 6 in Lulu lemon speed up shorts if I am running up stairs for training. Size 4 is fine for jogging. Why I returned these: I had to return these because the 28.5 inch on size medium which I ordered is too long because I am 5”1’ tall. So the pants pool at my ankles a bit. I now know I need a 26 inch inseem. If you are 5”5’ or taller these will fit you without pooling. Second, I tried these on in 70F degree temperature room and I sat down and watched TV for 10 minutes and I got overly hot. (And I am always cold). These joggers insulate and trap heat in. They would be good to wear outside when it’s below 55 degrees if you are going to run or jog in them. In my 70 degree house just sitting I was way, way too hot. (And I am always cold because I have a hole in my heart). Polyester keeps you warm. Depending on the weight. I wear Colorful Koala polyester blend leggings in the winter, fall and spring and they don’t show sweat. But in the summer I switch to leggings with nylon they breathe 100% better here in Texas. In my experience nylon pants breathe very well in high Temperatures in Texas. I wish G gradual had some joggers with pockets made of nylon for summer. So these G gradual are great for outdoor workouts in much cooler weather. But I couldn’t wear these casually indoors at 70 F degrees. Regarding sizing: According to the size chart provided based on my natural waist 27 inches and my hip 37 inches instead of buying a medium it says I should have purchased the XS. I hate the waist being too tight when I bloat so I probably would have sized up to a small so the waist would be bigger. . I bought these because they said they were stretchy. These are NOT very stretchy. I returned them because they were too long and too big and not stretchy enough for yoga and running up stairs or doing high knees. I tested them. They feel stiff. They were a polyester papery feel. I hope this helps. If your tall these will work for colder climates workouts.
