I have about a dozen of these GGradual pants in multiple colors. Mostly the front zipper pocket joggers. I don’t like the high waisted ones. They Work for a Petite (Although as Medium/High Rise pants) These are super comfortable joggers which appear to be made of medium weight swimsuit material. I got the camo ones because I've got a roll of fat around my lower belly that I wanted to hide, but these pants hide that imperfection without the help of the camo. (for reference I'm a chunky petite, about 5'2", 35 " waist, 41" bum with a short waist, and ordered the XL, which is my usual size on Amazon). PROS: 1. Comfortable 2. They can be worn as high or medium (or low?) rise. 3. Because they're cuffed at the ankle they're not too long for me 4. Two Deep zippered pockets. 5. A wide waist without a knit band is flattering. It also has a tie at the waist. 6. They claim to be breathable- I'd say they're about 50% breathable, which given their medium thickness seems pretty reasonable. Considerations: 1. When I wear them as medium rise there's a little extra material in the crotch area, but not enough that it is uncomfortable. 2. After wearing these awhile they got a little static cling, but not enough to be bothersome. Note that the material is similar to a medium weight swimsuit material. All in all I'm happy with this order.

Comfy! **Update 04/2024** I repurchased these in a size XL in the color OLIVE GREEN, just to see if I liked them better in a bigger size. I often prefer to size up now, as due to a surgery I had years ago, my lower tummy has gotten sensitive to things pressing on it. While these are definitely looser in the waist than the L, I think they unfortunately look a little sloppy. They're baggy in the crotch and just overall don't look like they fit as well. I may have not minded that in black, but in this slightly lighter shade, it's pretty obvious. I'm a bit in the fence about if I'll repurchase the large or not, we'll see. I got these in the color BLACK in a size LARGE. For sizing reference, I'm usually a medium-large or a 8-12/29-31 in bottoms and jeans. If it helps, I wear a size 6 or 8 in Lulu leggings. I'm 5'3" tall with a short torso, and my measurements are about 44"-33"-44" (bust-waist-hip) right now, give or take. I found these to be mostly TTS, though I'd recommend potentially sizing up if you have larger hips/butt. I still had plenty of room but would potentially size up to an XL if I wanted them baggier. The material is super soft and stretchy, more brushed than athletic, though it has a hint of slickness to it. They are super comfortable. The style is interesting, as it looks like it would be a jogger but it's cropped instead. I personally hate joggers, so I thought these were a nice change. I have thicker thighs and calves from years of soccer and these were super forgiving in those areas. I'm a little on the shorter side and found the length was a bit longer than I'd prefer, but I can just roll them up twice and they're perfect, or get them hemmed. In my picture, my left leg has the hem rolled and the right leg does not, so you can see the difference. They hit the top of my foot unrolled. I really like these because they're good for running around, working out, or even for casual workwear. I do wish they didn't have the logo on the leg and instead had them either on the back of the waistband, and/or made it tonal so it wasn't obvious. Overall, I'd definitely recommend these. I don't know if I'll grab any other colors, as I don't wear this type of thing often, but I still like them.

Watch the length I'm 5'3" and these are much longer on me than the model 😅 The material is nice but would be terrible if your haven't shaved your legs in a day or two. The pocket outline shows through the front. SIZE UP. I usually get a large, but got an XL based on reviews. Comfy. Would recommend.
Love, love, love these pants!! So comfortable and stretchy. The perfect hang-out or travel pant. I’m 6’ and 160lbs and large fit perfectly. I sized up from my normal medium.

Great fit and comfortable with a small adjustment I bought two of these in two different colors and sizes and I love them both. Green is Medium size and black is Small size. For a reference, I'm 5.8 and 155 pounds. One thing I didn't like was the elastic waist (an enemy to apple shapes) it's super tight and constricting. However I ended up removing the inner elastic from the waist and now they fit perfect. They sit flat on my waist and the draw string allows for a natural waist adjustment. The waist may not be an issue for smaller waisted women, but for apple shapes the elastic waist may be a bit problematic in terms of creating a muffin top with the way it is. However if you're handy with the sewing machine, it's an easy and quick fix. Otherwise, I like the fabric, it's smooth, cool, it doesn't wrinkle, dries super fast, and no changes after multiple wears and washes. The side pockets are also great, they are functional and again create a nice hip line for someone with narrow hips. Definitely reccomend this one.

Nice & thick, but not great about having to cut! I love the comfort and the pockets and the thickness! Very nice pants overall. But, as you can see in the front pic, very long. Even with long legs! So, I will have to cut these if I keep them, and not being a seamstress, that makes me a bit nervous. Otherwise, great pants! I just haven’t quite decided about whether I want to have the cut them to keep them yet.About me. I am 5’6, used to be 5,9” (still have the leg length), and weigh about 125. My measurements are 40”-28”-36”, and 36DD. I usually wear a small or size 4 in bottoms, and a small to medium in tops depending totally upon the cut and stretch. Overall, I’m a fairly thin, not in great shape, 63 year old woman. So, probably like a lot of people.I have worn the Sketchers Go Walk pants for years, and have many pairs. I love them, except for their unique ability to attract cat hair!! I happen to have tried a couple of pairs in a different brand of 4 pocket yoga type pants last year. They were made by a company called FirstWay. They were quite similar to the Sketchers pants, except they did NOT attract the cat hair so bad. So, I recently searched for them on Amazon, and couldn’t find them. So, I’ve tried 3 other brands and this is what I found.One of the other two brands I tried, Gradual was also nice and thick. The third brand, Nuveti were both definitely thinner than these! I will post reviews of both there.

Great color, fabric feels amazing For reference, I am 5ft 1in, 112lbs, 34 D/E chest, 27 waist, 37 hip. I ordered the small in plum. I would call the fit accurate to slightly generous. It's great-feeling fabric that is soft on your skin, not itchy, nothing that feels binding or tight. The wide waist and lines of the hip. and backside are flattering. The style is relaxed, classy athleisure chic - great for going for gourmet ice cream or getting on the plane. Concerns I have - The color is lovely but nothing like the advert which looked like an elderberry color, this is more aubergine/red plum. - They were very wrinkled on arrival, so they may need to dry flat, which is annoying Even with these concerns I still think they are a good pant and a good value. Very good looking and comfortable.

Roomy in the legs I ordered navy blue and went up one size for these Gradual Capri Pants to see how they would fit. I'm normally XL but ordered 2XL and they are roomy and comfortable. I wanted roomy in the waist and they deliver. I could have ordered my usual size for a more fitted look. The waist band is wide so it rests on your waist. It feels really good. The elastic in the waist is soft and moves along with your body movements with complete ease. I can't stand tight restricting waist bands. Btw, I love the high waist band. At 65 yrs I don't want to struggle with low, riding down , plumber's butt causing waist bands. Legs are cut full. These are not form fitting capris but they don't look baggy. Side pockets are ample. They sit flat against the capris and a bit towards the back of the garment. They arrived not directly on the side, at least they aren't for me. I'm 5'4" and these are true capris, not too long, not too short. Fabric stretches very nicely in all directions.

Not the nice stuff These are the kind that feel like 1980’s velour outfits, at least to me anyway. I have ordered several velvet type clothing and there silky soft and then theres this. This picks up fuzz like no other. My others it slides right off on these its like a magnet for fuzz. They are also very shiny. They are so high rise they went up to my ribs. The waist part was quite tight even for a large. A size US size 12/14 should have been larger in my opinion. Be sure to measure your inseam. 32 inch inseam is not for shorter legs that these days are usually petite with jean sizes. If you are less than a 32 inch inseam then probably will need heels. Id only recommend these to people who like their pants super high and like the shinier glitter style velvet. This isnt the buttery soft kind. It will work with matching tops of similar material though. They are long so measure yourself if you dont already know your size. One upside they are stretchy just the waistband is a tighter elastic. I took stars off for how tight they are in relation to their posted measurements for US sizing. A 12/14 in US size should have been roomy for me not snug especially for stretchy material.

These are some really great pants! I have been ordering most of my pants on Amazon in a size Medium lately, but I took a chance on the small this time, and it was a great fit for my 27" waist, 37" hips frame. They skim my shape without being clingy or showing too much. The waist is sufficiently high to cover my belly button, and the length is perfect for just skimming the ground when wearing a low heel (I'm 5'6 and my slippers in the photo have a 1.5" wedge). The velvet catches the light nicely, and I love the front seam, which gives them an element of style. All that, and they're stretchy, warm, and comfortable too! I really can't wait to wear these for some kind of festive holiday event.

Nice Quality, Well-Made, Stylish & Versatile! I'm so glad I took a chance on these capri pants! As a 5'4", 115-120lbs woman, the size small fits me perfectly. They're well-made with strong stitching and high elastic waist that adds a trendy touch. The relaxed fit is comfortable and the rolled hems with button tabs add a nice detail. The stretchy material is just right - not too thick or thin, and lightweight. Despite initially hesitating due to the relaxed fit, they look and feel great on me. I love the 6 utility pocket's which make them versatile for casual wear, walking, hiking, and more. Plus, they can easily be dressed up for evening wear with a nice top, heels, and belt. They're reasonably priced, and despite being 68, I feel good, comfy, and trendy in them. I believe they'd suit any woman regardless of age, size, or shape. Highly recommended!

Never know what you're gonna get It’s hard to find the right fit because I’m 5’2”, 30” inseam. Inverted triangle shape maybe? with a fuller waist. Because of this, when I order online, I get different sizes so I don't have to return and reorder all the time, but.... It’s exhausting. Normally, I wear a 6 or 8/28 in high waist jeans and 4 or 27/28 in lower rises. S-M in leggings. Inseam depending on style 25”-32”. All orders below I ordered 32” inseam and none were that. I included pics of 2 black (can't see1 of them and hard to see difference in color) and one of the ice blue.I ordered:4 (S) Ice Blue Fit was oh k, but afraid it might stretch to be too big because fabric is stretchy.6 (M) Ice Blue Too big all overThe small is more like a size 6. The fabric was decent quality, the color is a very light blueI ordered (XS) Lapis longing blue The fit in the butt was ok but bordering on too tight and uncomfortably tight in the waist. The color is med. dark blue with feathering …fading.. bleached? in the butt and on the front thighs. The fabric felt cheap. Thin, kind of crunchy or stiff but stretchy.I ordered(S) Always Black too big! Bigger than the 6(M) the color was very black, the fabric different from all the others which I kind of expected, but ok and the quality was good for the price. I liked these and was happy I had ordered the XS assuming they would be the winner or so I thought….(XS) Always Black, when I tried these, the fit ok except that the fabric felt thinner and the color had a slightly washed look to them and then the length was shorter than all of them! UGH!!! So frustrating!All sizes were shorter than the stated 32”. They varied between 30 and 31. So bummed because I liked them for the most part. I could accept the lesser quality if I could find one that had the qualities of three put together fabric of the ice blue, the smaller size and color of the Always black and then the right length of 32"! LOLMy favorite fabric and fit were the ice blue. Always black my favorite color, so I will try one more time and order XS 32” inseam Ice Blue and Always black and pray that’s what I get. I’ll try to update.****UPDATE:Ended up order the “Always Black” only in XS/32”So disappointing and frustrating. What I got was extreme faded black looked like they had been washed and again the inseam was not 32”! The last 3 photos are the newest pair! I mean come on!😞

Good fit lightweight denim When my husband and I sold everything we owned & moved to Mexico, I packed on almost 30 lbs. We overindulged in all the delicious food here, and stayed in Airbnbs for months with no access to a gym. As a result, none of my clothes fit anymore, so I needed something I could wear that was at least as flattering as possible given the current state of my body. Since my size 10 jeans were too tight, I decided to get a 12 in these. In the photos, I weighed about 180 lbs, 37 waist , 42.5 hips and they were the tiniest bit snug, but the spandex in the denim allowed me to confirm to my body instead of giving me muffin top. Since I'm 5'7" with long legs, I ordered the 32 inch inseam. The length is perfect for a low wedge heel on me. I love that these jeans are lightweight with some stretch. So much better than the stiff denim from the old days (I was born in the 70s, raised in the 80s). The denim back then was thick and so stiff if you gained an ounce jeans weren't wearable anymore, and even if you could squeeze into them, they dug into your skin and left indentions and red marks. This more modern, stretch denim is so much more comfortable to wear, and the lighter weight fabric is more bearable in hot climates like Mexico.

In the photos, I weighed about 180 lbs, 37 waist , 42.5 hips and they were the tiniest bit snug, but the spandex in the denim allowed me to confirm to my body instead of giving me muffin top. Since I'm 5'7" with long legs, I ordered the 32 inch inseam. The length is perfect for a low wedge heel on me. I love that these jeans are lightweight with some stretch. So much better than the stiff denim from the old days (I was born in the 70s, raised in the 80s). The denim back then was thick and so stiff if you gained an ounce jeans weren't wearable anymore, and even if you could squeeze into them, they dug into your skin and left indentions and red marks. This more modern, stretch denim is so much more comfortable to wear, and the lighter weight fabric is more bearable in hot climates like Mexico.

Keeping but there are things you should know I read all the reviews and went into this knowing that these are not high quality. What gave me hope was the curvy girls I saw that loved them. For reference I'm 5'5", 36/27/40 measurements, 138lb. The good: I don't have a gap in the back. (this never happens!) The length is perfect. (also never happens) They are a little stiff but mostly comfortable. My thighs and butt have room to breathe! The bad: The distressed look on these is so tacky. I'll try to mute it by using dye. The seams are awful and could need reinforcement in stress areas. Look at the belt loop placement. Couldn't be bothered to make them symmetrical. The hardware is cheap looking and you'll need to rub a wax candle on the zipper to prevent sticking. Overall if you're athletic build/curvy these are so worth it for the cut alone. I very rarely find pants that fit me. I want to cry. When these inevitably fall apart, I'm going to take them apart and sew a higher quality copy of them with fabric that has stretch. This is such a relief after years of skinny jeans and manufacturers that pretended we didn't have bones, muscle, and fat on our bodies. With those very human things, you can also have a small waist! I'm mostly happy and glad I bought them. Took two stars off because they won't last long and I shouldn't have to dye them and resew the belt loops right out of the box.

Shorter inseam than advertised Unfortunately, I really do need every bit of the 32 inch inseam that was advertised. I will be sending these back as they do it just a little bit too short for flats. Would be ok with tennis shoes but that’s not why I needed them. I also feel like the hemming at the bottom was shorter than shown but that’s just my impression. They are very stretchy and would be comfortable to wear, just not long enough for me.

3 1/2 stars These jeans fall between 3 and 4 stars and I don't think I would buy again. The good: The fit is great. I am 5' 7" 135lbs and normally wear either a 4 or 6 in other brands. The small in these fit nicely. They are a little tighter around my waist than I'd like, but I do have a pretty big waist-to-hip ratio (menopause belly!) However, they are stretchy so are wearable. The length is perfect on me and they are wide in the leg without being super-wide and baggy like some brands I've tried. The bad: The denim is low quality and the fabric is stiff. I have washed and steam-ironed them to soften them up a bit but they are still pretty stiff. Also, on the pair I received, the distressing is uneven. I ordered the deep blue color and there were three parallel white lines that looked like scratch marks on one leg. Luckily, I have a deep blue felt pen so I lightly touched up the white lines and now they aren't visible. I am sure I will wear these but they won't be my go-to jeans.

Soft and comfy I bought these in blue and love them. Very stretchy and comfy. I’m 5’7 and 155lbs and purchased a medium. There is still a lot of room if my body decides to grow, but they did not stretch out after a few wears without washing. I am disappointed that the second pair I ordered in the Pecan had white lines on the top. I think to make them look worn but it doesn’t look natural. In the ad photo the lines are not noticeable. I will be returning. Would love them if they didn’t have the lines. Very pretty color.

Size chart true for US sizing. Super soft and cute! For comparison: I am 5’5” and I am usually 120 lbs but post holiday I’m rocking 125-130 lbs. I normally wear US size 4 or 27 in pants and size medium bikini bottoms. I have wide hips, some junk in the trunk, and shorter legs. Product: With the extra winter weight hanging on tight, mostly due to my love of chocolate and avoidance of the outdoors when the temperature is under 60, I decided to order medium Grapent jeans. I did not want the pants to be too tight and hinder my couch potato winter lifestyle. That was a mistake. The provided size chart recommended a size small but I have trust issues when it comes to size charts so I went up a size. Not only are the jeans plenty big in the waist, maybe for 8/10/12, but there is quite a bit of stretch in the material. Also, the fabric lost a bit of shape after a day of running errands. I fully regret not getting the recommended small because the material is very soft and stretchy so I think I would love them if they fit better. That’s my bad! The fabric is not a true denim but rather a medium weight cotton/poly blend. There are functional hip pockets as well as back pockets. I especially like the thigh rip because it is a different style than my other ripped jeans. As for length, I had to roll the cuffs to wear with sneakers. I’m normally a 29” inseam and these jeans were probably 30-31”. These waist just qualifies as “high” landing just past my belly button. The rear area was the only fitted part with the legs being looser. Recommendation: These mom jeans are stylish and super comfortable. I recommend going by the size chart in this case and rest easy knowing the fabric has plenty of stretch. The only negative thing I found was the tag instructs to hand wash while the product page advertises machine washable. I am going to machine wash but hang to dry and hope for the best. I really like these jeans and would recommend to a friend!

Comfy, good material I really liked these wide leg pants. I'm 5'0" 175lbs and I got a large. I purposely got capri length so they would fit me like regular pants and they are the perfect length. The material is not see through and the fabric feels flowy. They have pockets which is great. The elastic is comfortable. No complaints!

Casual capris with beach-y fabric; a little prone to wedgies I got these capris pants so that I'd have some casual summer capris in a fun color. SIZE/FIT: I got an XXL in the olive green, and they fit as expected (the waist is a little snug on me, but I knew that going in, since I have a 42" waist - but they're pretty comfortable and stretchy regardless). I'm 5"6', about 220 lbs. The capris pant stops about 5-6 inches above my ankle. APPEARANCE: I love the color of the fabric; it's a lovely, light, slightly muted green. I like how the capris look from the front and sides. On the backside, however, the fabric kept getting caught up on my behind, giving my a slight wedgie appearance. For that reason, I took off a star. FEATURES: These pants have nice sized, open pockets, big enough for a phone or wallet. I like the wide, elastic waist. FUNCTION/FABRIC: The fabric of these pants is light and kind of crinkled; they would be great for the beach or on a hot summer day. Overall, I'm very happy with these pants, aside from the wedgie issue.

Make sure you are wearing the right underwear! These are soft and comfortable. However, with the high waist and smooth material good fitting underwear is ESSENTIAL, also make sure your shirt is tucked in perfectly, or wear one that needs no tucking. These have good stretch, I wear an 8 curvy L cut in jeans, these are a medium they fit my waist and stretch to accommodate my large hips and my booty. I can totally see myself enjoying these in the next few months here in Florida as it will be nice and warm, like today it's January and 80. These are lightweight and don't have that slightly stiff feeling of a pure linen pair of pants, instead they almost have a silky tendency! Now, the only drawback for me as a busy mom, is not being able to throw these into the dryer with everything else. I did wash these and then draped them across the rack to dry before trying them on again for pictures, I didn't notice any shrinkage in regular washing! I think I might need another pair for variety's sake, instead of my beloved leggings. I'm 5'10" about 160, long torso, long thighs compared to calves, for reference 4 stars because I'm being persnickity about not being able to toss in the dryer on medium with everything else.

oversized and soft This review is for the GRAPENT Wide leg palazzo pants, xx-large, dark blue. I usually wear an XXL in pull on pants, 17/18 in jeans, and XL (XXL is usually a little too big) in leggings. Approximate measurements are 41” waist and 51” hips, 5’6” and a 30 inch inseam reaches just to the floor on me. These pants are a delight to wear. They are unbelievably soft, thin, stretchy, and flowy. The style is meant to be oversized so definitely don’t size up even if you want a loose fit. The size chart was based on body measurement and not the dimensions of the pants. It says that XX-Large, US size 20 - US 22, waist 36.5 - 38”, hips 47 - 48.5”. My waist and hips are bigger than that and the pants still are a flowy fit on me. The waist has a few inches of smocking and is very stretchy. The fabric has a good horizontal stretch to it. These pants drag on the ground for me. I wouldn’t expect this with a 29” inseam but I measured and they are indeed 29 (maybe 29.25) inches. However even with the waist an inch or two above my belly button (which is perfect for me) the crotch sits a little low. It looks and feels good but that’s why the leg length is too long for me. They look like the could be a straight wide leg but are actually about 16” at the thigh and 20” at the hem. The pockets are huge and stretchy and don’t lay completely flat across the front but rather droop out a little at the top. As I mentioned, these pants are delightfully comfortable. They are a little too oversized and long for me to want to wear as everyday “real” pants. Instead I’ll wear them primarily as loungewear that I’m OK with wearing out of the house for something like running an errand.

The pockets are huge and stretchy and don’t lay completely flat across the front but rather droop out a little at the top. As I mentioned, these pants are delightfully comfortable. They are a little too oversized and long for me to want to wear as everyday “real” pants. Instead I’ll wear them primarily as loungewear that I’m OK with wearing out of the house for something like running an errand.
Comfortable, stretchy 160lbs 5’3 These fit amazing! Did not stretch out and become loose (wore them for over 12 hours). Very happy with this purchase.
Comfortable, fit perfect I’m 5’3 125 pounds, small was perfect. I love the way they feel, super comfortable!

Very comfortable but not a straight fit for bigger thighs I’m 5’7 and usually a size 12 so I got a large. They were very comfy and fit well with good stretch all around. I wanted a true straight fit though and they are snug around my thighs so I don’t think I will be keeping them. Again that’s personal preference but it’s just not my thing. LOVE ❤️ I love these jeans, so much I ordered in more colors. They are lightweight, have just the right amount of stretch, comfortable and the perfect length. I am 5'6" and weigh 155. These pictured are the washed black. I saw someone's review said they had too much acid, mine don't at all!
Perfect fit for petite ladies I’m 5’0 and the small fits perfect. Very comfortable and not too big of a gap on the back.

Comfortable, Roomy Jeans with Some Stretch I ordered these jeans in size Medium in the color Medium Blue. They have a recycled look to them. They are cut roomy in the thighs and legs, which I find comfortable. They are made of 76% Cotton/26% Polyester/2% Elastane so they have some give to them. After laundering them three times, they haven't noticeably shrunk. For reference, I'm a long-waisted/short-inseamed 5' 4", 124 lbs, 29" waist and 38" hips. The length of these on me is at the longest side of OK. I think I could have ordered these in a size small, as they are a little baggy and the waist slips down a bit if I don't wear a belt. They have a strange smell that hasn't completely washed out after 3 washings, but I suspect it will eventually. Overall, I like these comfy jeans and I think they look kind of boho stylish.
Cute pants I ordered these pants in the XL. They are cute and fit great. They are pretty long. They come past the ankle and I'm 5ft6in. My waist measures 40inches and I they were not tight at all.

Very nice jeans, cute and comfy! I am 5'7" and 155lbs with a 31" waist. I normally wear between a size M and L and got these in a size L, they fit perfectly. These are super nice jeans. They have a nice deep color and a cute style to them. They have some stretch as well and are comfy to wear. They didn't stretch too much throughout the day and stayed in place nicely. I would highly recommend!

Cute straight leg jeans! Exactly as pictured! Denim is comfortable, soft and sturdy! Runs true to size chart, I ordered a size medium. For reference I’m 5’7 approximately 155lbs, I have a big booty and thick hips and thighs. I love the mom Jean vibe these pants have, especially from the back! Highly recommend!
Looks great! Love these jeans, they're comfortable and fit so nice. The flare is nice but not too much. Wearing a small at 135 pounds and 5'3".

Excellent Jeans! I don’t know what this brand is doing, but every pair fits amazingly. I’m size 14, 5’9”, 200 lbs, and bought an XL. The fit like a glove and are the perfect length. Comfortable Pants First off I would say that are the most comfortable stretch dress pants I have come across. They have cute little pockets in the front and elastic waistband. This product is true to size. I am 5'3. I weigh 165 lb and I have big hips and got a large but I could have got a medium.

Extremely Comfortable ☺️ I am 5’7, 155 pounds and I ordered a size Large in the color Black. I go back and forth typically in sizes medium and large in pants. Sometimes I am a size 6 but often I am a size 8. The large fits me in the waist extremely well. These pants are made out of 80% Nylon and 20% spandex so they are very stretchy and soft all over. One of the comfiest black dressier pants I now own! The waist is elastic and although it looks like it buttons up in the front, it is only there for visual purposes. These fit me tighter at the waist and hips and then are a straight leg with the slightest gradual flare going all the way down to give a tiny amount of wideness at the bottom. I wouldn’t consider these wide leg pants though. The only thing I wish I could change about these pants is the length. This is a normal problem for me though. The length is perfect if I wear a flat shoe but anything higher they will be too short. I like to wear a chunky block sandal or something with a heel when I wear black pants like this and unfortunately I will not be able to do that. Other than that, these are extremely comfy and I love them!

Comfortable, Flattering and Versatile I seriously want to live in these trousers! The material is so soft and reminds me of a really high-quality pair of yoga leggings and the fabric is the perfect thickness. They're polished enough to wear to the office, but they're also so comfortable that they're perfect for yoga or just lounging around the house. The fit of these pants was perfect for me. I ordered the Medium, and for reference, I'm 5'6", 150 lbs, and a size 8. They fit true to size, and the stretchy material makes them super easy to slip on and off. I love that I don't have to fuss with any zippers or buttons - the buttons on the front are just decorative. The high-waisted design is perfect, and the waistband has a gentle compression that sucks you in a bit without feeling tight or uncomfortable around the tummy. I can wash these pants on a gentle cycle, hang them up to dry, and they're ready to go in no time. Plus, they don't wrinkle. I'm definitely planning on ordering more of these pants in different colors. They're such a great find and so comfy!

Small reg vs small petite (both fit great) Very good product. I like the material and the waist band elastic is comfortable, and doesn't extend fully in the front so it doesn't get bunched up when pulling the ties. Based on reviews, I ended up getting the small and small petite to try. I am 5'3.75" and 130lbs, usually wear a size 5 juniors or 4/6 depending on the pants as I have a booty. I was concerned as based on the measurements I didn't think my booty would fit in the small (39" hips) but they did, easily! Plenty of room in both sizes. I had room behind the thighs as well, and only had to pull the drawstrings slightly, which is surprising for me with pants in general. I laid out the small reg and small petite and tried on both. The only discernable difference I found is in the length of the pant leg. Small reg measured at 31" inseam, and it was 2" longer than the petite. For me both fit well, and just depended on what "look" I would want- fully over the top of the tennis shoe with wrinkles (reg) or just laying on top (petite). For reference the one pictured is the petite and the shoes are standard asics sneakers (about 1" heel).

The length fit perfect but they were baggier than my liking, so I did have to send them back for an XS Tall. For reference, I’m 5’6 and around 135 lbs. They fit my waist and legs great. The length could be just slightly longer for my liking (I have long legs and a short torso, so my pants sit up higher on my waist— and I also have slim legs so finding pants that fit my body type can be hard) but overall I’m very happy with my purchase and would recommend these pants! I’ll probably end up buying another pair.

If buying in black color, size down Feb 2023: I normally wear a medium… Medium petite was at least an inch too large… I was swimming in them… See pictures. Reordered a small petite And it fits perfectly… See pictures. Washed it on cold and tumbled dry on low for 20 minutes, then hang dried, no shrinkage. For reference, I am 5’3”, 123 pounds, a little belly fat, And hips at 40 inches. The waist really doesn’t matter because this is a lower rise pant. Love how lightweight it is, I am a massage therapist. I have also ordered this in steel blue, let’s hope the small petite fits in that as well. Luv all the pockets and it is a very soft material and dries quickly.Mar 2023: I did order the PANTS in steel blue as well and the small petite fits perfect. I also ordered the matching scrub top… I tried the small in the scrub TOP though it fit, I like things a little looser… It was fitted across the chest and in the arm holes a bit tighter than I prefer, and when raising my arms it raised up on my hips, as did the medium, but on the small I had to pull it back down. The medium Came back down on its own when I lowered my arms. I returned the small scrub top for the medium. I am 34C. I don’t like to feel constricted in any way and when massaging I am moving my arms a lot in all directions, so I do not have time to tug down my scrub top. So for me I could wear either the small or medium scrub top but I prefer more room so I chose medium to keep. I love How light and soft the material is. I wash on cold and tumble dry on low for 20 minutes then hang dry. No shrinkage. Very comfortable.

Postpartum body: Buttery soft, stretchy, tummy compression, not see through I'm currently 3 months postpartum and have been on the hunt for some capris leggings that fit all my curves while I regain my body. ⭐PROS⭐ Color & Fabric: they are true black and not see through. Fit: I'm 210 lbs and still losing baby weight. These hold things in place just enough without being uncomfortable. Despite Amazon's recommendation, I ordered a size down from the suggested size. It also is stretchy enough that they aren't tight around my high waist or calves. Feel: so soft! Sometimes it really does feel like you don't have anything on! Haha 🚨CONS🚨 The fabric isn't the most breathable so keep that in mind if you are wanting to use these for working out. The fabric collects lots of hair. The tag inside is not centered. So, you have to be thoughtful about putting them on to make sure they aren't on backwards.

GREAT for petite women & lovely fabric! Great to not have to pay 100 bucks for Athleta petite city joggers!!! Score for us shorter ladies!! These pants actually fall in the right spots on our legs and knees. They’re not just shorter in length 🙌🏻🙌🏻🙌🏻For reference, I’m 5’4” 117 pounds and these are the purple ones.I usually get an XS in joggers and leggings, but i needed a size Small in these. The highwaisted waistband is tiny. Though that’s not necessarily a problem area for me, I’m straight like a boy there. No curves. No tiny cinched waist. I probably could have used another 1/2”-1” there. But the fabric of the waistband is soft and comfy, so it’s not bothersome.NOTE: I have one leg that’s shorter than the other, so no the waistband isn’t sewn more curved on one side. It just looks that way b/c one hip sticks out more than the other.The fabric is lovely too. But be careful with your panty line. Though the fabric is not chonchy, it’s too thin to be worn without a layer between the pants and your cheeks in order to be proper. You just need that extra thin slip like layer. Can’t be cotton b/c it’ll cling. Target has some great silky options in their Auden line in the little drawers.

Type of FABRIC Relevant to FIT UPDATE: Top of thigh has begun to pill. Very small and low pills, but no longer smooth. It’s not the worse pilling, it’s tiny but I can feel it under my hand. *** There’s a fabric term that I don’t hear used much anymore: “hand.” Hand is the feeling of the fabric- sheen, weight, stiffness, stretch, texture, pile, etc. If you read description this manufacturer uses 4 different fabrics lumped into one product listing. It makes it d*#n near impossible to select the right product because reviewers are talking about different things. This fabric is lightweight, low sheen, low compression (read: stretchy). The pattern is nice looking (grey marble). I’d personally size down on the grey marble leggings. Like you, I read ratings because I want what I want and hate making returns. The leggings I ordered are light weight and STRETCHY. STRETCHY does not generally facilitate tummy control and there is no double panel in the front. I chose the fabric that I visually liked. It’s 88% polyester/12% spandex. I sound smart but I have no idea what the hand of that fiber mix feels like so I took a stab in the dark. I kinda lost. Constantly hiking them up and no tummy control. My tummy is not that big, I just want a little flatter front, and I like the feeling of additional core support. There are 3 other fabrics/fiber combos used which I think should be listed separately because if they are a different fabric, they are a different product. I hate it when vendors group products together. Shoppers have no idea what product is being described, because apples, oranges, bananas and lemons are all being called “fruit.” For reference, I’m 5’ 5, 139lbs, 30” waist, 40” hips. I’m high waisted (I did like that these leggings hit me right at my real waist) and I have muscular thighs and butt. When reviewing, talk about the fabric hand, and your measurements. It’s sooo ! They’ve given us a communication platform. We’ll have to figure it out by talking to each other. A note on Pocket Placement: The side pockets are placed too high on the side leg, except if you’re carrying a bottle of Champaign, apparently (???). But if you’re sitting and you need to reach your phone, it sucks. Pretty much have to stand up. NOTE: I think the higher the spandex percentage, the more compression you’ll get. I also associate spandex with a higher sheen (Think Jane Fonda 80’s Workout) which I did not want. But I’m not sure. What’s your experience? To GYM PEOPLE: Include a compression rating next to the fiber content descriptions. For leggings, I suspect this may be a defining factor for buyers. Fiber content is meaningless unless you’re, well, you.

Comfort & No Budge for High Intensity I previously ordered a pair of these pants in a Large around Black Friday. They were running a deal, so I thought I would try them. I love that pair, but they are starting to feel a little loose, as well as my few other brands. (I will add this is my favorite brand as of now, so I keep watching for deals. I’m ballin’ on a budget over here.) I have recently started jogging/running, and my pants would work their way down, and the seams in the crotch area are uncomfortable during running. I decided to size down to a medium to allow for a tight fit. You can tell they are more snug in the pictures, but they are not too tight. I just got these in medium today and already took them for an outdoor run. I absolutely love them! I did not have to adjust one time during my run, and I felt no seams while running. I was able to focus on my workout, and not my wardrobe. 💕 Also, no unseemly wet spots. I appreciate all the pockets as well. There are two side pockets, big enough for me to slide my phone in, and also a pocket on the inside of the high waisted back panel. Pictures were taken after 1.5 mile run. I will definitely be ordering more! For reference: Weight 170-175lbs Height: 5’5”

Mostly good These are pretty true to size but i have 2 complaints about them. The waist band is to tight compared to how loose the legs are. I have huge legs, from my thighs to my calves, everything is well above average. My waist is below average compared to my hips and thighs. The band around my waist is slightly tighter than I'd expect for being a 2x but the legs are much bigger. You can see both in my pictures. Its not a big enough problem to return them but it's still something to think about when purchasing a pair. My only other issue is that they're not 100% squat proof. My hips/butt are a bit bigger than you'd expect for my waist so that could be the issue though. They only became see through in a deep squat though. Regular bending wasnt an issue. The quality is fantastic, and i love the side pockets! My measurements are as follows, waist - 38 inches, hips - 55 inches, height - 5 feet 2 inches.

The material is not the buttery soft material it feels more like a cotton tshirt to me or like a denim jegging maybe. I don’t love that it has a thin band at the waist because it cuts into me (still squishy from having 3 kids) but I know if I went a size up they’d be way too big because these felt a little loose everywhere else but that band. I’m on my hot mom journey so to find decent leggings for a great price while I’m still working on it is great. I’d buy again to avoid spending too much while losing weight. Currently 170-175# range and 5’6” with “mama hips”. I got size large

Yes yes yes! I ordered 2 pair the camo and white marble. Omg yes! Lol had to leave a review! Excellent quality super comfy perfect fit for me. 5'5 148lbs. They feel super thin and breathable but total coverage . Definitely recommend!

I love these leggings!! I am 5’4” and 130 pounds. I bought a size small and they fit so perfectly. I’ve got a fairly big butt for my size and they pass the squat test. The material is awesome, pockets are great. I’m going to be wearing these all the time.

Nice compression and high waist, but runs small. 3 month postpartum review I like the thickness and compression of these but they definitely run small. High waisted is accurate. Overall I would not replace them with smaller ones, but would just return them. The material was ok, they are not transparent.

Each legging varies!! I discovered these leggings back in 2019 & have written raving reviews as I thought they were the most amazing workout pant I’ve ever worn. I am beginning to replace older pairs as I work out a lot and they are experiencing some wear & tear. I am extremely disappointed in the newer ones that I have ordered. The fabric varies from legging to legging, the sizing seems to vary as well & today on my most recent purchase I noticed neither pair matched with the website description of 88% polyester & 12% spandex. I am 160 pounds, 5’8” & the medium was a perfect fit. These leggings were always reliable, good quality and GREAT for running, HIIT workouts, lifting & lounging. I also notice the crop had gotten shorter & I never used to notice how much my ankle was exposed (especially in the winter months) . These are still my favorite leggings, but it’s been extremely annoying buying the same size product in different colors only to be surprised with how each one fits. Just an FYI.

Super comfortable Exactly what I was looking for in a lounger/jogger style pants. Perfect for working out, laying around the house, or running errands. They have a fitted yet loose fit in all the right places. The material is stretchy yet almost silky feeling. I would buy several of these again. I got a large and am 5' 6" and 155lbs and they fit perfectly at my waste. If you're a bit taller you could wear them down more and get some extra length but you would have a slightly baggier section at the top between the legs.

Love The Gym People I have so many leggings from this brand and love them all. I am 5'7 and 145lb and have always ordered a small (even when I was a few pounds lighter) and the small works best for me.

Fits without sag Looking at the size chart, I took the lower range of my waist size. And went with a M. Normally a L, but gave it a shot. Love the fit. The crotch does not sag or cameltoe split, the length is good! The V-waist is awesome not to pull on my belly ring. Minimal pulling up. (Fits my non-existent carves too) I am 5'9", 165lbs normally wear about a 10 pants size. Was worried about the sag and shift but after a few washes even, great fit and my favorite pair of leggings. Not translucent either.

Everyone should have the staple black pair of leggings in their collection and these would make a great addition. I got a Large in them and they fit perfect so I would say true to size, if you're in between you could go either way as they do have a lot of stretch to them, it would just depend on how tight you want them, for these type of leggings I use more for comfort that actually working out so I don't like them to be too tight so a Large was a perfect fit for me.Fabric is more of a performance material - not the buttery soft fabric like the Lulu aligns or anything - so it is smooth on the outside - I will say they are still very soft and comfortable for a more performance material. Length will definitely be full length for most people - if you're on the taller size then these will become more of a just above the ankle length - I am 5'4" and personally I am fine with either a fuller length legging or a slightly shorter length so these get A++ for the length they have and being able to accommodate lots of people.The leggings in general have minimum seams to them - no side pockets on this set - it has a slightly tapered seam in the back (more on that later) but overall if you like minimal seams then you will like these for sure. I do get rolling down in the stomach area with these which isn't uncommon in leggings as I carry a lot of my weight in the midsection so something to consider - Overall I am very impressed by the comfort and quality of these leggings, if it wasn't for two things I would have rated these a 4/4.5 stars.The reason I gave them only 3 stars is for two reasons: The biggest reason for me is the back seam detailing - it is very low - at least on me and the reason this annoys me is because they feel like they are falling down all the time when they aren't because the seam hits me below my hips so I constantly want to pull them up and drives me a little bananas. The 2nd reason is that due to the type of material stuff clings to them - as you can see in the last picture, hair, dust etc. really cling to them which isn't the end of the world but it is a bit annoying when you have to constantly "clean" them off.

I’m no stranger to black yoga pants (read - my go-to everyday wear pants). I own several different kinds which I rotate regularly. I can say that these leggings are top notch! They are super soft on the outside, and a more slippery material on the inside which makes them easier to get on/off. They are actually high rise I don’t feel the need to tug on them constantly to cover my belly button. They are stretchy and comfortable. They also have a little inner pocket at the front waist for keys. My only disappointment is there being no side pocket for my phone. That would have made them perfect! They are light so good for warm to moderately cool weather - good for this in-between season time. For reference I am 30” waist and 40” hips and a Large fits me. 37$ is a little bit more than I’d want to pay - you can get similar tights at Winners for 30$.

Worse than I imagined I didn't go in to this with super high hopes. Unfortunately it was a lot worse than I could have imagined. Even aside from the 'runs' these have like pantyhose get. I ordered up for length and it's just super ridiculous. The one photo shows how low the crotch lands when the ankles are long enough, and the other how high when they are pulled up. I'm a medium usually and although I went up several sizes, they actually still did fit fine around the waist but not knees. No length seems to be added for sizing up. The result was super awkward fitting tights. I love the print but that was not enough to save this. If you have long legs or are over 5'6" imo avoid these sheer tights or you'll be super disappointed.

Hanes EcoSmart Joggers I like these! They're cozy and for the price I ordered them at ($16) I think they're just right. They feel nice, they've washed nice but my biggest issue is that they don't have a drawstring. Sizing for reference - I like my sweatpants to have a more "boyfriend" fit so I ordered a size up (X-Large). I am 175 lbs and 5'3" and normally wear a Large/Size 12 in pants. Amazon suggested the Large for me but I ordered the XL. Pros: +Thickness - listed as "midweight" and I'd say this is accurate +super soft for the price +wash well +slim fit (I like a slimmer silhouette on my sweats so that I feel more put together if I wear them out of the house) +Pockets!! +stretchy waistband Cons: -Ankle taper - I wish the jogger cut ankle taper band was a little smaller. -No drawstring

So comfy I love wearing sweat pants on days when I don't have to go anywhere, or especially if it's a snow day. There's just something awesome about them that kind of helps you to relax when you wear them. Now, I must admit that I have bought and wore this brand many times before, but not this particular kind. Needless to say, if you, me, or anyone else sees the name Hanes on the tag - you know that it's a good product. Since I've never wore this kind of sweats before, I wasn't really sure on the size. I tried them on the night they were delivered and much to my surprise, they fit perfectly!! I'm 5'9" and around 170 pounds. These are so soft and comfortable to wear that I almost don't want to take them off!!! The pockets are a good size - and thank goodness they're not the fake pockets that a lot of other brands have, which I do not understand whatsoever. I cannot recommend this pair of sweat pants more!! If you don't want them to possibly shrink, I definitely suggest washing them in cold water and drying on low or no heat. That's what I always do and it works great. I promise you will love how these feel when you put them on!

Nice midweight sweats These hanes ecosmart joggers are a nice thickness and are comfortable. I ordered my normal size small and the waist is a little too loose on me and there's no drawstring to tighten it. Im 5'11" and they're a little short on me too but most joggers are. These are pretty nice joggers but the fit is just slightly off for me.

Very comfortable worth a try! These joggers are super comfortable, waist band isn’t too tight, also ankles are not too tight. Perfect for coming home from a long day and getting into your comfy clothes. Highly recommend these joggers!

I bought these pants for the sole purpose of wearing them at home. I have several allergies and synthetic fabrics irritate my skin. I needed 100% cotton pants to lounge in. They serve the purpose. They’re comfy, they don’t irritate me, and they fit me well. They’re not the most fashionable pants but I don’t mind since I’m only wearing them at home. For sizing reference, I’m 5’4, 132 pounds, and wear a size 6/8 pants and I ordered a small.

You know it's good quality..it's by Hanes When I saw the Hanes brand I knew what to expect & I was not disappointed. Instead of guessing if I need a large or XL I ordered a Medium size. The fit is perfect, not tight anywhere or to baggy & the waist is comfortable. I like the wide elastic on the ankles & I fold the material over the elastic so the pants don't drag on the ground. I'm around 5'1". The 2 deep pockets on these sweat pants are a total plus & they lay flat. The fleece inside the pants is not so heavy that you could only wear them in a snow climate. I live in CA and can wear them right now b4 Thanksgiving. I went with the nice Java Frost Tan color for something different instead of the same-o black but the color doesn't stand out on my skin color like it does on the striking model in the photo. It almost blends in with my skin color so I'm glad they're not tight leggings or from a distance it would look like I forgot to put pants on. You will really like these sweatpants no matter your height. Excellent quality. No need to size up.
Perfect. I love these. They are so comfortable and soft. Exactly what I was looking for and will probably get them in more colors! The fit is great too! I’m 5’6” about 180 and got the large.

High waist, “old school” style I am 5’6” and 125 lbs. I wear small or medium in sweats/leggings. I ordered the small Hanes sweatpants. The fit is great in the waist and length. They do have functional hip pockets but no drawstring. The waist is high and hits above the navel. The fleece is soft and the black has a slightly faded tint. The legs are baggy and the cuffs at the ankles are the skinny type rather than the thicker “jogger” style. These Hanes are more 90’s/early 2000’s style that I have noticed the teens wearing.

Nice with some minor annoyances These are basic sweatpants in a pretty pink color. They are an 80/20 cotton/polyester blend. The gathered waistband has covered elastic just under 2" wide, and the cuffs are gathered with 2" wide covered elastic. The 32" inseam is plenty long for my long-legged 5' 6.5" frame. The sizing is generous overall, but there's no drawstring to adjust the fit/make sure your pants stay up so picking the right size really matters. The pockets can hold a small smartphone, but they are shallow enough that it tends to fall out when sitting. The tags are a bit scratchy so these might not be your cup of tea if you are sensitive to sensory issues. Overall, these are adequate sweatpants, in my opinion, but there's nothing about them that makes them a compelling choice.

At the current price of 29.99, I find these to be overpriced.

Decent quality - true to size I saw a lot of reviews saying they weren't 100% cotton, mine say they are. True to size and comfortable. The waste band is a good thickness and the draw strings work well. On the thinner side but I was expecting that, overall great for the price. They do have an odd smell right out of the package but nothing a wash won't fix. If you have long legs you might need to size up, I'm 5'3 and got an XL and they're a great length on me.

Comfy & 100% Cotton! I only wear Single-Fiber clothing and prefer natural fibers. These Hanes women's joggers are comfortable, fit well (medium, I'm 5'3". 130lbs) Really like the cuffed ankles, keeps me warm and pant legs stay down when I sleep in these- which I'm doing all winter long. Pockets are well sized and positioned- not a lot of bulging. (Who wants their hips to look wider? Right!?) Waist is comfy and non binding and doesn't make my old lady belly look more old-lady. Very good purchase just sad I can't get more colors and that they are out of my size.

Not bad, but not the thick "sweat pant" of old I usually wear a size10-12. I bought the large in these. They fit nicely with a bit of room, perhaps they might shrink a little. They are about 2-3inches too long. I like a 31inch inseam. Even if they shrink, I’ll need to roll the top or hem the bottom. With the drawstring, I can snug them up and they fit well enough for now. They are a light weight material. They do not have a fuzzy inside, and the density is akin to maybe 1 1/2 to 2 t-shirts of thickness. SO, these are not overly warm, but are fine for around the house, and especially suited for spring or summer weather.

Perfect work at home/leggings alternative; Plus the price is great! I like to "dress" for work even though I'm working from home during the pandemic. For cold days I wanted an alternative to leggings and these pants fit that purpose. I would describe the fabric as a "tight knit" so not too much like sweatpants in appearance, but I probably would not wear these to the office (if I ever work in my office again!). I ordered Large after reading reviews that these fit a little big. I am 5'8", 195, usually wear size 14/L-XL in bottoms. The fit is slightly loose, which I like. I have long legs and the length falls just below my ankle/top of my foot. I ordered Charcoal and Black and will look at some other colors. Update: the black are not as loose a fit as the charcoal, so size varies a little.

Well made and fits as expected (5’2” 135lbs) They’re very comfortable and pretty warm. They’re great for the fall, or just on cooler days. I love that they actually have pockets! They’re not super thick, and they don’t feel cheap at all. I have a short torso, so this mid-rise is great. I’m 5’2” so they’re long on me, but I’m used to that. I have to fold the bottom up 3.5” if I don’t want it to touch the ground. I’m 135lbs and they fit just right. As long as I do something about the extra length, they don’t make me look frumpy. These are my go-to pants when I just need to throw something on.

Great for Teams, lightweight and comfy These pants were just what I needed. I do bodybuilding competitions and needed something lightweight, plain, with long sleeves/long pants to sleep in and wear around the event so the heavy spray tan didn’t get all over the sheets and venue hall. I wanted something 100% cotton because I didn’t want to sweat and streak. I found them! They match the Hanes zip up hoodie perfectly and I was able to get my coaches logo printed on them easily since there are no other designs. The inside is a thin, breathable feel. I didn’t find that they shrunk at all when I washed them but I did tumble them on a very low delicate cycle to avoid this as much as possible. The length of these are long, which I was happy with, but one hem is slightly longer than the other. This didn’t bother me since it was less than an inch. Overall they are comfortable and light and a good price for what I needed. I am 5ft 4in - 119lbs - size small in both the pants and hoodie. Pictures taken after 1 wash.

Does not feel like there's much cotton in the blend These sweatpants (for lack of a better word) look nice and fit well and are very comfortable, but I don't believe that the fabric is 60% cotton. I have numerous other items of clothing that have the 60/40 cotton-poly blend and you can feel that there is cotton in the blend. These sweatpants feel like they are 80% polyester at least. I think they are mislabeled or there is active deception at work. They are not thick and warm, they are thin and lightweight. I would not call them sweatpants, but not sure what else to call them. I bought a size medium. I'm 5'4", 142 lbs.

Just okay I took a chance on these pants because they were under $10. The fit is weird everywhere. I'm only 5'2" so I expected the pants to be longer than capri length, but they hit a couple inches above the ankle. This might be okay if they didn't flare out at the bottom. The waistband doesn't have enough elasticity, and even when I pull the drawstring as tight as possible the pants are still a little big (I weigh about 115 pounds). If I roll the waistband over the pants fit okay, but I will only be wearing them for pajamas.

Comfortable and nice enough to wear out of house Comfortable, wide, gentle waistband with drawstring is nice! Best of all, these taper nicely so that if most of your weight is in the stomach, you're not left with billowing fabric around thighs and legs. They're actually flattering enough to be fine for running errands, or casual wear. The side seam on legs is prominent, that is, the stitching is visible (like top-stitching?), but the thread color blends well with the fabric, and I'm fine with it. (see pic) The fabric is on the thin side, but very soft and comfortable against the skin. Reminds me of some Lululemon joggers from a few years back. I ordered another pair right away - they were so comfy. Size Medium feels perfect for me, at 5'6"' and 136 lbs (have to use drawstring - there is ample room in waistband).

Sized weird, wrong color These were soft and thin, but the package says the color is black Heather, but I held up a rich black blanket as a color comparison to show they are gray. My order says I ordered gray, but I'm fairly certain I ordered black. Either way something is wrong because the package and color of the pants don't match. Other than that, they're sized weird. They're mostly fitted to my legs, but the waist was too big in a way not even tying them would fix. I ordered size large at 5'6" 190 pounds wearing size 12 jeans, and I usually wear large and maybe extra large. Never medium, but even if I did get a medium in this, they would practically be leggings considering how fitted the legs already are. I'll be returning them

Ok for around the house These are definitely worth the sale price. I am 5’3, 165, normally wear a 10-12 in pants that have a little stretch. After reading reviews I bought the medium. They fit really well, BUT they are odd in shape. There are also no pockets, it has what is called a mid rise-the distance between the crotch & waist-which is perfect for me, but if you read a description on pants that says the waist falls at “your natural waist” and you like that, then these may not fit. They might be more of a low rider on you. I’m short, so I love a mid or short rise…These are also a good length on me…soooo if you’re taller…..The material is more like a heavy t-shirt, they are not fleecy or fuzzy warm, and as I labeled it, I think these are fine for house lounging but they aren’t, imho, nice enough to wear out or even if company came over…not quite pj bottoms, but not too far off the mark.

SIZING IS WAY BIG Wow...these ARE HUGE! I am 5’5 128 lbs. I usually wear a 4 and I was hoping these would be baggy but I’m not sure they’re going to stay up when I walk! I’m hoping that if I wash them in hot water and dry them on high heat they will shrink! I’ll update if they do.

Small would fit LeBron James! I included this photo of my bewildered face--yes this is the recommended size Small. The waist comes up to my ribcage. The sizes are off. Hanes to the rescue 😗💨 I was afraid to purchase these at first because I always need to try on my pants because of my shape. I’m 5’4” and weigh around 155 lbs, I have thick hips/thighs and booty and majority of the time I wear a medium in pant sizes. The medium fits just right for me, the material is nice and thick, they’re not too long (Depending on the type of shoe you’re wearing) and the color is a true black. I like my pants kind of fitted, but if you don’t then I do suggest that you go up a size. For these to be Hanes sport they’re good quality, I recommend these and I will definitely be purchasing another pair.

Great fit for plus size! 10/10 recommend!! I’m 5’7 wearing a 20 and these fit perfect! They have the right amount of stretch in the waist, love the pockets! I’m gonna need one in every color I love these pants they fit very comfortably and the waist still had plenty of room to stretch. Super comfy and true to size. I love these pants! Super comfy. Super cute. And the size was spot on. I'm 5'8" and was worried they would be too short but they weren't. They were perfect. Love the pockets and the bow.

Discovering the Ideal Fit: Awkward Pockets, Functional Waistband, & Versatile Comfort SIZING & FIT I found myself quite torn between sizes when selecting these pants, so I decided to try both a small and medium. After much consideration, I have decided to keep the black ash camo in a size small and plan to purchase a pair in black in a size medium when I have the funds. The size choice ultimately depends on the desired look and feel. I have provided pictures wearing both the small and medium sizes to illustrate the difference. The small size offers a flattering fit without sacrificing comfort, while the medium size exudes a laid-back, jogger-style vibe with a more relaxed fit. When I asked my other half for his opinion, I first showed him the mediums. He preferred the smalls, citing a better fit for my body type. Although I leaned towards the mediums initially due to owning plenty of yoga pants and I was seeking comfy joggers, his insight prompted me to reconsider my choice. For reference, I am 5'8" tall (these pants hit right at my ankles) and weigh 123 lbs., with an average to athletic build. My current measurements are bust 35 inches, waist 27 inches, and hips 35 inches. POCKETS The pockets on these pants feel a bit awkward to me as they are positioned higher up than I am used to. When I put my phone in the side/s pocket (iPhone SE), I have to push it over further, so it stays in place, but it does stay secure. It ends up sitting more on my hip. The zipper on the right front pocket operates smoothly in both directions. OVERVIEW I really appreciate that functionality of the adjustable waistband on these pants; it's not just for show, which I have unfortunately encountered in the past. While I was initially wary of the thickness of the waistband depicted in the product photos, it is not uncomfortable at all. These pants have a soft, pleasant feel against my skin. They are versatile enough to wear for workouts, lounging around the house, running errands, or simply dressing casually. These are excellent joggers that I highly recommend!

When I asked my other half for his opinion, I first showed him the mediums. He preferred the smalls, citing a better fit for my body type. Although I leaned towards the mediums initially due to owning plenty of yoga pants and I was seeking comfy joggers, his insight prompted me to reconsider my choice. For reference, I am 5'8" tall (these pants hit right at my ankles) and weigh 123 lbs., with an average to athletic build. My current measurements are bust 35 inches, waist 27 inches, and hips 35 inches. POCKETS The pockets on these pants feel a bit awkward to me as they are positioned higher up than I am used to. When I put my phone in the side/s pocket (iPhone SE), I have to push it over further, so it stays in place, but it does stay secure. It ends up sitting more on my hip. The zipper on the right front pocket operates smoothly in both directions. OVERVIEW I really appreciate that functionality of the adjustable waistband on these pants; it's not just for show, which I have unfortunately encountered in the past. While I was initially wary of the thickness of the waistband depicted in the product photos, it is not uncomfortable at all. These pants have a soft, pleasant feel against my skin. They are versatile enough to wear for workouts, lounging around the house, running errands, or simply dressing casually. These are excellent joggers that I highly recommend!

comfortable, flattering, reasonable, and OK for shorter folks! i can't tell you how much searching i've done on joggers/loungers, and until these arrived i resolved to give up Amazon hunting and just go to the store. i've had the size M army green joggers for over a month now (washed + worn 4x now, still great). i'm 5'2", 163 lbs, athletic build, hourglass shape (more butt/thighs than average jane, but nothing drastic). photo doesn't do them justice (i tried, haha) but they are very soft/sleek-feeling, comfortable, flattering, great pocket room (with a cute zippered mini pocket), and they have the perfect amount of elastic at the waist and ankles - won't squeeze you to cause rolls but stays where it needs to (the ankle elastic is also for the shorties). i will warn that if you're heavier and/or have extra curves/rolls/dimples, these pants may show those (they do cling slightly and the material tends to hug a bit, but comfortably). to avoid that, a darker color would probably be best (that's why i ordered dark, i don't know that a lighter shade would work for me). i've returned every pair i've tried in the past 2 months *except for these*, and i just ordered another pair in gray (M) today. The only reason I'm not also ordering more (or in their capri style) is because it's cold weather now and i'm still losing weight/toning, so I may be in a size S by warmer weather time. but i fully plan on ordering the capri style too. i can wear them to workout, while running errands, or to lounge around the house. Oh, and they're very reasonably priced. Perf. Highly recommend these!

Super cute pants but? Okay I love the look of these pants, beautiful color, style is cute and although very thin, they look nice on. Why am I returning them? For me it's a personal preference, I ordered a medium because I figured they would be more generous in the waist, my waist is 27 inches. I really dislike anything tight around my waist. I am very picky about how things feel on me, if I feel scrunched in before I eat? I can only imagine how uncomfortable it will be after I eat. With these pants, they're not super tight but they felt just too constricting for me in the waist area. The bottoms are also elastic - not my preference again. I actually think a large would fit me better and flow better. I'm 5'5 and 110 lbs and the smallest part of me is my waist so....well? Try them on but I would size UP - especially if you like things not to feel tight. Pretty pants though huh? They look cute on in my pics - just wish I felt more comfortable in them.

They are super comfy though and relatively flattering. I’m 5’3” and 140 pounds. I do have a bit of a pooch in my lower stomach (FUPA) which you can see in these pants. Don’t expect them to hide anything. Also the band around the ankles is very cheaply made and a little tight. I don’t have particularly large ankles or anything. The pair of pants that I bought it doesn’t really matter if the seams match. The pattern hides unmatching seems really well. I noticed that in some reviews on pants where there was a distinct line they complained that the seams didn’t match. Overall they’re a nice pair of pants for festivals and other special occasions.

Not flattering on everyone I'm 5'2"...ish 130lbs. With my sneakers, these graze the floor. If you're shorter then 5'3" you may want a petite. Not sure if it's because I'm shorter, but joggers just don't look right on me. I'm bottom heavy and these balance that out well, but they have a lot of fabric in the front and um... Idk what to say about the unfortunate way it works itself into my butt... So maybe not the best of your chonkier in the bottom. The fabric is soft but not very stretchy. The cuffs ride up pretty high in a sitting position because the fabric doesn't give and stretch while you bend. The waist band is comfortable with a firm hold. Your pants are unlikely to slide down with full pockets. If I sized down, I would not be able to tuck in a shirt. You'll see it.

The talls are about 3-4 inches longer in leg and rise. Ladies, I am 5’7” and I usually wear a Small (Cherokee) or Medium (Healing hands or Greys anatomy) regular length scrub pant, but decided to try a tall just to see if the length would give a slimming effect. Well unfortunately the Medium Tall is way to big on me. Not only are the talls about 4 inches longer in the legs but, the rise was about 3 inches higher as well. I put my size M regular length pants (green) over the size M talls so you can see the difference. (These are the exact same pants, unwashed.) Just wanted to share in case this would help someone make a decision on what length to get.

Ceil color is too short Let me start off by saying, I am a loyal heart and soul customer. All of my scrub bottoms are from heart and soul. Perfect if you are petite—UPDATED REVIEW Height: 4'11” Bust: 34B Waist: 23-24 in Weight: 95 lbs Original 4/2018 Review: I'm a XXS petite in both Cherokee and Grey's Anatomy scrub pants, and they are always still a little big on me. I’m small chested and not “curvy”. This is my first time purchasing HeartSoul scrubs. I love the jogger style of these pants. The fabric is definitely more of an activewear type fabric compared to my other scrubs. They aren't see through. Not too thick or thin, in my opinion. They're not warm like my other scrubs, but I live in Southern Arizona, so it's perfect. If I wear the waistband slightly below my waist, it's still a little scrunched at the bottom, but no big deal. I work as a nurse in a hospital, and these are still professional looking. It's about time I found a pair of scrub pants that don't drag on the floor. In the original photos (first 4), I have the pants paired with the HeartSoul Pitter Pat top in XXS. UPDATED REVIEW 1/4/22: I had purchased two pairs of these joggers in 4/2018. They are still going strong. I added one new picture. I’ve washed them sooooo many times, and they have not even lost their color, it’s crazy. No exaggeration. Side pocket zipper, drawstring, etc. are all intact and functional. I have tried the Figs and the Cherokee infinity joggers and have found Figs to look weird on me because I’m short and the Cherokee infinity was way too suffocating on the waist. These are very comfortable and an hesitant to buy any other brand.

Warm/Cozy Yes, my mirror needs windex lol, sorry in advance. I was looking for a pair of joggers that weren’t super thin or silky, these feel more substantial compared to most others. They’ll be great for walking across campus in the winter months and just something comfy to wear in general. I ordered medium and they fit relatively well, I am between 5’7” - 5’8” tall, 150-155 lbs athletic/average build. I do wish there was about an inch or so more on length, but I kind of expected them to be a bit short since I am on the taller side. The pockets are nice and roomy/deep and there seems to be a lot of wiggle room around the hip area, I wish they were a little more slim fit but that may take away some of the comfort idk. I could roll the waist band down and wear them lower, or keep it up and wear around my natural waist if I wanted. The band does feel just a tiny bit tight right now but expect it to loosen up a bit after being worn a few times, at least I hope so bc I always feel they’re more comfortable that way. All in all they are nice, and I’ll be wearing them. I took off one star for length and fit in the hip area.

Very nice quality, size up if you are between sizes. I am 5'7" and 164lbs with a 31" waist. I normally wear between a size L in bottoms (between size 10 and 12). Based on the size chart I should have been a size L, but I size up to an XL because I hate it when the waistband on a legging cuts in to my stomach. The size XL fit great, I could have probably worn a L as well.These do have a thin elastic band around the top of the waistband, so if that sort of thing bothers you or pinches you I would size up.This is a wonderful midweight fleece, that is super soft on the inside and has a nice sleek exterior. The color is true to the product picture. I love the two deep drop pockets on the side and love even more the fact that one of these has a zippered pocket on front of it. I also really like that there is a loop to hang a badge or keys or whatever off of. This is a nice feature. I often wear these types of fleece leggings with a dressier tunic and boots to work and always struggle with where I should clip my badge.I would highly recommend them, they are very nice. I have only washed them once so far but I didn't have any issues with shrinkage or pilling.

Comfortable and form flattering I love fleece lined leggings especially in the cooler months. These are warm but breathable so even in mild temperatures they don’t overheat. These leggings can sit high on the waist which emphasizes hip curvature and a narrow waist. The deep pockets are perfect for storing a phone especially if using during a workout with earbuds. There’s also one zipper pocket that is shallow but could store money, cards in case one doesn’t want to carry a purse or wallet. I’m 5ft about 108 lbs and the XS fits true to size. The material is smooth and stretchy but can pick up fine hair such as that of pets. The seaming in the back discreetly flatters the shape of derrière there’s also a small fabric tag on the front hip I assume to attach keys. Nit exactly a safety feature but I appreciate that the design keeps the active wearer in mind. With the material being moisture wicking, it’ll be nice to choose these leggings regardless of the precipitation. The value for the price is reasonable as this will be a well loved pair.

Helped Loads With the Cold! These leggings are very cute and functional. I needed fleece leggings as I work with kids and the mobility of leggings with pockets is essential for me. I’m barely 5’1 and they bunched up at my angle, so I’ll need to get them hemmed. It’d be nice to have a petite option, but it’s not a major issue for how much warmer I am at work. I’ve only had them for a couple weeks, and I’m curious to see how well the material will withstand pilling at the thighs. Will update if there are major issues.

Loose ankle area I really, really wanted to like these pants. They are buttery soft and warm. The length was great for my 30" inseam legs (being 5'6" and these hem of the pants hitting my ankle). The pants were 10.5" around in that area, so it was loose from below my calf to my ankle. The first pic shows how loose they were. The second on how a tight fit would be on me. Disappointed in how it fit on me, but if you like that fit, then it's a great product!

Style is great but quality could be better I am 5'1, 166lbs. I originally ordered the medium 29"inseam in black a few weeks back and they fit perfect. I just ordered another pair (medium) in the navy blue and gray pink and since they dont offer that color in 29", I ordered the 30" for both. They arrived at the same time and it was instantly apparent that there is a quality assurance issue. The gray pink is a large and at least 32" inseam if not longer even though the label reads (M). The blue came looking exaclty like the first black one I ordered. As you see in the picture, there is a huge difference in the two even though they are ordered in the same size. The other thing I've noticed on the black one is the fabric is pilling on the inside near the front of the ankles. I really love these pants for the squat proof aspect, the tapered waist, and the comfort however there are some improvements that can be made to the quality of the fabric and the quality assurance of sizing. I am more than happy to pay extra for a longer lasting material but beggars can't be choosers so wish me luck on my next order!

Comfortable, versatile and good fit I bought these yoga pants for use at the gym and for daily activities. They did not disappoint. I like comfortable and versatile clothes that I can wear for those and most of my other activities. These pants fit the bill. The quality is good and the fabric is a thicker knit and not thin and flimsy. I bought the black in the 28" length, which was perfect, and selected the style with pockets.
