Flattering, Well-Fitting, Heavyweight, Warm, Comfy-Cozy Slacks I was not expecting such a high quality, heavyweight pair of trousers when I ordered these! I'm blown away! These would be perfect to travel to colder climates in or simply to wear on cool days in more moderate climates. They are really comfortable and they look very nice on...like an expensive pair of business trousers. The back half of the waistband has elastic which could easily be concealed by simply running a belt through the belt loops. I found no fabric content tag on or in the garment, but the listing states that the fabric is 70% polyester/30% wool. Laundry instructions were attached to the trousers on a tag connected by a string (which you can see in some photos I've attached). It does not specify dry cleaning. It suggests washing by hand, avoiding high heat, and not wringing them. I ordered size Medium in Black and they fit me perfectly (without much extra room) except that they are about an inch or so too long. For reference, I'm 5'4" and long-waisted (therefore somewhat shorter-inseamed). My waist is about 29", my hips are about 38", and I'm about 124 lbs.

Cute, warm, thick These pants exceeded my expectations. I feel like I've walked out of a dark-academia mood board with these pants combined with fall styling. The pants are thick in material, soft, and easy to clean. They're not transparent or thin, but they do have enough fit in the back to show undergarment lines so I'd recommend seamless undergarments. The band hits my waist and holds up securely, that's usually an issue for me with pants so I'm very thankful for the fit! My only note is that I'm short so they're a bit long on me unless I'm pairing them with boots, so if you're 5'4 or below like me, expect to wear something with heels. I'm 5'4, 110lbs, a 24in waist, and typically between an x-small and small for fit reference.

True to size- on the fence about these.... Update: I wore these last weekend in 30 degree weather 20-30mph winds walking around the city. These jeans are warm! They are not super cute. Wear a long top to cover the bulky pocket area and tuck them into tall boots and they will look fine.(if it's really cold you'll prob be doing this anyway.) If the ankle was skinnier it would be ok. Wish they were a tad longer as I have long legs. Good for the price and very warm!!! Im on the fence with these. I ordered a medium. I'd say they are true to size. I am 5'7, 135lbs. I picked medium because I'm on the bigger end of size 6 and I'm tall. I never wear a size 4 but sometimes I could be an 8 if the jeans run small. My favorite jeans are size 6 long from american eagle. These fit fine, they're a little thicker but I wouldnt say make you look overweight. If you're buying these jeans I'm assuming fashion isnt your #1 priority but you dont want to wear boyfriend cut jeans from eddie bauer either. That being said my main problem with these jeans is the pelvic area. Not very fashionable. The zipper feels weird and the pockets pull showing the inside a bit near the belt loop. Check out my pics. The seam on the left leg wants to pull inward too. If I'm wearing these jeans for fashion, I wouldnt recommend but if youre wearing boots and a big sweater or coat then I say yes buy them. They feel like they're going to be super warm. I'll update after wearing in the cold.

Review from the plus side The reason I gave these jeans a try was bc in the description, it says they changed the sizing to US sizes. I’m a true-ish size 16 (US), if stretchy can fit 14. I stand about 5’ 11”. I really like these jeans! The length is perfect IMO, they hit right above my ankle bone. They have a decent amount of stretch and are jean material (not jegging or legging material). The fit is perfect except for a few things which may just be bc of my body type. Now in the pics you can see the pulling of the pockets, and the zipper fell throughout the day, but the fit is still pretty good for me. Big butt and thighs are hard to dress. I’m worried that if I go a size larger, they will be too loose in the waist (& possibly everywhere else) and fall. The reason I knocked off a star is bc the lining isn’t like pictured; it’s not velvety like shown. It’s basically the lining in fleece lined leggings. Still soft and should be warm, but still.

Warm & Comfortable So warm and most importantly COMFORTABLE! I'm 5'2" 114lb and I wear size 2 in jeans so I ordered size S. Way too stretchy and loses its shape I have pros and cons for these jeans. Pros: definitely warm (which is why I bought them) Pro: Xs small fit great I’m 5’4 105lbs Pro: comfortable but only for a time which leads me into the cons Con: they are verrryy stretchy. So while they are comfortable and they fit good that only lasts for a while before they stretch out and your butt is super baggy. Washing them seems to put them back into place until you wear them and a few hrs later your back to baggy. Con: very high fit! You have to yank these things way up past your belly button for them to fit good. So I’d buy them again for the warmth part but I was really hoping to look good while being warm. Of course they are longer on me (since I'm so short) but the cuffed ankle doesn't allow that to be a problem. Pic is taken upon immediately opening them from the package. Color looks as advertised for the dark grey. The material is on the thinner side so you can wear them outside without burning alive, but they're still plenty warm for the cold! Average leggings that don't quite live up to expectations Let's start with the basics. I'm 5'4", 150 pounds. Ordered the large-xlarge and the fit is fine. The length is also good; it's full length as long as you don't bend or squat. And, despite how thin they are, they did seem squat-proof in the mirror. They are comfortable. Although they are polyester, they do feel breathable. However, that's really where the pluses end. They have no compression or tummy-sucking power at all, despite claiming "tummy control"; every bump and lump shows up, even in black. They do have pockets, but they are just up to the wrist in length, and just barely fit my Samsung galaxy 9 - a larger phone will stick out. Also, since they do not compress, my phone bounces out of the pocket with any sort of jumping around. They also have some fleecy lining, but it is SO THIN! These are appropriate for a cool indoor environment, but not for any sort of outside cold weather. Overall, they are good lounging leggings, but not appropriate for actual exercise-wear or outdoor warmth.

Good material, but size up I am 5’ 10” and weigh 145lb and I ordered the size 6 regular which is my typical pant size. Unfortunately, I did not like the way these fit and would prefer a size 8 long. The material feels comfortable and lightweight and is quick drying. There is an elastic string inside the waistband with an opening in the front if you need to tighten the waist. This elastic opening also leaves two weird bulges in the middle of the waistband. I think the ankle cuff should have been slightly larger around to prevent the excessive bunching happening at the bottom of the pants. I plan to use these pants for hiking and kayaking. I do suggest that others size up in the waist size and length. Overall, the material is nice, but I recommend others size up in waist and length size. I also think there are a few technical issues with the design.

Lightweight joggers but short on me! Fits more like capris. The Women's Cargo Joggers are lightweight and quick-drying, making them suitable for hiking and other outdoor activities. The fabric is not as comfortable as yoga pants. It is more lightweight, kind of noisy when you walk but it does have a decent stretch, allowing for easy movement. I appreciate the seven pockets, which are useful for carrying essentials. However, the fit could be improved. The length is not for tall people. I am 5'9" and weigh 124 pounds and got a size 4. so the sizing also runs a bit small, so it's advisable to order a size up. Additionally, the material, while quick-drying, feels somewhat flimsy and might not hold up well to rougher conditions. Overall, these joggers are functional for light outdoor activities and travel but have some shortcomings in size, fit and durability.

Good fabric and comfortable I am 104 lbs and ordered a size xs. I work out daily, and didn't want to spend the money on lulus. These are very comfortable and kept me warm during my workouts. These are great! These are a true Legging. Thick black, go up to your actual waist vs your hip bone and are not sheer at all. I love them! I have quite a bit of a pudge in front of my stomach. Most of my jeans, even the super stretchy skinny jeans, even it out. These don't. And its very obvious. Finally these fit super well around the waist but got a bit too loose around the knees. Not much an issue but there you are. As for a huge plus! There's front pockets!🎉🎉 Im not sure if I'll return yet as they're not bad pants, just not quite right for me which is a shame because they're such a good price.

Okay so I was really hesitant to buy these. I'm 5'8" and was really worried about the length. But I took a risk and assumed I could wear them as capris, worst case. Well I'm glad I bought them. Hips are 41" around and waist is 32" so it can be hard to find pants that fit my ass that actually stay up around the waist. These pants fit my body great! They're a little snug to pull over the booty but once they're up- they're a dream. They're slightly tighter in the waist than most pants so if you are apple shaped or have a wider tummy than booty, these may not be a good fit for you. I do have muscular legs and calves and they are not uncomfortably tight in these pants. In fact, they're appropriately loose even when sitting. No tight pulling and has slight looseness and give when seated. I ordered a size medium based off reviews and they fit exactly how I expected. Large would have been too baggy and not flattering on my body for my personal taste. They do have quite a bit of stretch so I'm glad I sized-down. I'm sure they'll stretch as I wear them and I don't wash my pants after every wear so a large may have been problematic. Apologies for the crappy angle on pics. I don't have a full length mirror and had to make do with the mirror I have! I even reccomended these for my sister who is 5'10". 6ft sister MAY be okay in a large if the hem is a bit longer. They'd definitely be more classic ankle pants. At the ankle, no rolling. They're seemingly petty wrinkle resistant which is awesome. Tried them on right out of the bag and there are no creases from packaging. I will likely buy these in other colors. I purchased navy and black. Black is pictured. See no roll, one roll, two rolls. Also tried to get a pic of the waist band and rise of pants. Ordered a Medium. 5'8", 170lbs, hourglass. Great fit.

Cute! Shrank but still fits. I am 5’9” and weigh 190 lb. I typically wear a US size 12-14 and ordered an XL. The photos were taken before they were washed, and one leg was slightly shorter than the other, maybe 1/2-1”. The first time I washed them I only let them dry about 15 minutes on low temp then hung them up to finish drying. They needed to be ironed afterward and did not shrink. The second time I washed them I forgot about them, and they went through a full dry cycle on low temp. They came out wrinkle free but shrunk 2-3” in length. They also shrank in width but still fit. They wear like hot pants from the 60s. The material is a stretchy twill and I’ve received numerous compliments on them. They also hold their shape well during wear. My only caveat is that it has no pockets. I’d love a hidden pocket in the back. Overall, I think these are a good value for the money and will order more.

Order up if you want to wash! I gave these pants five stars because I like them a lot once I figured out what to buy. I usually wear a size 2/4 depending on the brand and am 5’6” tall, 120 pounds. The first pair I ordered were on this site called “ The millennium”. I ordered a small but received a medium and didn’t realize it. (they might’ve sent it to me because Company knew they were small ?) They were a little big when I tried them on and then I washed them and they fit perfect! So order up.I bought some more colors which I thought were the same, but weren’t. They were called “Point Pants”. Same brand and looked the same in the picture but weren’t the because they had more nylon in them than the millennium. I ordered a small and they fit perfect and didn’t really shrink. They were much thicker which would be good for winter, but it is spring and I wanted a thinner pant so I am keeping the medium size millennium pants so I can wash them. I hope this makes sense because these pants are great for winter and spring and summer. And they are also a great price!PS. I would’ve liked to have them a little shorter, but they were all out of the “short”pants.

Great pants for the price! These are great! I'm a college student with a 40-hour/week summer internship where I can't wear jeans, and I don't own a ton of business casual-type clothing. I only had two pairs of dress pants, so I wanted another pair to break up the outfit rotation (and to make laundry easier) but didn't want to break the bank on something I probably will only wear once during the rest of the year. These fit the bill! I am 5'7" and 140 pounds with a 28 inch waist and a 39 inch hip at the fullest point (aka I have a huge butt). I ordered the medium, and apart from being just slightly too long (by maybe half an inch, but someone with a smaller butt would probably need a hemming job), they fit really well! I like that they're a higher rise in the front (so my tummy is contained) as I just find that really comfortable. They're VERY comfy, flexible, and stretchy, so I could see someone who wears an 8 or maybe even a 10 at my height fitting in these well (I usually wear a 4 or 6 in pants depending on brand). They don't have pockets, so they're pretty much leggings made out of nicer material, but they do have inside and outside seams so they look like any other skinny fit dress pant. I ordered navy and it's very true to color. Thanks, Amazon!

White pull up pants Edited review. At first I loved how these pull on pants fit. As I looked into different makers of pull on pants. I decided on these. I loved the price. I then read the comments. Yes, buy 1 size down. I am 5'8" and weigh at average 205. These size 14 pants fit perfectly fine and comfortable. I am satisfied with my purchase. Now that I have used these pants a few times, I have discovered that when wearing cotton undies with this pants it causes for my undies and pants to slide down. I am finding the need of pulling the pants up every so often. Unfortunately, I can't return them anymore. I ended up sewing in the side seams by 1/4 inch each side.

Very good product, good quality, elegant, and greate fit. I love them! (I would love for the leggings to have a little zipper in the waist so they could be a little more tight in the abdomen, but they are wonderful nonetheless.) The fabric of these leggings is thick and helps contour the figure. They are not sports leggings, they look a lot more elegant, more like pants, so they are perfect to wear in the office. They are made in the USA! with good finishes, and the high waist is perfect to wear with blouses (which you can put inside them) But ( there is always a but) the red color at least has to be used with light-colored underwear, You can see the dark underwear a little bit through them (as you can see in the pictures). To take care of the leggings I recommend washing them in a Delicates Laundry Bags , with COLD water and avoiding sitting on surfaces such as gravel, this way they will last longer. In the long term ( 1 year if you use them ones a week) and since they wear out on the butt with rubbing (and white elastics appear). I had some that were a very small size for me and for obvious reasons the butt was worn out before. I'm 5' ,121 lb and the small size fits me.

Confortable ! In the picture - Teal in size large - regular length ( 5'7-168cm / 200lbs-90kg) I needed something confortable and decent enough to wear to work after having my last baby. I bought multiple colors starting with XXL after baby to now Large. These pants are great for work or casual depending on how you accessorize. Pro: - the pants are slim but not so Skinny - perfect for me as I have "big legs" - lots of stretch - Color does not fade overtime Pro or con depending on shape: - wait is slim and great for hourglass figure but has also lots of stretch Cons: - Durability, Not great! it starts to have the little elastic thread coming out of fabric after a little while and it's worst on the black with white thread version (Blackwhite-p44897sk). For the price and how the pants fits for me its acceptable. Just know that you will not keep this for years. I hope this helps. Good luck!!

Love this! I got this in a S, M, and L to see which one fit the best! So I got this in a small, medium and large to see which size would fit me best. I am 5'5 for height and 115-120lbs(I haven't weighed myself recently to know my weight exactly). Waist is 29inches, hips is 34 inches. The size small was soo small! Size medium seemed too small because the zipper seemed too far out but because these pants are soo stretchy, it does zip up without feeling like the button is going to rip off. The size large for me was too loose feeling for my legs. The butt, it just didn't hug my body right. The size medium was the best choice for me. And let me tell you, it is soooo comfortable! My current new favorite pants for work especially! But... these are really nice pants for wearing while I am off of work too!I love that it is stretchy. I love that I can choose the length of these pants. I ordered the medium long. I love that there is a zipper too! Zipper plus stretchy and not thin quality is just super excellent!! Get this and find the right size for you!Edit: I accidentally put this in the dryer once! Oh no!!!! But actually, I still wear this for work. I am soo surprised that I can still wear it. The zipper part on the top portion of the pants remain still stretchy. My legs fit fine as well. The only difference that I could tell is the length of the pants. It did get a lil short after being in the dryer. Not high waters short, just a lil short. Still wearable for work for me. Since this product is stretchy, it does stretch back out some. I do not recommend putting these pants in the dryer though- it smelled a lil burnt.. hang dry is best.

Great first try on I haven’t worn these out yet, but first impressions are good. The color is rich and the material feels sturdy. I’m 5’6”, ~140 lbs and the Medium fit my waist and legs great. There is a slight bunching of the fabric near the crotch but nothing crazy. I could have gone for an inch or 2 more in length, so if you have long legs, you may be getting high waters with these. The high waist and stretchy fabric make them comfortable to sit in and pretty easy to move around in.

I was SUPER skeptical of these jeans but they are really great. I like them A LOT. I gave them 4 stars only because A) the material is such that if you have cellulite or rolls of any kind, it will probably show (the material is a little thinner) and B) because if they offered an extra small option I would probably need it but it’s not available. For reference I’m 5’5” weigh 108 lbs and my dimensions are 32-24.5-35.5. I wear a small or extra small in most clothing. I often have a hard time with pants because despite not being super tall all of my height is in my legs. So the typical 28-29 inch inseam provided by most woman’s pants is just too short. (I look for 32 inseams whenever I can find them!) But these fit great! I’d prefer they be a little bit snugger around my butt and upper thighs but all things considered these pants are a win for sure. I would say pay close attention to the photo provided by the manufacturer of the pants listing the dimensions. They seem to be pretty accurate. Seriously, give these pants a shot. Especially if you’re someone who has a hard time finding them long enough. They aren’t the longest pants I own BUT they are longer than average for sure. And they are very stretchy. I can see now why people reverence being able to fit into smaller sizes quite comfortably. I’m going to buy a few more pair in different colors :) UPDATE: Love LOVE these pants!! I got them in more colors and they all fit great. They seem to wash well too. So far the colors haven’t bled at all (though I have washed them with like colors) and they have not shrunk. I have not but them in the dryer though. I did see that some of the sizing is inconsistent but nothing significant. Also, the white pants are a bit see through so be prepared to purchase nude undies for when you wear them. Otherwise I am so super happy with this pants and I can’t stress enough how hard it is to find them that fit right (especially online). TRY THESE!!! You won’t regret it.

Seriously, my New FAVORITE pair of jeans I am 5'3 and about 110 lbs, I purchased a size Medium: my legs are long and I guess for a short person my thighs and calves are bigger than the normal 5'3 person.... I'm just un proportionate... But these jeans are long enough to not look sloppy, look super cute with booties and not short enough to look like flood water pants (like the rest of my jeans). They don't ( or at lest not yet) get loose around the knees, and they have pockets! FRONT AND BACK POCKETS! which is nice b/c then my bootie doesn't look as flat as it is, and a front pocket is nice to have. I love these jeans in the fact that they are causal enough to wear to work/church and casual enough to wear to my daughters soccer practice without looking like I'm wearing leggings. I live in these jeans! Usually when i find a pair of pants, they will fit my waist and be too short or too long, or they fit my legs, but not my waist, or they are long enough and fit my waist but are too tight on my thighs... (un proportionate) These jeans are super stretchy and so comfortable that when I get home I am not dying to get out of them. They hug and fit in all the right places. I only gave 4 stars b/c the zipper doesn't like to stay up. Other than that, I want every color, I purchased black, wine and charcoal and cant decided which color is my favorite.

The only downside is that they are short in length. I am 5'3, 240ish pounds and ordered a 1x. I have a tattoo on my butt that if I am not wearing covering underwear can be seen a little through the material. But that's not enough to keep me from loving them! Definitely buying more colors!

Pretty great, maybe size up I really like these pants and man are they stretchy. I’m 5’9’ and about 150 lbs. (I’m usually an 8/10 in pants) I originally bought a large, but since I’m pear-shaped (W: 27.5”, H: 42.5”) they were tighter in the legs than I was wanting, although the waist fit perfectly. So I ordered an x-large and that was overall a better fit. I have to wear a belt to avoid slippage in the waist (story of my life) but the legs are not quite as a snug. (Except the calves…they are still really tight. Maybe I have big calves?) So I guess my advise would be to order a size or two up if you don’t want your pants to feel/look painted on. They seem pretty good quality. The material is a bit strange, but the color is a beautiful maroon. I’m pretty sure I’ll order another color sometime soon.

I want a pair in every color. Let me start off by saying I hate buying pants. Doesn't matter if it is online or in the store. I can try on 20 pairs of pants in a store and maybe, MAYBE walk out of the store with 1 pair of pants. I have a high waist, little to no eh-hem rear, and thicker thighs. This can make it very difficult to find pants that I love and that fit well. To make matters worse I am a size 7 or 27 which I have found is an increasingly hard pants size to find when you are a frugal shopper. For reference I am 5' 4" and 133 lbs. I came across these and decided to give the size Medium a shot. These pants are all I've ever wanted in pants. So far I have bought two colors: Dusty Rose and Burgundy. The colors are gorgeous. The material feels similar to jeans, but it is much softer. They are super stretchy and they fit like a glove. I really feel beautiful in them. They can be dressed up with a nice work blouse for the office or dressed down with a band t-shirt for a concert. I want to own a pair in every color and toss all my poor fitting pants. I just hope these stay available for long enough for me to buy them when I have funds. Edit: By the way... They have pockets. Real pockets. Deep pockets! Women's pants never have pockets. These are the perfect pants.

Great pants, size is forgiving, order your true size. I was looking for some comfy pants to wear during spring and summer. I’m in love with these pants, like the rest of the reviewers. I’m two months post partum, 5’9 and 220lbs. While the reviews said there was lots of stretch, I was hesitant to order my true size (XL). I ordered a 2x and it was too big. I can take them off without unbuttoning the pants! I ordered a white in 1x and fushia in xl. The xl definitely fits and is super comfortable, but as the pictures show, there is more bunching in the legs so I think the 1xl fits a little better out of the dried but the xl is better at the end of the day because it doesn’t stretch out as much. I washed the pants according to the directions and I did not notice shrinking. My pink pants did not bleed either. The white pants are see through so wear a long shirt and light underwear. Can’t go wrong with this price or these colors!

Medium WAY too big, weird cut I’m not sure what it is with the brand Hybrid & Company, but you could order five pairs of pants the same size and they’d all come out different. I always give it a 30% chance when I order…I got this red color of skinny jeans in a size small at first, and they were a bit tight and I couldn’t even button them, so I went up to a size medium which are the pants in the picture (smudge on mirror, not on pants). These must’ve been mislabeled or something because they’re HUGE. I’m 5’4” and 108 pounds but…come on. Then the smalls should’ve fit? And the cut is long & slouchy. Such a joke.

I literally love everything about these pant. They are pretty stretchy they actually stay at my ankle even after washing and drying multiple times now and still haven't shrunk up. They are actually still black and don't fade color after one wash, even now still look like when I got them and i have washed them a handful of times. There are front and back pockets!! (Which is really rare in women's pants like these) and for the price they are just AMAZING!!! I have been looking like something like these for soooo long for work because I have to wear black pants but they always tend fade out really quick or shrink up. I will definitely be getting some more! I'm 5'5 and between 103lb-107lb and wear a size 2 in all pants I own

Super stretch a tad long for petite women Purchased black in size medium for my daughter. She is 5’3” and about 140lbs. She likes the fit as they are skinny jegging type of pants, with super stretch. She wasn’t thrilled with them not being high waisted pants. She usually wears a size 8 short in AE jeans for comparison. Will update for washing and durability. Overall I think they’re perfect to wear for work or even dress up with a cute sweater or shirt. Update: washed alone and hung up to dry. I then decided to throw into dryer for five minutes to maybe shrink a little. They came out fine, and I’m ordering a second pair.

Excellent work/dress pants BUT only two pockets, rear ones are faux iChosy makes a high quality dress pant. I need this type of pant for work. I like a slimmer leg and these are a really good fit, they are a modified bootcut just as they claim. The material is a good sturdy blend, feels very supportive without being too binding because of the stretch. They are a little too high rise for me (12”rise) but are still comfortable enough. I usually need a 33” inseam and these are 32”, but somehow they are long enough for me. Another thing I like is the button closure, I hate those slide hook types that are pretty common in dress pants. Unfortunately, there are only two functional pockets, not four as stated in the description...the back two are faux pockets. For work I need four functional pockets so I won’t be able to use these for work. I took off a star in the rating because of that. Here are the actual measurements of the size 10: Waist 29” Hips 38” Rise 12” Inseam 32”

High quaity material and craftsmanship but VERY long! These were just a bit wrinkled when they came from the packaging, but right away, I felt they were really high-quality fabric. They are thicker pants with sturdy stitching, and they ironed out very easily with a quick steam/poly setting on my iron. After that, they did not wrinkle even when I grabbed them and balled them up, so that's great news. They will travel well, and when I arrive at my meetings, I won't be a wrinkly mess. The main issue is they are VERY long. I am 5'7", and these have at least two extra inches that will need to be hemmed up. I would say though, the quality of these pants is so good that I am willing to hem them rather than return them. As far as the other sizing - I wear a 16 and they were perfect. They don't ride up in the back and they are nice and flat in the front. Once hemmed, these will be my go-to pants for meetings and travel.

Extremely Comfortable & Pretty Pants I was really pleasantly surprised when I received these pants and put them on. They are very flattering and they are extremely well made. The sewing is excellent and the way they’ve put the pants together is quality. I am 5’2” and I ordered size 10 short and they are the perfect length for me. I usually wear a size 8 in stretchy pants, but I wanted to be sure that I’m going to be comfortable and that was a good decision. They fit perfectly and the cut is very good. The black color is very nice and attractive. I’m planning to purchase additional colors of these same pants. Zipper quality is smooth and well sewn too. I even received extra buttons with the pants in case one is lost. They are machine washable and they recommend hang dry which I usually do with my pants. It’s also excellent that the manufacturer offers 20 different options of sizes and length of these pants; that is impressive. I also love that these pants have pockets. A great purchase and definitely a recommend from me for anyone looking for quality good looking pants at a reasonable price.

I have received compliments from everyone I’ve seen and I bragged to my other half how beyond comfortable they are. In fact, I already ordered the black pair.

Great material but waist band runs small and doesn’t have much stretch I bought these in a size 6. Size 6 women’s at stores like JCPenny normally are my ideal size. The waist on these is absolutely Too Snug and tight , it is very uncomfortable. Normally I’m either a size S or M depending on the brand. These pants are 14 inches across the band when I measure them with tape (per see the picture attached), which seems like it could fit especially with these being advertised as stretch, I don’t think the waist is stretchy at all for this type of pants, I would probably have to size up to an 8 but I’m not sure I will be as these I am going to return due to the band being non stretchy imo. For reference right now I’m weighing around 130 lbs , my height is 5’1.5 inches my waist measures approximately 28-29 inches and my hips are 40.5 inches, athletic build.The material is smooth and pretty thick and feel comfortable on the skin as the fabric is 53% cotton 39% nylon 8% spandex , so material feels professional and soft. Front pockets are real and back pockets are fake which I think looks good because it doesn’t look bulky on the backside but smooth and sleek on the bum. Overall these pants could be great , and the inseam options are very good for different heights, but with 8% spandex I would expect way more stretch and these don’t have that. I hope this helps someone as I have a hard time buying clothing. On my experience stretch pants usually can accommodate 8-10 lbs loss or gain weight and these just aren’t forgiving and won’t stretch to your size with weight fluctuations.

Seriously good pants! I am ALWAYS afraid to order pants that I can't try on. I went by the size chart and ordered a 12 (For reference I'm 5'8 and my measurements are 41-31-42). Typically I wear an 8 or 10, but I wanted to be safe. I definitely could have ordered a size down. BUT I still seriously LOVE these pants. The material is thick, but sooooo stretchy! And they are truly high waisted, which I love. (No having to pull them up when I sit down so my kangaroo pouch isn't squeezed in half) There is a little bit of "puddling" at the ankle, but I'll usually be wearing boots with these so I don't care. And there's pockets! Good ones that I can actually put my hands all the way in! (The back pockets are not real pockets, but who cares when you have real front ones!) And although I haven't tested them to see if they are 100000% opaque, I'm pretty sure they're going to pass a lot better than some others I've tried. 1000/10 recommend.

Material that moves with you I ordered these black stretchy work pants in 14short since I'm 5'4" so I tend to need pants on the shorter side. Even though I ordered the short version, I still find I have to roll the cuff of the pants under once to give me a clean look, otherwise the pants look baggy on me- I included a photo with one pant leg cuffed and one pant leg as is. My hips measure 45" with a 34" waist, so according to the size chart I was taking a chance on my normal size 14, but aside from being a bit too long, these pants fit me like a glove. Love that the pants have an actual zipper and strong hold button closure, plus real belt loops. The butt has two fake pockets and the front has two real pockets but they're small. I appreciate how these pants are actually high waisted. The material is stretchy, comfortable, and moves with my body.

Super soft, like pajama bottoms should be First off let me say that I am 5‘,4“ tall 167 pounds and my inseam is 32 inches. I am wearing an extra-large which I just washed to see if they would shrink. When I first got them they were much longer than what you see in the picture, the fit around the waist, however, is about the same. So expect them to shrink up I would say I lost about 2 inches on length which is fine because they were way too long. If I were to buy these again, I would probably try a large since these are super roomy I can adjust the drawstring but there’s still a little bit too big for my build I am however buying another pair because they are super soft. Just the way a pajama bottom should be. They are also very lightweight. So if you’re looking for something that’s going to keep you warm in the winter this isn’t gonna be it. But if you’re looking for an every day lounge pant, that’s lightweight and comfortable then stock up. These were crazy affordable and were delivered the next day. Highly recommend.

Color was off.... I've included a hand full of pictures. First off, I purchased the gray striped pants... they are definitely more like a purple color. If you like to wear high rise pants the length is good. If you want these to sit on your hips , then they are very long. Also the crotch sits very low,then. I personally wear my pant higher up. So the length is ok.and the crotch is where it should be. The quality of these ,is what you would expect for 13$. Nothing special. The material is soft, has a slight stretch and Is light weight, I did buy these with the idea of house/lounge pants.. maybe the floral ones would be better for out of the house. As in other reviews ,there is no stitching or metal rings around where the draw strings come out at the waist. I'm going to stitch this myself. Should only take a few minutes. If I dont do this , I am 100%sure the strings will come out in the laundry or they will get tangled up and rip the holes.I do think these pants run a bit bigger , I am 165lbs ,5'7" and I normally wear a size Medium to large.top. or , (if your familiar with AE size pants) wearing a 6/8 in AE pants.. I bought these in a size Medium theu fit ok. But have room.and if I were to buy another pair , I'd order a small.

Perfect for itchy growing belly None of my pants fits anymore and leggings are too uncomfortable these days I bought these on whim hoping to get some relief and comfort as I’m on my feet a lot and pregnant. Fantastic Pants!!! I LOVE these pants so much that I bought three different patterns. I wound up in the hospital from pneumonia and woke up (after ARDS and a coma) with Critical Care Myopathy, unable to move. Plus we were way out of town and I needed pants (and tees but that's another review) for physical therapy ASAP, so when I saw these and how lovely the prints were, we went out on a limb and ordered them. They arrived super fast (I was in the hospital for two months total, so a couple days was perfect), fit beautifully, and they gave me the boost I needed to get moving again. And oh, I got SO many compliments. Literally my new favorite pairs ever, glad I bought more than one pair. Just for size reference, I'm 5"5 and in the pictures I weigh around 110 give or take I think. I'm now around 115 and the medium still fits exactly like I wanted it to--loose and baggy, but not ridiculously so. I had to relearn how to walk again these, so when I tell you they're comfortable, I'm not kidding, lol!! Word to the wise: Put a knot at the end of each tie, or your drawstring will come out in the wash. Save yourself the headache and do it ahead of time.

Overall great, just a few minor complaints These came out great, they feel heavy duty and the definitely do the job, they are pretty hot to be in during hot weather but that's to be expected from pants like these, I took these out for a 2 day hike and they held up great, I felt like I could abuse these as much as I liked and they would still hold up. There are a few specific aspects of the pants that I will go over: 1. The knee pads: Trying to insert the knee pads will cause you to re-evaluate your entire life but once you understand the technique it's not a big deal, other than that they function great and really protect your knee when kneeling, only complaint is that they are very hard and inflexible so they can get uncomfortable when sitting. 2. The size: I measured my waist before ordering and ordered a 34" waist size, but when I tried the pants on, they were too loose on my waist even after tightening the waist adjustment mechanism, so I was forced to use a belt (which I was planning on using anyway so it wasn't that bad). 3. Overall comfort when walking: While walking in these pants after a while I noticed that the extra reinforcement material on the crotch area in the back of the pants would fold into itself and would bother me so I had to readjust it every once in a while, also the material of the pants is not very stretchy but I didn't find it bothering.

Love the pants, but apricot color is very unforgiving I ordered these joggers in the color "apricot" in an attempt to diversify my wardrobe,but I'm so sad I didn't go for one of the darker colors. In this light color, these joggers are pretty see-through, and seem to accentuate every nook, groove, or bump - making it so I don't think there is a pair of underwear that would work and it seems as though you can see my cellulite/all the bumps of my legs. Agree with all the tall girls . . LOVE them As another reviewer said, finally sweats that are long enough and don't come from the men's department. They are plenty long and don't have extra room for a package I don't have. I am 5'11", 170 lbs. with an athletic build. I got an XL, with the 34 in. inseam. I meant to order the 36 in. inseam as much of my height comes from my legs but as you can see these are plenty long. The pictures were taken after the sweats had been washed and dried. Although the tag says not to put in the dryer, I did and they came out fine. I tend to wear my pants down on my hips a little probably from a lifetime of not having pants otherwise long enough and you can see that the drawstring remains in the original position so they fit very comfortably around the waist. I didn't know what to expect with all of the reviews having contradictory information on sizing. They are super soft and kept me warm walking outdoors in freezing temps (that's not hyperbole the temperatures were below freezing and my legs didn't even get chilled after walking nearly a mile). My dog who oversaw the making of this review thinks they look really comfortable and will be great for many long walks.
color_display: baby polar fleece, dark heather grey

user_comment: SIZE UP I have such strong mixed feelings about these pants. I'm 5'8", about 150 pounds, pear shaped, and all legs. I wear size 6 pants and I'm almost always a very solid medium. I work at an outdoor school where the temperatures are in the 20-40s(F) for months. I don't need to win any fashion awards, my main goal is warmth and comfort. I LOVE that these pants come in multiple lengths because that's incredibly hard to find for sweatpants and my legs are long and I need my ankles covered because it's cold. I took the advice of everyone and sized up and bought a large in 34in the first time (black pants in pictures). The waistband, even sitting on my natural waist, is still incredibly tight. You can see in the pictures where it's making my tummy squish out the top. I love the way the rest of the pant fits and the length. I decided to order a second pair, this time an XL in the same 34in length (grey pants in pictures). This waistband fits a lot more comfortably and I can wear it lower on my hips which I find more comfortable, but they are so baggy everywhere else. They are also at least 2 inches longer than the black pair labeled the same length, which I find weird. Another thing I'm not a fan of is the line across the top of the butt. I'm not sure its purpose because it's thick, unflattering, and I can feel it through the pants if I'm not wearing a base layer. Despite the flaws, they are thick and warm so I'm keeping both.
color_display: baby polar fleece, light heather grey

But it's suitable for poolside, beach, outings, cruise that kind of situation or lounging at home. Material is fairly see-through and is like a stretchy rayon. The drape is lovely. You'll need to steam it when you purchase it to get the wrinkles out though.

Not flattering on certain body types but so cute! I got these for a costume party (beach resort theme) and they fit the bill. Comfy, flowy, but made me look bigger. I don't need help with that. LOL I'm size 14/16 XL/1X 5' 5.5" 205lb and suggest following the size chart. Pretty accurate. Pick the larger size if between sizes but not necessarily a size up product. They just grazed my ankles. Love the built-in-shorts so I never had to worry about a wardrobe malfunction.

Great material, stitching, fit and love the dual leg pockets! 💙 I purchased my regular size L. I’m 5’4” athletic build, 160 very muscular legs, these fit very comfortable, like a glove but not compressed. I treadmill, hike, do weights, push ups, etc. They stay put, minimal adjustments needed. I first used champion brand, but they stopped making my favorites so I took a chance on IUGA brand and was not disappointed. I like how flexible you can be wearing these pants, and they don’t cut into you. Another great feature are the dual leg pockets and the extra waist pocket. I can easily fit my iPhone X in one, my earbud holder and keys in the other. No pilling after daily use for several months. Stitching has not come apart yet either. Price is very good if you wait for the deals. Way less than I paid for other brands. I’ve purchased a couple capris and three pairs of IUGA bike shorts as well. Great product!⭐️

A pleasantly surprised tall girl. First things I must note: I'm 6' 2" with a 37" inseam, 29 1/2in waist, 42" hips and I ordered a dark gray medium. Not going to lie, I opened up the package and thought I might give these to my sister who's 5' 9" since I ordered the black ones as a gift for her, but I decided to try them on thinking, maybe they'll stretch or they'll just be nice cropped yoga pants. Lo and behold they did indeed stretch out! (Thank goodness.) Still a tad short but they turned out much longer than they initially appeared (holding them up they come to about my mid shin). They're also pretty high in the waist for pants not specifically designed for tall women. I did still have to hike them up a bit and, once I did, they stayed in place pretty well if I was just walking around but they definitely wiggled their way down as I did some light test stretching up and down (see progression of pictures). That might just be how yoga pants are but as someone who doesn't typically wear crop tops but is exploring the idea, this is something I pay attention to. Fabric-wise, they are super soft and feel like they're snug enough to give you that smooth, flattering, firm, shark skin feel. There is a seam at the very top around the waist that makes them feel like they're digging in a bit more there (see last picture) but it doesn't make them uncomfortable, just maybe slightly unflattering if they slide down and you don't have a completely fatless stomach like me. I also decided to try on the black ones (just in case, you know) and they fit very similarly but I'd say a touch longer in the legs and a touch looser in the top waist seam. The black fabric is also very soft but also slightly less velvety soft, but not by much. I am a very tactile person and I tend to buy/keep clothes purely on their softness so I notice these things. Overall, I am very pleased with these yoga pants and plan to purchase at the very least a pair in black for myself, if not other colors.

A must HAVE! Love the way the fabric feels!!!! I’m 5’8” XXL in between sizes. I sized up because wasn’t sure of the tightness but it fit well and high waisted! Hugs your hips and legs and will not need the Tug Up Game we all play with our sports bottoms. These pants I will not lie- I was skeptical of. I had three requirements for new yoga/workout leggings: compression, not too high of a waist, and pockets. The pair I ordered were only available in black in XS so I chose to take a chance and get them. Out of the four pairs I ordered from different vendors- these are the ones I will not be returning. Packaged well and in interior sealed bags in the package and with obvious iuga pride. These black compression leggings are opaque and panty peeking protective. Thick, soft, cottony and without the shiny workout legging look. Comfortable and warm enough to wear outside and for yoga inside as well as running errands. I am 5’4”, bw 105-110 pounds and I order pants that stop at the navel/ I see the new trends are waist and higher and this is the closest I have found with a rise that I can work with. Lunge proof, squat proof and truly stay in place for all yoga positions. No pilling or pulling of the material and they fit like a second glove. I was so impressed for the price, I went ahead and ordered a second pair in burgundy and hope to see the same quality arrive when I receive them. Thanks! Have since ordered a third pair in navy- I am loving this brand and their products! Thank you iuga!

Mid-Waist, Great Quality Yoga Pant As an 8 months PP, stay at home/work at home mama, I live in yoga pants. The proportions are not good These sweatpants are thick, warm, have pockets, and the elastic around the ankles which I prefer. My biggest issue with these are the rise; it is ridiculously high. I can’t even recommend them for someone tall because the rise would still be too higher, and the legs would be too short. If you like rolling the waistband on your sweatpants then you will like these. Personally, I don’t like rolling mine, and in order for these to fit decently, I need to roll the waistband down twice which then cuts off access to the pockets. I don’t know why I didn’t look harder for well fitting sweatpants. I spent over a 100 dollars on these and it’s too late to return them. I’m one of the photos I’ve attached, I folded the pants down from the crotch area to show that the rise of the pants is almost as long as the inseam which is comical and crazy. I ended up finding perfectly fitting sweatpants in the children’s section at Target for 9 dollars each on sale. For reference, I am 5’3, 110lbs and I bought smalls. At Target, the XLs (14) in the girls section fit me perfectly. They’re on the fitted side without being tight.

Thick and cozy! I got these pants from the IUUI store in size XX-Large, although I normally wear X-Large. The fit is great, and I am glad I sized up. Cute comfy warm sweats I love my nylon cargo pants & got these cargo sweats for the winter. I was worried ‘wide leg’ meant bell bottoms or flared but these are thankfully more straight legged bcz flare or wide leg makes me look fatter & shorter (5’3”/150-55) . Got the black in medium and it’s a comfy & roomy fit. They’re a little long for my height but they may shrink a little bit once washed. If not, then I can do a quick hem. I have to hem a lot of my pants so it’s no big deal.These have upper, flat angled pockets and lower cargo side pockets. There’s an elastic waist and a drawstring tie as well. They have a very roomy and comfortable fit & look a little dressier than plain sweats too.The outside fabric is smooth sweatshirt material and the inside fabric is very plush and fuzzy. Can’t wait for it to get cooler (can’t believe I’m saying that 🤦🏻‍♀️) so I can wear these out & about.

Great pair of pants from fall right into the deep of winter These are a rich cocoa brown, heavy-weight fleece lined... I hesitate to call them sweatpants -- maybe level-above-sweatpants. They're a little more crisp than ordinary sweatpants -- really nice. The waistband is wide and lays flat and the tie is on the inside, so there's no bulk or added visual detail at the waist. I'm 6 ft tall, 139 lbs, wearing a size Large and they are barely long enough for my taste. But these are really, really nice pants; they'd look amazing on someone of average height.

TO ALL MY TALLER GIRLS Me: 5’10ish 140 lb woman. I’m typically a small-4/6 34 inseam 27 pre pregnancy 33 postpartum I bought a small- before babies Bought medium- postpartum To all my tall girls that feel out of place… MY TALL GIRLS, THESE PANTS COULD BE FOR YOU! These pants can definitely be dressed up or dressed down for a more chic look. They are thin and perfect for spring/summer weather. The material isn’t high end material but it isn’t trash either. The stitching is nice through out and clean. Normal trousers sit lower low but these are true high waist pants. The bottom is cuffed up and its sewed to keep in place(see picture) If you carry your weight in your midsection I can see these highlighting that area for you. Consider wearing shape wear to smooth everything out. I noticed it was less forgiving on my postpartum lower belly but pre-pregnancy I did not have that problem. The inseam seems to be approx 32 but because it’s cuffed and only sewed on 2 opposing sides- it slightly drapes adding a some additional length. If you’re short, these will be way too long for you. Pros: THEY FIT TALLER WOMEN!!!!!! I put these on with a low block heel and also platform sneakers. Classy/chic look can be achieve getting the most usage out of styling these for different occasions. Iron/steam easily Cons: They aren’t wrinkle free and hold a bit of wrinkles if you stay in a position for way too long. It doesn’t wrinkle nearly as bad at linen. 👍

Plus Size > They're okay for around the house I'm being brave enough to post a picture with my shirt tucked in so you can really see the fit. First, the fabric is nice enough, comfortable and fairly soft, but it doesn't lend itself to giving you and shape, support or figure really. Second, l was hoping these wouldn't be too fitted through the knee as I have an issue with fat upper legs in form fitting pants. Not a good look on a plus size gal 😧 These have a bootleg type break from the knee down, which I like better than a tapered pant leg, but they just aren't super flattering above the knee. Third, the pants are a bit long and I'm 5"8. I'd be okay with this but if you're shorter than me you'll have to hem these! All in all, I'd keep these for around the house lounging or house cleaning, but I was looking for something I'd feel a little more comfortable wearing for a walk or running errands. These don't quite make the cut. But I DO appreciate any company that at least tries to give us plus size gals an option.

So cute and comfortable, sizing runs small in 100% cotton I love these pajama pants. They’re so comfortable. I have another pair from this same company that I have worn into the ground for the past year. I decided to buy a few new pairs from the same company in the same size I’ve been wearing. For reference I am 5’5” 118lbs with 26 inch waist and 38 inch hips and got the size small. I ordered 3 pairs of pajama pants from this company but for some reason these lemon print pants run smaller and is a different material. The lemon print pants are 100% cotton while the other 3 pairs I got are 98% polyester + 2 % spandex. The lemon print cotton pants are much lower rise. Because they are 100 % cotton I have no doubt they are going to shrink considerably. I would highly suggest if you get a cotton pair to size up. The weird thing is the listing didn’t say it was cotton. I thought I was getting the polyester ones which wash and dry great with no shrinkage. So be advised the lemon print pants are all cotton and you should size up. I hope that helps!

Thin and comfy. Cheap string material. I’m not gonna lie, you get what you pay for HOWEVER I still love these pj jogger pants. I’m 5’4, 120-125lbs and I normally wear a size small but thanks to other reviews saying it shrinks like a MFer, I bought a size XL and it was perfect. When I initially took it out of the bag, it was definitely a XL but after washing in cold and delicate, it shrank to a small (length on me was now perfect and waist area was loose but drawstring holds it up) AND the string is cheaply made so it got tangled/scrunched/undone ish…still usable, just doesn’t look cute. So I would say order 3 sizes up. Material is soft and thin which is perfect for spring, summer, and fall. Plaid is as depicted. I’ve only washed it a couple of times and I honestly don’t know how many more washes the string will last but since it’s so cheap ($11.99), I went ahead and bought 2 more of the same black plaid (backups just in case lol), 1 grey plaid, and I’m about to order the animal print :) Would recommend because it’s cute and inexpensive but you do get what you pay for.

soft and comfortable! But beware! They shrink! I'm 5 feet tall, so like most pants I bought these thinking I would have to hem them several inches to fit me correctly. They were pretty comfortable otherwise and actually covered my entire butt with no waistband gap. So into the wash they went before the hemming just in case they happen to shrink a bit. I'm so glad I washed them first! They shrunk an entire 3.5 inches and now they fit me just perfect without having to hem them at all! I liked them so much I bought 2 more in different colors. I have included a picture of the before washing (green) and after washing (pink) to show how much they shrink.

So, basically the seams in the front and back don't line up...but it doesn't affect the way it fits/feels, so I'm not really complaining. Just kind of a weird sewing job. 2. They're too long for me, I'm 5'1.5", and I step on them as I walk, so I'll probably end up cutting them. Not a big deal, since I'm wearing them to sleep in. Overall, I'm happy with this purchase and will probably order another pair.

I'm 5'6, 140lbs and ordered a large. They fit nicely, the legs are really long, they drag on the floor, but they're snug. These pants are great!! These pants are sooo cute!! I was a a bit nervous of the quality, if they’d look cheapy, but they don’t! They’re really really cute and fit nice! Waistband is fitted close to body (so cute with body suit) but not too tight giving muffin top. The fabric texture is like a biiiit of a swooshy type material, but not full on. And not really loud or weird sounding. Just a tiny, fine swoosh. Elastic tightener things at waist and ankles🤌🏽 they’ll be cuuute with flat sandals and cute sneakers. They don’t seem see through at all, but if one of you got the white, they might be. I ordered the small, black, and I’m 5’1, 105lbs for size perspective. The black has a pretty, very faint type of little sheen to the fabric, which I think adds to the look of it being more quality. Not a matte black. The material is a bit thicker but not super thick. I think they’ll be perfect for fall and spring when there’s a tiny bit of chill in the air with a body suit or a T-shirt. Winter might work too with a turtleneck and a cute jacket. Summer humidity and heat, you’d prob feel a bit sweaty. I really like these pants and I’m super happy I got them! I sent pics to my best gf group chat and a few of them already ordered their own☺️ If you’ve been thinking about bringing back the parachutes, I feel like these ones are the move👌🏽☺️

A cute style and the print with a “log cabin” kind of feeling to it. These pajama pants are soft and comfortable, but thinner fabric than I hoped for. I live above the snow line and winters can get chilly. It is not unusual for outside temperatures to remain below freezing all night. That said, after trying these on, I think that for a climate like mine, these pajama pants are best suited for spring and fall months - not for colder wintery weather or snowy nights. I do love the pockets and the waistband is comfortable, not too tight. These pajama pants are LONG. I will need to hem the length (I am 5’2”) a few inches.

They're okay, could be better I had high hopes for these sweatpants and they left me disappointed. They are advertised as a baggy fit however I am a true size Large and I sized up to the XL for an extra oversized fit but there really isn't much wiggle room. They are also not nearly as long as they appear in the pictures. I'm average height (5'5") and the cuff sits right at my ankle with almost no scrunching. The cuff is also very wide for some reason and almost falls over my heel. The color is good, just like the photo. The fabric is okay, heavier weight and soft, but doesn't feel as high quality as some of the better-known name brands in a similar price range, likely due to the high polyester content. Overall, I'd give 3.5 stars. They are wearable, but I wouldn't buy again unless they lowered the price. At almost $35 I had higher expectations.

Amazing quality BUT I’m 5’7 & sadly have gone from a healthy 157 to 130 due to being sick. I based buying these in a size Medium off other reviews. I’m happy with the size. I’ve had to buy basically all new stuff bc of the weight loss & it sucks, but these sweatpants definitely don’t suck at all. I have to keep my legs extra warm when it’s cold out bc of what I’ve got going on with my health; these sweats checked off all the boxes with keeping warm big time! I live in the North East & there’s never a consistent temperature, especially not in the Spring. It’s like 78 or more during the day & then low 40’s upper 30’s at night. It literally KILLS me with the super cold nights. The material is very thick, SUUUPER soft inside, I love that they don’t have a draw string & the length is perfect IMO. I read other reviews about them not being high waisted. Idk they seem normal to me, but I’m not entirely sure what to expect from high waisted sweatpants either 🤷🏼‍♀️ For these being a Medium & me being so frigging skinny now 😢 I was expecting to be swimming in them. I just ordered another pair today in a different color & almost went with a large because they don’t seem that baggy to me but whatever, I ordered another medium. The only problem I have with these pants are the stain on the left ass cheek. Like WTF is that about? They arrived 2 days ago & came out of the package like this. They definitely weren’t used by myself or anyone else, so maybe it was a defect Unsure what the stain is about, but I intend to exchange them for the same color & size. Arrived next day with prime shipping. Well packaged, no weird smell or anything; just the rando stain. If you’re looking to stay warm & comfortable + if you have curves, I think you’ll love these sweats. They’re definitely a high end quality.

I’d say they’re high-ish waisted I’m 5’4, 110lbs. I’d say these are high waisted. Or at the very least mid-waisted. Definitely not low. I haven’t noticed anything weird going on in the crotch area either, sits fine on me, though I do have to admit that the waist band is so bulky that it just does nothing flattering for your figure. So I’d pair it with a very tight shirt to create some kind of contrast

Comfy. These pants appear to be true to size. The fabric is soft, stretchy, and lightweight. The black color doesn’t stain light-colored panties and is not see-through when bent over unless stretched to its max. The high-waisted band covers the belly button but can be folded if preferred. My wife is 5’7”, about 190 lb, and wears XL in most clothes. These pants are very stretchy, and she probably would’ve been okay with size L, but she prefers to size up because L, in most cases, is tight on her waist, and she doesn’t like the feeling of discomfort around her belly. Also, she would rather hide lumps and bumps—her words, not mine. But I love all of it as it reminds me of what she went through to give me three beautiful children. We ordered XL, and the inseam is over 31’. My wife likes long pants that almost touch the ground with her shoes on. That said, it’s hard to get pants on a budget with the inseam 31-32 online. Most sellers don't post inseam details, and we end up having to buy and then return, which is inconvenient, to say the least. Just a word of advice. To ensure you get the right fit, Don’t go by the length of the pants; always go by your inseam.

SIZE DOWN-RUNS BIG I love everything about these pants. The color is a beautiful deep burgundy. Material is soft. It has 4 real pockets. Length is perfect for someone of my height. I ordered a 6. I am giving it a 4 star rating b/c through out the day, they did tend to ride up a bit & are starting to bunch at the top of my boots, but that could be b/c these are big on me, comfortable, but big. I would definitely recommend getting these if you are on the marked for comfortable, burgundy pants.

Good Quality White Stretch Jeans, But Run Large I'm 5'9", 120lbs, 27" waist, 34" chest, tiny butt, and non-binary (no hips). I really wanted these to fit me because I adore them. If you have some good hips and butt, I think these might run fairly true to size, but for someone with my figure, you'll definitely want to size down. They didn't show panty lines which is rare!

Really cute pants Absolutely love these pants! So comfortable, great quality and well made! Unfortunately I followed the size chart and ordered a size too big. To be fair I’m in between sizes and I ordered based on hips. The material is very stretchy so definitely do not size up. I should have ordered a size 10. I absolutely love the length and the cut! Highly recommend!

I'm 5'7 and weigh 120 lbs, but my hip width fits the size 6. The jeans are bigger than I'd like, looking a bit baggy when they should be a slim fit. Overall, they are a great causal jeans.

Great quality, stretchy, soft and comfortable. Let me first point out that these are good quality jeans. A little big around the waist, but I sized up because I’m in between sizes. Accurate to product photos. More for looks than comfort on me. I am 5’6” inches tall, 123 pounds on an empty stomach, and a consistent US size 4 in pants and jeans. I ordered size 4 in olive from Khaki and Blue and they do fit true to size. The waist is low-rise. The material is stiff and even makes some noise when I walk. No stretch whatsoever so size up if you are between sizes or don’t like things digging into you while seated. After sitting for long stretches, the lack of stretch really starts to dig in around the seated section of my lower torso and upper thighs. There were some steam marks upon arrival on the back of the pants but they came out in the wash. All 4 pockets are deep but the pants are a little too tight for me to even be able to use them for much. I’m afraid the size 6 would be too loose in the waist but probably would’ve been less intrusive for my desk job. However, I do like the style and look of these pants with the flaring leg and hip hugging. In conclusion, the stiff/thick material with no stretch will keep this olive pair out my regular line-up but the design and style will draw me, when I’m feeling peppy and can stand up a good portion of the day. Definitely a high quality pair of pants!

Awesome, awesome, awesome pants These KICZOY Corduroy Loose Mid Rise pants are beautiful and amazing looking on. My pup was cozy on my bed so he's included in my photos. For starters though, I have to address the one problem. There is stretch EVERYWHERE in these pants except the waist. The waist feels binding because it doesn't move and stretch like the rest of the pant fabric. IF, they had put some stretch in the waist, part elastic, all elastic, or a band that stretches these pants would have been 10+++++ star quality. The pink is a soft mauve and it is BEAUTIFUL. Not too light, not too dark. The corduroy is small pin corduroy and it stretches amazingly well. Stretches in every direction. The fabric is light weight, flows beautifully and feels extremely comfortable. The legs are straight but FLOW into a bottom flare. It looks gorgeous. Pockets are ample plus two back pockets. I LOVE these pants, they fit me extremely well EXCEPT for the tight, stiff binding waist. If I'd lose 5-10 lbs they'd fit my waistline. I can wear them now but they are tight. I'm 5'4" 154 lbs.

Good color & quality but the fit is challenging I really liked the color and material on this pair of pants and I wanted to love them. In terms of fit though they are pretty boxy and unattractive on me (I'm 5'3" and 125lbs). The waistband was a little tight and the wide legs made it look more like pajamas then pants.

Great color. Soft and cute. I was looking for a pair of brown corduroy pants and found these. They came quickly and were packaged neatly. They're actually a knit that looks like corduroy, but is a lot softer and a little stretchy. They fit as expected: snug at the waist and flairing larger pantlegs. They look great on and I got a lot of compliments when I wore them to work. I'm taller (5'9") and usually feel uncomfortable in pants that are a little shorter, but with my ankle boots, they looked cute and fashionable.
So cute! Love these pants! I am 5 feet tall and these are the perfect length for me. The material is nice quality and super comfortable. They fit true to size. The material is lightweight.

Comfy and long enough for tall girls Love that the fabric is light and very comfortable. Being 5’11 it’s challenging to find pants long enough but these are perfect (haven’t washed them yet though). For reference I ordered a medium and I’m 155 lbs.

Feel and look great. I love these pants. They’re both casual and dressy, whatever you want they look fabulous with flat flats and great with 2 to 3 inch heels. I’m 110 pounds and 5 foot tall and these are a small short and fit perfectly washable and rival I have two and I’ve just purchased another. These will be the staple of my summer wardrobe.

Buy these pants! So darn comfortable, and they launder PERFECTLY. They’ll come wrinkled in the package, but don’t let that dissuade you. After they’re washed/dried, mine don’t need ironing. I’m just under 5’4” and the length is perfect for me to wear with flats or up to 2” heel.

Very thin, poor fabric... but comfortable and pretty cute anyway I'm 5'10, a size 10, and sized up to an XL Long to be safe. They're still on the smaller side. The long length is definitely not long-- they're too short for me and haven't even been washed yet. My biggest problems are with the material. Because the fabric is pretty clingy, the pants don't want to hang as wide as the product images, but these pants are still pretty cute. It's neither as stretchy nor as nicely draping as you would expect. It's wrinkly in a way that doesn't look fully intentional. And it's so, so thin. You can easily see both of the front pockets through the pants even though the thighs aren't fitted. This means that even 'seamless' underwear has a high chance of visibility. I would not pay $36 for pants made with this material.

QUITE happy with these pants! These pants are very stylish, comfortable, and fun! The only potential qualm I’ll throw out is they do seem prone to wrinkling; they came out of the bag with deep creases but I’m hopeful after drying them and maybe a light ironing will get those out. I actually really like the material, it’s a little thicker than what I’d expected but it prevents them from being see through. The material is also lightweight despite being a bit thicker, and it flows well. They are the perfect length for me, landing just above the ground with no shoes on. The waist part also feels great, and I’m 5’3” and 130 lbs. I got the medium/short size. I think they pair very well with heels and I’m excited to start wearing these regularly because they’re so comfy and functional! Will also mention they do have good sized pockets. I’m planning to pair with a black t shirt, black heels (see pic), black purse, and some gold jewelry. :)

I will try to figure something out with these pants, maybe wear some sort of tights underneath so it is not as noticeable. I really wanted this cream color and I’m sure that if it was a darker color, I would have not had this issue of being see-through.. I would recommend and would repurchase these pants in a darker color for sure!!! Please heart if you have found my review being at all !!! 🤍

I love these pants! These are so comfortable and super versatile. I got these in a size small/short and they fit me perfectly! For reference I am 5 ft and weigh 115. I was able to wear these with flats, which was amazing, most of the time I can't wear any pants with flats because my poor little legs are so short. I felt elegant and comfortable, which is honestly the best combo!

Want in every color! These are the thicker fabric ones which are perfect for the colder weather but not too bad for summer. It's always cold in the office anyways! For reference I am 5'5 120 lbs and got a small. The waist is pretty stretchy and forgiving. Ive been looking for ampair of trousers and these are by far the most flattering. I got a different pair from this brand and they fit great as well. Highly recommend these!

Very dressy and classy, while still wide and comfortable. Very nice looking pants, very dressy and professional. I ordered the beige. The word says beige, but the picture looks more like a very light sand, almost white. They are as pictured. I was actually hoping for something a little more beige because I’ve never had much luck with virtually white pants. They all seem to stain so easy. The first time I wear them they come home with an ink spot, coffee stain, or something. These did not! I was very impressed. I wore them to the office on a regular workday. I took the train into the city. Wore the pants all day. When I came home, they looked great. A few wrinkles around the hip flexors, which is normal for an office job. I was eating a breakfast bar at my desk and dropped a few chocolate chips on the pants so I went into the bathroom and just wiped them off with a wet paper towel and the chocolate came right off. It’s great to be able to have such a light colored pair of pants that don’t stain easy. And they’re so easy to care for. They were a little creased out of the package, as you can see by some of the photos. I tried them on right away. I was wearing a black and purple knee brace and I just happened to have on black underwear. Could not see through them at all, which can be a problem with such light colored pants quite often, and they fit perfect. I touched them up with a steam iron and it came out fine. Care instructions. The tag inside the pants list just the care instructions symbols, and I didn’t know what they all meant, but there is a card attached to the pants that translates them. You might want to save the little card, that’s what I did. Care instructions for these pants translate to: Do not bleach, dry clean, Machine wash up to 86°F general cycle. iron up to 230°F tumble dry low. So you can dry clean them and they’re very nice pants. They’re worth it if you want to. But it also says machine wash up to 68°F gentle cycle, tumble dry low, iron up to 230°F. So I threw them in the washer by themselves on delicate, and then threw them in the dryer on low. And I am very impressed. They’re clean and they came out of the dryer virtually wrinkle free. So I’m not gonna bother to test iron them, they don’t need it. Very easy to care for. Sized as stated. I measured myself and bought the one according to the measurement chart, and it fit perfectly. May be too short for average to tall people. The one thing the measurement chart does not say is the inseam. This concerned me because I’m 5’4”” with a 28 inch inseam, and very long waisted. These pants fit me perfectly, I can wear them with flats. They come up to my waist. I measured the extra large inseam is 29 inches so it’s perfect for me. However, as my sister says, for anyone who actually has legs, lol, they may end up, looking like high water pants. But from my short legs, they’re perfect. I’m thrilled with the inseam.

Could not see through them at all, which can be a problem with such light colored pants quite often, and they fit perfect. I touched them up with a steam iron and it came out fine. Care instructions. The tag inside the pants list just the care instructions symbols, and I didn’t know what they all meant, but there is a card attached to the pants that translates them. You might want to save the little card, that’s what I did. Care instructions for these pants translate to: Do not bleach, dry clean, Machine wash up to 86°F general cycle. iron up to 230°F tumble dry low. So you can dry clean them and they’re very nice pants. They’re worth it if you want to. But it also says machine wash up to 68°F gentle cycle, tumble dry low, iron up to 230°F. So I threw them in the washer by themselves on delicate, and then threw them in the dryer on low. And I am very impressed. They’re clean and they came out of the dryer virtually wrinkle free. So I’m not gonna bother to test iron them, they don’t need it. Very easy to care for. Sized as stated. I measured myself and bought the one according to the measurement chart, and it fit perfectly. May be too short for average to tall people. The one thing the measurement chart does not say is the inseam. This concerned me because I’m 5’4”” with a 28 inch inseam, and very long waisted. These pants fit me perfectly, I can wear them with flats. They come up to my waist. I measured the extra large inseam is 29 inches so it’s perfect for me. However, as my sister says, for anyone who actually has legs, lol, they may end up, looking like high water pants. But from my short legs, they’re perfect. I’m thrilled with the inseam.

Perfect fit, so comfortable! I'm so glad I found these pants! I'm also glad I packed them for my California trip. They were the perfect blend of business casual, comfortable, and summer vibes! The elastic waistband is a life-saver. The material is soft and light, and super easy to iron, no hassle. I am about 5'7, weigh 165lbs, and I ordered a size large. Perfect fit!

Short and curvy approved! Here are the details: The questions we all have were fully checked off. I’m 5’2, 194 pounds, and built like a box. Seriously, you could pack me in the same rectangle box these pants arrived in. I ordered the lighter beige (think French vanilla) and the darker khaki (not a light tan but a true khaki color). I was going for the quiet luxury or “old money” aesthetic that is timeless and put together. So, how do these pants fair against the luxurious box that is me? Pros: Tummy: I have one. Do these fit mom bellies? Yes. What about the pooch effect? A little. When I sit down there is a little pooching from the fabric but if I add a blazer or sweater then it’s hardly noticeable. There is zero, and I do mean zero, stretch in the waistband so if you’re between sizes I would go up a size. I am a new size 16, from an 18, and it took me a full minute to be okay with a fitted waist since most of my pants have elastic waists. Buttons and belts: The buttons (there are 2) were sewn on well. The zipper didn’t catch and after two flights of stairs to test out whether these pants could handle the demands of being part of my closet the zipper stayed put. Belt loops are wider so while a thin belt would still look cute I would go with a standard thickness in a belt to get that old money look. Length: I could walk barefoot in them as they’re wide legs and while maybe an inch too long they’ll do nicely with heels added to them. I walked outside with a pair of booties on and they did not drag the ground. The hem on the bottom of the pant legs is nicely constructed and gives a tailored look. Thighs: Did not make me look like a can of biscuits about to make an escape. They draped nicely over my luxury edition thighs and didn’t seem to hurt my feelings. Cons: There really aren’t any that would stop me from ordering more of these. They probably require hand washing, gentle detergent, or Woolite. (Photo attached for instructions). Can you see through them? I couldn’t. I could see the line but with shape wear that would solve the issue. Bumps and lumpy bits showing? A little but this is where shape wear comes in. All in all, these are high end quality with an everybody price tag. They’re pants with substance meaning they are true corduroy trousers with a little weight to them. They’ll likely do fine through autumn but I will add a second pair a size up so that I can wear footless tights under for an extra layer of warmth during winter. 10/10 do recommend!

I am tall so that probably won't be an issue for most. The waist is a medium rise and they have a functional drawstring and pockets. The material is soft and it's fuzzy on the inside. It's the perfect weight, not too thin or too thick.

So comfortable and soft! Love the pockets! KUT/SO Women's Joggers with Pockets I got these and the matching crew neck sweatshirt. They are more of an Ivory or Winter White than a true white. They have a warm undertone. Fit perfectly and true to size. Leg length is good and I'm 5'7". Love the pockets! Waist band has three layers of elastic and a working drawstring with capped ends. Midweight flecce which is warm but not so warm they're too hot indoors. 60% cotton/40% polyester brushed fleece. Machine wash cool, line dry.
