Size ordered: Large (then exchanged for Medium) Height 5'5" Waist 34" NOTE: "Red Tie dye" pattern is not as described; mine were bright pink, all over the pants, and nothing like the picture. NOTE: The black pair's dye bleeds in the wash. Would not recommend washing with light colors 👍 Updated review: I've exchanged size Large for size Medium, as I found there to be a bit too much excess material throughout the legs. I am happy with the medium fit. The wasteband difference between the Large and Medium is minimal, and the primary difference is just a bit more snug throughout the legs (which I am very pleased with, as I love the non-restrictive wasteband) I've been searching for a flattering jogger style lounge pant, that has a similar silhouette to a skinny jean with comfy slight bagginess through crotch area, for what feels like forever. I love these so far. So soft and stretchy (think breathable thin spandex material) lounge style, but I don't find them to look frumpy, and they still have a distinctive jogger shape. The wasteband is surprisingly comfortable and non-restrictive, especially for a pair of pants with fitted legs. The pockets are too small for my liking, unfortunately, and my smartphone has to be angled and pushed in strategically otherwise it will fall out. Overall, happy with my purchase and have ordered more.

Purchased both medium and large sizes These joggers are great! I consider them to be one of those “Amazon Gems”. They are extremely soft, very fitted (almost like leggings), they have pockets which I love, and they have a drawstring with a very expandable waistline. I love that the waistline is nice and big w/plenty of room for my “mom belly”. They are super comfy and a great value, I only paid $12.99. I purchased both the medium and large in black because I wanted to see the difference. I am 5’6”and weigh 145 pounds, so I go back and forth between med and large sizes. There was barely any difference between the medium and large size. The only difference I noticed was the waist band on the large was slightly bigger than the medium. Other than that, there was really no difference. The length and width of the pants are pretty much the same size. I thought maybe the large would be a little bigger in the leg area and maybe slightly longer, but they weren’t. That’s not a problem for me, I can wear both sizes, but I figured I would mention it in case it could help somebody. Pictures: Size medium- The 3 pictures that I’m wearing a camo shirt and house slippers are the size medium joggers. Size large- The 2 pictures that I’m wearing a grey shirt and flip flops are the size large joggers

Perfect! First off, I'm a guy. I have been looking for joggers that fit like skinny jeans for a minute now and found these, ordered a black pair in medium. They fit perfectly!! Couldn't be happier. They do hug your legs so if that's not your thing you might want to look elsewhere for a looser fit, and I can see how some shorter folks may not appreciate the length of these, but I'm 5'8" and they fit like a dream. They are soft and don't lose shape like my old gilligan & o'malleys joggers, those would stretch after one wear especially around the knees. They did have an intense odor to them when I first opened as other reviewers have mentioned but you should be washing them before wearing besides a test fit anyway. I didn't know if I wanted a large or medium as I heard they run small but I went for medium in black and they fit just fine since the waist is properly elastic. The pockets are fine as well, not too small so I really have no complaints so far. Time will tell on the longevity of these but for the price they can't be beat! They are warm but breathable so perfect for any environment imo. I also read that the color can bleed but these haven't caused me trouble yet. I dont know if different colors fit different or cause those colors to bleed but I only wear black and these are fine. Absolutely plan on buying a few more of these black pairs! Love them!!!

COMFY SO SOFT. SO COZY. I ordered the medium over the recommended small after reading reviews and am glad I did. The fit is between joggers and leggings, which is what I wanted. I got these as an alternative to scrub pants for work. The pockets are not substantial but will be suitable for what I need. I immediately ordered another pair for lounging at home. They hit just above my ankle as long as I wear them at my hips and stretch them down. At belly button and slouchy, they're capri length. 34" inseam for reference. Do not recommend wearing at or above belly button, by the way. The reviews ft. wrinkly camel toes are REAL. PS cat (his name is Hades!) not included. He's just clingy.

VERY NICE! Not sure if these LALAGEN Womens Plus Size Fleece Lined Leggings are quite leggings or more like snow pants? Which ever they are they are extremely well made and fit amazingly well. I'm not plus sized, but I can sometimes wear plus sizes. It gives my very not flat belly more room. I ordered 2XL and I'm usually a Misses 14 size or XL. The 2XL fits me well, no bagginess, no pouches of extra fabric. The length fits my 5'4" frame well. These are a cross between snow pants and leggings. Outter fabric is smooth, soft, plush, luxurious feeling. Inner fleece is extremely soft and look at the way the lining is sewn in! That is quality! Outter fabric sheds water, and overall these pants feel very cozy and warm but are extremely light weight. Outter zippered pocket on the back waist. Yoga style waist fits smoothly and comfortably around my waist. Feels SO soft! No side or back pockets just the zippered waist pocket. Legs are straight and tapered, no bottom cuff. These pants/ leggings are far better made than their price reflects. Want to get more of these in the other colors. The navy is a beautiful dark navy. 100% recommend.

warm and soft with weird pocket These fit pretty good, they're true to size and are comfortable, I'm only 5 feet tall so I expect to have to work around length and these aren't super baggy. The only weird part in the back pocket, rather than pockets on the sides of the legs like most leggings these have one on the back with a zipper to keep things secure in side. I might find that I love it and that all pockets are inferior, however at the beginning stages of my introductions to this new pocket placement I am skeptical and hesitant to open myself up to that potential love. They are soft and warm, the fabric isn't so thick that they're hard to put on, but they're soft and fuzzy inside and will work great on cool days.

Comfortable, practical put together These have many color options, I have about 7 pairs. These are a nice thick material and have pockets that are big enough to hold my phone. They are thick enough to be comfortable in the winter time but not too much that I'm uncomfortably hot while running around the house cleaning. I got a bit concerned about wearing sweatpants all the time. I'm a stay at home mom and I want to be comfortable but I also don't want my husband looking over from his office thinking I've given up and am not trying anymore. These are easy to match with a t-shirt, sweater or something a little dresser and I feel put together. It's easy to run out of the house and do quick errands, go to an appointment or the grocery store or drop something off at the school for the kiddos. I wouldn't wear these to go out with friends or a date night but they are suitable for more relaxed office wear and I love them for traveling. These pants are supper stretchy but are tight enough so they do not slide down through out the day. I am 5'6" and depending on if I'm working out or not I am between 122-130 pounds. It's great that these fit me well no matter how I'm doing on my eating and exercise. I got a small tall because after these are washed and dried they do shrink up a bit so you can't really judge the fit until that first wash is done. Hope this helps!

Not for Petite Frames 5’3, apx 110lbs, 28” waist, 36” hips. Could not find a size chart so I ordered M based off a similar pair of pants I had. I have been hunting for a nicer looking pair of lounge pants for a few years since the tie on my favorite Old Navy ones broke. Unfortunately they don’t sell them- or anything similar- on their website and I cannot access a physical store to check. Based on the photos these seemed like a good replacement; slimmer fit, pockets, drawstring(more polished than elastic) and slight contrasting stitching for interest. While the pants are very soft they were not as expected. The contrasting stitching was absent and while they stayed on my hips without being tied, the pants were huge. I measured and the rise was about 12”, where my Old Navy ones are 9”. I decided not to try sizing down though since they were not as nice as my Old Navy ones. Those I could pair with a cute tee and babysit, go to the mall, or a casual restaurant in. These I wouldn't answer the door in. Honestly I don’t think they would have fit well sizing down either as they just seem to be made for a curvier person. 3/5 Stars: They are soft and I like the drawstring and pockets. I can’t really criticize the sizing since there isn’t a size chart listed. But I think the photos are a bit deceiving in terms of fit and stitching color.

I measured and the rise was about 12”, where my Old Navy ones are 9”. I decided not to try sizing down though since they were not as nice as my Old Navy ones. Those I could pair with a cute tee and babysit, go to the mall, or a casual restaurant in. These I wouldn't answer the door in. Honestly I don’t think they would have fit well sizing down either as they just seem to be made for a curvier person. 3/5 Stars: They are soft and I like the drawstring and pockets. I can’t really criticize the sizing since there isn’t a size chart listed. But I think the photos are a bit deceiving in terms of fit and stitching color.

Looks awesome, but not well made So there’s good and bad. They fit as expected, which was surprising. I’m 5’8” and around 175-180#. A large fit me nicely. XL was too loose in all the wrong areas. Sexy/cool factor- Definitely 10/10 Stretch- 8/10 Comfortable- 8/10 - not a 10, just bc they may be less comfortable on a hot day. As they are pretty “sticky” when on. Material and stitching- 3/10 The material does not look or feel like it could hold up to much. The stitching is coming apart before even wearing them. The seams are really weak in places and coming apart. Overall, I was really disappointed in this as I intended to wear them for a date night. They may serve as a costume or one time wear. But not a good product for the money. If they were cheaper, like half the cost, I probably wouldn’t be returning them.
Great pants! I bought these & first got a pair of plain pleather pants… RETURNED. Then re-ordered & got what ordered. Hot hot hot!! Love! Got large for 5’6” at 155 lbs. hope this helps!
Loved these ..fit was perfect I got a medium. I'm 5'3 138lbs . Very comfortable and also lined inside so it's not sticky or hot .

Sizing is off The quality of these pants were surprisingly good. But oh, the sizing—I’m 135 lbs and 5’11", usually a size 6 or 28 in jeans, and I decided on a medium, expecting some extra room for potential shrinking. Little did I know, I'd end up with a comedy of proportions: the waist was like a generous invitation, while the fit around my butt and thighs decided to put on a tightrope act. The length? A creative take on "keeping it high and dry!" They claimed a 41-inch inseam, but apparently, part of that took a detour above my knees, leaving me perfectly prepped for a stylish flood-ready look. On the bright side, if you’re tall or looking for capris these pants will have you poised to confidently conquer both fashion and unexpected puddles!

I actually ordered these for my daughter who is typically a small but I sized up to a medium bc she wanted the oversized wide leg fit in the model pics. I’m typically a medium so I used my experience for the review. The faux leather material is very soft & has a ton of stretch to it. It’s not all that thick on it’s on so it’s lined w a thin fleece that feels great against the skin & adds warmth. The fact that the material is thinner helps these drape properly though. When I first put these on I was shocked by how high waisted these actually are. I have a short torso so the super high waist creates a look in the front created by too much fabric. It looks like it’s pulling bc they’re tight, but they’re not. There’s just too much fabric for my waist. The hips & booty fit very nicely though. The pants hug my booty & then drape down in a straight leg style. They look cute on & the cut of the legs is great however the waist is definitely smaller than the size chart has listed. The waist of these pants looks completely different on the outside & inside. On the outside it looks like your average button w zip fly made from the same material as the rest of the pants. On the inside something totally different is going on. There’s a thick elastic band sewn to the waistband on the inside & it definitely doesn’t have the same stretch as the rest of the pants bc it feels tight on me & according to the measurements it shouldn’t. The size chart measurements show the medium waist to be 15.8” halved or 31.6” all the way around. I have a 29.5” waist on a good day & a 30” waist on a bloated day so the waistband should fit w room to spare opposed to feeling tight. Thankfully the smaller waistband will help these fit my daughter properly, but definitely something to be wary of when choosing a size. The length of these is also ridiculous. I’m short so most pants are long on me, but these take it to a whole new level. They’re not only too long when I’m on my toes they’re too long in over 4” stilettos. Thankfully my daughter likes to cuff pants that are too long & if that doesn’t work out I’ll hem them. Other than the waistband, the pants seem to be well made, but I fell the $40 price tag is a bit high. Definitely better to buy when on sale for $30 like now.

Sizing inconsistent! I purchased these in Ellis, Black, and Seattle, all size 6 Medium. First off, they're not very high rise- more like a mid-rise. My Levi's all have a much higher rise, so if you're looking for a true high rise, look elsewhere. Secondly, you're going to want to order a few different sizes bc they're pretty inconsistent. The Black pair were so tight I could barely get them over my hips! The Seattle were slightly looser, but still tight, and Ellis was just right! All were a tad long (I'm 5'6 145lbs). Going to return for Ellis size 6S and Seattle Size 8 Short. The Black pair had a cheaper fabric quality, more like a jegging but the Seattle and Ellis washes are ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ quality. Will definitely try those two washes in a Short!

Cute jeans I ordered these as a loose-fitting pair of jeans so I could wear to work comfortably. They fit the bill. They’re nice and loose in the thighs. The waist is a bit too big, but I usually wear a 0-2 so I expected this, I just need to wear a belt. Otherwise these are very comfy and I like the fit. They’re definitely a denim material, not a jegging- which I like. The rise comes to a little under my navel. They hold their fit throughout the day and don’t loosen up. I’m only 5’3 and they’re still a little long, even with the short length but not too bad. Overall, great for the price.

So comfy I love these pants. Waist was exactly perfect and can’t beat the elastic waist. Length was long (but I am short, so that’s typical). Fabric was very soft and comfortable. I am glad I ordered two. For reference, I wear a 14 and am 5 ft even.

So comfy I love these pants. Waist was exactly perfect and can’t beat the elastic waist. Length was long (but I am short, so that’s typical). Fabric was very soft and comfortable. I am glad I ordered two. For reference, I wear a 14 and am 5 ft even.

Great for curvy pear/hourglass shaped people! Let me start this by saying I wasn't expecting to fit in these as well as I do. I bought these knowing I could pass them down to my sister if I didn't fit in them as I wanted to. Jeans are hard for me to find that fit well, and I usually opt for trousers or leggings instead. Day 1 of their arrival I wore them to work (see picture) and I had no issues with them. They proved to withstand all the bending over, on my knees and fast movement through an 8 hour shift. They didn't make me uncomfortable, they didn't cause any chafing, or didn't unzip and expose my underwear like I've also experienced with jeans. I got a few compliments from coworkers and customers on them, as they usually dont see me in jeans. The flare on them is adorable, however I will say as im 5'3, I did have to roll them so they didn't drag while at work. So shorties, be aware of the flare drag! Color is beautiful I love the wash. I didn't notice any weird smell from them. And they're very thick, as I stated before I'm constantly on my knees at work and don't feel much stretch from them. I attribute that to their mostly cotton makeup, it's not cheap polyester that easily tears under pressure.

Awesome 1970’s Retro look Returned the bright blue day color, that cost 3 times as much but kept the elevated retro color that was a steal at $16.00 and took a chance on boiling them for 30 minutes on stove top and drying on sanitize cycle for maximum shrinkage. See photo laying on top of retuned pair, the length shrunk almost two inches. Still need to wear with heals, so not for everyday at almost 70 years old, but they make me feel young again. I am 5’3” tall with longer legs and shorter torso. I bought the size 12 short which did shrink all over so if you are big from the knees up these may not be for you. They were scary long looking out of the package but 99% cotton does shrink nicely. If I manage to shrink them a little more they could rate 6 stars!

Check the length before ordering I absolutely love these jeans. I have missed bell bottom jeans and these brought back a lot of memories. The only thing is… make sure to check out the length in sizing. I usually wear a regular length but if you look at the length for regular in these jeans it says 33. Longs are 35 and shorts are 31. I should have looked at that more closely and ordered short. That’s my error. The regular were a bit long on me. Next time I will order the short. Lastly, I apologize for the smudgy mirror pics. My littles love to play with that mirror and it always gets smudgy.

Good Flares I was drawn to these jeans because it said they were mid-rise (and on sale). I can’t do high waisted jeans and I have struggled to find mid-rise flares at a reasonable price. After reading the reviews I wasn’t sure if I would like them. Many sizes were sold out because they were on sale. I ordered an 8 regular in a blue wash and an 8 short in a grey wash. As you can see from the pictures, the regular comes up to my belly button. I’m only 5’ 3.5” and weigh about 135 but still. I was disappointed. They were definitely long, but I thought maybe with a heel I could get away with it. Then I tried on the short. They didn’t come up as high (although still a little higher than I wished). And the length was perfect. Some people said their size was a little loose. These are definitely not loose on me. I’m actually hoping they might loosen up a bit after I wear them. I’m returning the 8 regular and plan to keep the 8 short. I haven’t worn them other than to try on so I can’t yet speak for how they will hold their shape after all day. They do have some stretch to them; enough to make them conformable and not stiff.

Good material, odd fit for me. These jeans are quality denim and a great length for taller people. I'm 5'8", 175lbs, and I ordered the "Blue Veil Dx" color option in size 10L. The hem hits the floor and since I usually don't wear flat shoes, this isn't a problem. The price is unbeatable. I haven't owned a pair of Lee jeans in many years. Unfortunately, now I remember why: The fly area always seems to be crooked or slightly off center on Lee jeans. Every pair I've ever tried over the years has had this problem. After wearing for a while, walking around, etc, the fly pulls more to one side and looks crooked. This isn't the case on any other brand of jeans I've tried, so it must be some flaw with this brand. It's weird. But hey, they're nice jeans otherwise, and many people seem to like them. This brand just isn't for me.

Great jeans I was skeptical about buying such inexpensive pants. But I have worn Lee brand before and knew my size, so I took a chance. So glad I did! I ordered size 8 regular. I have a 34” inseam, being 5’8” and most of it legs! These jeans have a 33” inseam. Just long enough. The long size is a 35” inseam, which would be dragging on the floor for me. The fit is great for someone with slim hips and a small butt. Curvy gals might think the legs were too tight, but they work for me. Material is soft with just the right amount of stretch. Highly recommend!

Run small, off-center: Factory defect? These run snug. I got the biggest size, 18, in both the dark wash and lighter shade and I’m trying to decide if they’ll break in enough to be comfortable. The first thing I noticed was that the zipper is off center on BOTH pairs. My guess is this is a factory defect and that’s why I was able to snag them for $16. It seems they cut one leg a little smaller because it definitely pulls to the left when you zip it. I like that they’re long enough for me and don’t ride up in length from my big butt or pull down in back at the waist, as I’m 5’11” and 220 lbs. They definitely don’t do my perimenopausal midsection any favors, but that’s just how I’m shaped now so I guess I have to deal with it until I make more effort to change. They were a little high in the crotch when I first put them on and I was afraid I’d have a serious camel toe. But they’re starting to loosen up the longer I wear them, so that isn’t looking like an issue anymore. Overall, I’d say they’re better quality than most jeans I’ve tried from Amazon but definitely not the unicorn I’m looking for in a pair of jeans. If you buy them, wear them around for a while with the tags on to see how they feel after they break in some. That will give you a better idea of the fit. I just hope they don’t shrink in the wash because then they probably will be too small.

They’re not too long I love the high rise. get sick of having to pull my jeans up and these are comfortable. I ordered the size 10 in the retro color. I’m 5’4”, 150 lbs, and 30 inch waist. I ordered the short length based on reviews saying they’re too long. I always wear at least a 2-3 inch boot or heel with jeans so I should’ve known better than to order a short. They don’t cover the top of my 2&1/2” wedge sandal in the photo. So I’ll return for a regular length. I usually like my length to be 32”. I see the reg. is 33” for this jean. That’s ok, it’s flare, it’s not supposed to look like a high water imo. Should’ve just ordered a regular instead of short, but so many reviews said they run long. I was convinced they did. They do not. Even if I wore with flats I would maybe want at least another inch. Good value for the money.

OMG...now my favorite jeans! In case you are wondering. I'm 5'9" and about 152 Lbs. I ordered an 8 Long and I could not be any more satisfied with my purchase. The price point was great and the fit is like a glove, but in a comfortable way. I feel like they were tailored specifically for my shape. The stretch is just right and compliments my figure, at least in my opinion...lol. Now that I know which size jeans are the most flattering for me, I'm looking forward to ordering more in different colors. I also want to add that there were no design flaws in the material. I absolutely love the boot cut. Although I was born in Afro-Caribbean, Belize Central America and raised in Cali since 2yrs old. There is some part of me that is a Southern Belle at heart. Now that I'm in Georgia and appreciate some elements of the south, I can't wait to pair these jeans with my cowgirl boots.

Actually fits properly! I'm 5'1, 111lbs and finding pants that fit me boarders on impossible. Anything that fits my waist is way too long. I'm not spending $50-$75 on good quality jeans, just to go spend even MORE to get them tailored to actually fit. I figured try out Prime before you Buy and these won no contest. I tried Wrangler, Lee and Amazon Essentials brands; ranging $24 to $70. I couldn't believe these fit so well and only cost $24 (there was a sale at time of purchase). I've worn them with work boots and also boots with a heel. Both look great! There's no gap in the back, they're the perfect length, the fabric is great quality and they're comfy! My husband also agrees they look fantastic. I've already purchased two more pairs. Just for context, I also tried the 4 short and 4 regular, since it recommended that's the size I should purchase based on reviews. Like a lot of womens pants, I assumed they would run large but, size 4 is huge. So, if you usually find yourself purchasing size 4 pants/shorts/skirts, I'd go with size 2.

Excellent Pants, but be aware of possible defects! Let me start first by giving measurements for reference: 5'3" , 137lbs , 29" waist, 38" hips, 27" inseam That being said, I have tried all 6/8 short/petite relaxed/curvy pants on Amazon (Rafaella Gaberdine curvy, Signature Levi curvy Straight, Gloria Vanderbilt Amanda & GV Curvy Skinny, Dickies Curvy Straight, Lee Relaxed Side Elastic Straight, etc) and ALL of them either had a gap in the waist, were too tight across the rear, or were simply just too long.. however THESE pants fit great and had neither issue! I finally found a pants that not only fit, but are modest for work-- flattering but not figure hugging. I bought these in size 6 petite in colors (pictured from left to right) Indigo Wash (like a blue jean color/type fabric) Black Imperial Blue Mini Glenplaid (Black on light grey- NO BLUE in this, which i actually prefer) Cypress (Dark Olive Green) Jet Black/White Rockhill Plaid Falcon (pinkish brown) Upon close inspection, I noticed 1 of the 6 pairs I had purchased (Falcon) were defective. On the back leg, it appears as if holes were accidentally cut in the pants and then attempted to be "patched" by resewing it back together. (see pictures) Very disappointed that Amazon would allow this and have reordered/requested a return on this one pair. Because of the issue and trouble, I am deducting 1 star.

Weird cut for me! I ordered these capris in white and in “Sedona,” and they fit differently. First, I currently weigh 160 lbs and waist and hip dimensions are 34” and 40” respectively. I am wearing a size 14 in all photos. The “Sedona” denim had bizarre extra fabric in the rise as well as the hip areas. See how the rise puckers up into extra layers beneath my waist in the photos showing me sitting down? SO not flattering! The extra hip fabric is visible in the photos of me standing. (BTW, same shade of capris, but sitting down pics had a lot of natural light and standing photos had barely any natural light.) Anyway, maybe I just have funky measurements, but my “Sedona” capris puckered out on both sides of the hip region. I now seem to recall having the same issue with Lee’s Easy Rider jeans. For whatever reason, I don’t see the same extra fabric in the white capris; those are keepers. Hope this review and photos are to someone!

Flattering pants but sizing chart is incorrect I have other colors from this Lee style & wanted the tan color. Despite the material being the same these are a heavier feel. The other colors are more lightweight. Due to weight gain I opted for a size 12 regular rather than a 10. The sizing chart doesn’t fit my body shape at all. Based on their sizing chart with my hips being 45” & my waist being 39” I should’ve ordered a 14 or 16 so take that into account. I’m also 5’6” & weigh 175 pounds. The size 12 regular fits me perfectly as I attached pictures to reflect. The material is 98% cotton & 2% spandex. There is very little stretch to the pants. The material is soft. I launder in cold water on the delicate cycle. I don’t dry these out of fear these will shrink due to the cotton. These do come out of the washer extremely wrinkled despite taking them out immediately. In hanging them to dry the wrinkles don’t come out. These definitely need to be ironed each time. I feel these pants are of quality & have longevity as my other pants truly have. The price is well worth what I paid. I do recommend just be aware of the sizing.

Very disappointed that this didn't fit me how i'd like, they're so comfortable! Okay, so I bought these pants because I needed them for a work/volunteer gig. I had high expectations because I have had other Lee pants and I really want them to fit properly. I'm 5'3 and 110-113lbs, not exactly the curviest person around. I ordered a 4-Short. So...they do fit at the waist. Perfectly, I might add. Not tight, but definitely not loose. Very comfortably, exactly like how I think a pants should fit. However, they are both VERY baggy on my relatively skinny legs and a little bit long even though I bought the short version. When I put it on, I loved how comfortable they were, but disappointed how baggy and unkept they looked on me. I would get a 2, but I doubt the waist would fit. For my short queens (those sub 5'3), you might want to look elsewhere. I have a feeling they'd look rather long on y'all. They are otherwise brilliant pants and great Lee quality. Such a bummer.

Fantastic pants. Let me start with stats: I'm 5'6" 150lbs (athletic build, or pretty straight up n down build but spreading with age 😭so maybe I'm an apple now, lol. Anyway...), a 36DD Bust (usually M top,large in button ups typically) , a 29"waist (usually size 6 in jeans/pants or 4, brand makes a big difference there), and a 31" inseam. I got these in a 6 and if say they for well. There's plenty of stretch to the very soft fabric making these comfortable and flexible. They definitely just fit me comfortably in the waist line (no belt needed) any tighter they'd be uncomfortable. these also are definitely midrise pants which at my age I prefer. The length is perfect for flat or low heel shoe. I washed and dried these and did not notice any shrinkage. They came out softer and ready to wear. My use for these is for camping. Great value. These seem like great everyday pants as well. Breen color is pretty. Brownish green. True to product picture. I would also say these are a they're straight leg pant. These for over my ugh boots snugly, over a hiking boot nicely and with athletic shoes without dragging. I highly recommend these at this price.

Size depends on color I tried the Charcoal, Plaid, Black, and Flax. They also were shorter than the other colors in the “short” size (the others laid at the bottom of my shoes without dragging, which I prefer. The black and flax were up a bit higher). I gave 4 stars because what I kept are comfortable with decent stretch. It’s just trial an error finding the right size/color combination.

Size depends on color I tried the Charcoal, Plaid, Black, and Flax. Not sure but will try.... The pants received are slightly different than what was shown... I received higher waisted pant that has a zipper with a double button closure... since higher waisted, Waist sizing is fitted... if you have a defined waist no problem but if have no waist definition (or a short torso) go a size up or if you are in between sizes go with the larger of the two sizes... I wear size 8, am 5’2 with a short torso but have a slightly defined waist so I am ok wearing my normal size (but is snug) and the pants are perfect length to wear with heals (think business casual)... I have thick thighs so the pants a slightly fitted in the thigh but still flattering. the pant is very flattering on your bootie, in the advertisement you can see the model is slender and flatters, still is true for us full bottom beauties! material is kinda rough... Again I have thick thighs, so I am concerned the material will make noise as I walk... (oh dear, the sound of 70’s corduroy pants is popping in my head... best way to describe the sound these pants make) For the price, worth keeping to wash and wear... worse case I can give pants to one of my taller friends that has thinner thighs lol

Extremely flattering and comfortable Levi’s gets it right with curvy clothes. I have the worst time finding pants that fit my thighs and butt but also my waist. I have very aggressive dimensions, 44x36x50, and I typically have to wear leggings everywhere. But when I need to be a grown up and look professional, but still feel like a woman, these pants did the job and fit like a dream. For context, I’m about 5’7”, 250 lbs, with ridiculously thunderous thighs and booty. These pants fit and flattered with no significant waist gap. I will be getting more styles.

Very comfortable and fit like a “glove” These pants are perfect for casual wear or work wear! Straight leg, mid rise and regular fit! The length (long) is perfect for me. True to size (6) for reference I am 143 lb and 5’7”
I am 5’8” but have a long torso as opposed to legs. The regular length fits perfect and the size 2 is great on my waist, which is about 25 inches.

Length shrinks A LOT I am not a tall person by any stretch of the imagination -- I'm 5'3" on a good day. I ALWAYS have to order short/petite pants or hem up regular pants by several inches. Having seen the reviews on these saying that they shrink, I went ahead and bought the regular length ones. Goodness, am I happy I erred on the side of caution. They started with a 29.5" inseam (see photo). After two or three wash and dries, they've shrunk 2" up to a 27.5" inseam. They're nearly too short for even me, but they'll work. That aside, they fabric is thick, soft, and high quality. It's perfect for work, lays flat, doesn't wrinkle, and it gives a nice silhouette for curvy hips/bum. They're also pull-ons for extra comfort, and they have front pockets. I really do like them and I'll be getting several more pairs for work, but I feel bad for anyone taller than me.

Nice, but not for my body type These pants are the “pull-on formal” type with no fly/ button, a thick elastic waist and detailing like overstitched pockets and belt hooks to make them work-appropriate. The fabric is of pretty decent thickness but if I kept them I’d probably need shapewear to cover the vpl and lumps and bumps. I ordered them in long, as the current fashion for flood pants doesn’t suit me, and they would be just about right with a modest heel (I’m 5’6” and somewhat short waisted). I have a small waist and big booty, but I was hoping they would work for me due to the stretch and elastic waist but they gaped really badly at the back. If I went down a size they would probably fit better at the waist but I wouldn’t want them to be any tighter on the hips for work pants. I returned them because of this but think the quality and style are good.

Very comfortable! Excellent quality Not sure why these pants got low reviews. But these Lee pants are legit comfortable. My measurements: waist 36 inch, hip 44 inch. Material is a lil on the heavier/thicker side but soft and quality. Good stretch throughout. Try it out! I ended ordering more colors after this one.

New Favorite Pants! I, like most women, have trouble finding pants that fit right. For me, these pants are the perfect fit. I am tall and hourglass shaped. I'm 5' 8 1/2" tall and 133 lbs, and I bought a size 6. 1 - They are long enough without being too long. 2 - They go in at the waist, like I go in at the waist. I have a problem with a gap hanging open in the small of my back, and pants that go in enough to accommodate my not inconsiderable asset in the back without squeezing the bum too much, but also going in where I go in. These fit perfectly. 3 - They have good stretch to them, and are very comfortable. 4 - The material (at least this color) is not wrinkly, but not too stiff either. 5 - Body skimming without being baggy nor too tight. 6 - They are high waisted for tucking in, without being up in your ribcage as some high waisted pants are. These sit right below my belly button.

Pockets Are Real I’m 5’4 and a size 12/14 in most pants. I ordered a 14 just in case they’d fit small around the waistband and I was right. 14 fit perfect though. I should’ve gotten a 14 short despite what some of the reviews said since I’m not wearing them with heels. I’d say if you’re under 5’6 and not wearing heels with these, then go with short sizing or you’ll have to hem them like I do. The pockets are very real. They are business pants, not your average pants. You have to remove the little white seam inside the pocket. Gently pull at one of the corners of the pocket and keep pulling until the string is out and the pocket is open. Or you can be less patient like I am and just take scissors to the seam, but you’ll have to be very careful not to cut the pocket. I can’t speak on durability as I just received them yesterday and will be wearing them to work today, but they feel like they’ll hold up for a while. I’m ordering more soon in another color as well.

Comfortable, very loose fit for all-around wear. On me, baggie more than relaxed. The fit on these pants is soft, comfortable, and to size, although they lean quite baggie not relaxed - Lee Relaxed Fit Straight Leg Pant - Flax - 8. Most notable is the very generous cut for the back of the thighs. I’m 5’5” 130 lbs. and ordered a size 8 and it’s a definite baggie fit on me. However, the waistband, torso and upper rear area lean to a firmer fit, so going down in size may not be an option. (This whole design really takes me to a past decade.) The length is also generous. I can wear these pants with sneakers, but they do have that bigger casual front crease. If I go to 2.5” heels, the length is almost perfect. (See pics.) The Flax color is a nice beige hue. They can dress up, but I can also rely on their durability, they are well made. The material holds it shape well, and there is some give. Note: They do shrink up just a bit (waistband, torso) after the initial wash. YMMV.

Love them I love these way these feel and fit. The cotton is really soft, seams appear solid. The color is bright and they are just comfy. I really could lounge in them, feel like pjs. I'm 5ft 200lbs and bought a size large. Although I liked and wanted the relaxed feel, I could've purchased a small and would still comfortably fit in them. I have a lot of wiggle room and stretch, even in the waistband, which has a drawstring. I would knot the ends to prevent string from going in hole when washed. The waistband stretches another 5-6 inches out for me and my belly is 41 inches. I prefer the gathering at the ankles because tight fits around the ankle aren't flattering on me but with that being said a tall person can rock these maybe up to 5'5 or 5'6. I prefer the roominess of the large as it hides my fupa. I bought four designs and only have to return one as the red plaid has a hole in the right pocket. Oh, it has pockets! Not shallow but not deep enough to hold cell phone unless you had a tighter fit, like if I had gotten the small the phone would fit snug. They are not transparent so no worry about underwear showing. They are lightweight. I'll update the breathability later as I live in Florida and it gets hot. They have found a repeat customer. I'll update after I do my first wash to see how it holds up. Good value for the money.

Comfy Cozy Pants All Day Long I have these pants in 3 patterns, and I've bought more patterns for my friends too because I just love these comfy cozy pants! At 5'4" and 150lbs, the large size fits me comfortably. The medium was a little snug with less freedom of movement. The pockets are an okay size, not too shallow but definitely not very deep. The fabric material is sooo soft and has held up to being washed regularly. Since I've bought so many of them, I noticed the cut of the print is not the same each time, so you do run the risk of having a giant bullseye print right on your crotch or butt, or having the pattern repeat in a weird way also drawing attention to the crotch. So, I've had to return a few pairs as well for bad cuts of the patterns, or for having holes in the pockets or ankle cuffs. These pants are cheap, afterall. But I can't get enough of 'em!

Great Joggers! I absolutely loved these joggers as soon as I opened the package - as per previous reviews they are incredibly soft, the length was perfect. I ordered the grey camo print and the color was exactly as displayed on my monitor. I normally wear size 0-2 and ordered Medium in these pants as most of the reviews mentioned to size up; that didn't quite work for me - they were too baggy in the waist as well as hips most likely because I'm not that curvy (waist - 25" and hips - 36"). I am exchanging them for size Small - I will update the review when the new pair arrives. UPDATE: After receiving the new pair in size Small I can honestly say that these are my favorite joggers ever (I'm going to order more in different colors and prints and I won't break the bank doing so :)). The fit is great : not tight, not baggy, the fabric is thin, buttery soft and washes well (the pants kept their shape after a few washes). The waist band is very elastic and the drawstring is easily removable if that's your preference. The one thing I would mention about these pants is that on my frame (5'4") they sit above my belly button, which would make them high-waisted; if you're much taller than me, that may not be the case for you.

Thrilled I took a chance on this product. I was skeptical about less than 15 dollar pants, not gonna lie, but these are better than I hoped. Pardon the mess but I wanted to show the length and my room is a wreck. I’m 5’7 and 130 lbs and took a chance on size small, and they fit like a glove. Waistband feels secure but has some give, and length(which I was most worried about) is just right, resting on my ankle bone. There is a good bit of stretch too, so I can sit or lay however and they don’t feel stressed or pulled too tight. I do wish there was a little more slouch in the calves above the bands, but purely for aesthetic reasons. They’re also really, really soft. Thin but the kind of soft that’s inherently warm. I’ve ordered two more!

Different fabrics for different styles… I ordered two pair of these (black) and then black with orange zipper pocket. I LOVE the black with the zipper pocket but shockingly they fit and feel very different. I took pictures but it’s hard to see. The zippered ones are a more structured fabric (like a track pant) where as the others are basically a yoga pant material in jogger style. Both are nice but I got these bc I wanted something less clingy on my booty to be able to wear for errands etc. after workouts. The yoga material still was very clingy to the booty. All that being said both were nice and comfortable and decently long. I’m 5”10 with long legs and 170 pounds. Normally I wear a size 8 or medium. I got the large. I also like them overall enough to try some of the other jogger options. If they had more colors in the zipper options or even in that fabric I’d buy several more.

Super cute & comfortable!! I bought these leggings to wear camping. They are extremely comfortable. The material is very soft and stretchy... also kinda thin, so it's nice and cool to wear. The pattern is so cute with little images of bears, owls, foxes, trees, etc. Perfect to wear around our campsite. I thought the area behind the legs was a little to loose for me, but everyone has different body types. The waste line is spacious, the ankles hem line is not tight... overall happy with how it fits. Perfect So glad to have found these. They’re soft, lightweight, comfy and look nice. I would go with the Amazon recommendation on size. They recommend XL and usually on stretchy materials I do size L but I’m glad I went with their suggestion. Fits great. I’m 5’7” and am super loving that these are nice and long. So often joggers fit like capris so ankle length feels luxurious to me.

Wish these came in XS… 5 star quality joggers for sure. I’m 5”1 and 105 lbs and I got size small. I was worried they’d be too big because I’m an XS at Old Navy and they are. Granted, I kept them and flip the waistband over once, but I do that on almost all my athletic wear because I’m short, anyway. They’re made out of athletic weight brushed polyester- which, for those of you who liked the old LuLaRoe leggings before they turned to crap, picture that buttery soft fabric, but nice and thick and stretchy with really good drape. They’re solid and opaque, the and the seams are finished well—way better quality than you should expect for $12. I got 3 pairs and I’m pretty sure that isn’t going to be enough. They’re drapey and slim fitting, so you can get the put together “athleisure” look, rather than the “I’m wearing pajamas in public” look. They’re super cute with a solid tee or a tank- really easy to style. Definitely several notches above sweatpants, but with all of the comfort. The gold zippers on this pair make them even more stylish. Pardon my pic- it’s midnight and I’m already comfy in a hoodie but you get the idea. EDIT- after reading a bunch of reviews from customers my height and weight, the consensus is that they’re definitely not for petites. I wish these came in XS because I have no doubt they’d be perfect then.

I was looking for an in between of buttery and wind breaker material not so much cotton or fleece and these are perfect. Legging-like but comfortable. I just got these pants today and tried them on as soon as I got them! I was so excited after reading reviews but that nervous excited because of the negative reviews also. So they weren’t 110% to my expectations. I was looking for a sweatpant type because I love lounging around and I wear sweatpants EVERYWHERE I go. My first thought when I took them out of packaging: wow these are extremely soft!! Once I put them on, I noticed they were kind of... long.. in the crotch area? So I was like okay, I will pull them up more. I pulled them up more (literally above my belly button and even folded the top of them) and then I had a CAMEL TOE lol 😭 but I pulled them down very slightly and it seemed to help but it still has some extra room in the crotch area. I bought a medium, medium is my normal size. I wear a size 9 in pants and weigh 140 pounds, 5’3 in height. I would definitely be buying a small if I decide to buy with this company again. As others have said, it is a lot like a legging material. I get the name of the company literally has LEGGINGS in the name lol, but the title of the pants are joggers so I assumed it would be less leggingy. I just hate leggings because I have panty lines from them and haven’t yet found a decent underwear that is comfortable, safe for my bits, and is a no show. 🤦🏼‍♀️ Over all I am rating a 4 stars because they are super soft, super comfortable, and I will still definitely be wearing them! I am writing this review on 5/29/20 and will update if anything happens to them!

Once I put them on, I noticed they were kind of... long.. in the crotch area? So I was like okay, I will pull them up more. I pulled them up more (literally above my belly button and even folded the top of them) and then I had a CAMEL TOE lol 😭 but I pulled them down very slightly and it seemed to help but it still has some extra room in the crotch area. I bought a medium, medium is my normal size. I wear a size 9 in pants and weigh 140 pounds, 5’3 in height. I would definitely be buying a small if I decide to buy with this company again. As others have said, it is a lot like a legging material. I get the name of the company literally has LEGGINGS in the name lol, but the title of the pants are joggers so I assumed it would be less leggingy. I just hate leggings because I have panty lines from them and haven’t yet found a decent underwear that is comfortable, safe for my bits, and is a no show. 🤦🏼‍♀️ Over all I am rating a 4 stars because they are super soft, super comfortable, and I will still definitely be wearing them! I am writing this review on 5/29/20 and will update if anything happens to them!

Omg these are my new favorite pants! I want to wear them every day! They are buttery soft, fit just right, flattering, and perfect for wearing out to run errands or sleeping in as pjs. I was skeptical for $14.99 but I recently broke my pelvis and needed some comfortable pants that were easy to get on, comfortable to wear, don’t put any pressure on my mid section, pelvis or legs, thick enough to wear out of the house, and don’t show off my flabby tummy. These are perfect! The medication I’ve had to take has caused me to gain 60 lbs in the last 6 months so not only did I have no clothing that fit, my body shape has changed drastically (the meds actually redistribute body fat in several areas so almost every article of clothing I’ve tried on has looked terrible except these). I love that they have pockets and the waist is just high/fitted enough so I don’t look like I have a bloated tummy but not so high that I look like I’m wearing old lady clothing (I’m 41). For reference I’m currently 5’6 1/2” about 180 lbs and I bought size Large.

I love these pants true to size. Nice length and fit at waist. I love these joggers they are Fab! I was hesitant to buy them because there was a lot of mixed reviews but thought I would risk it. For reference I am 5 foot 7 and I weigh 159 and wear a size large. I have long legs and a long torso so I was afraid these would be too short because a lot of the joggers say there length are only 28 inch or so and I have a 30 inch inseam. They fit great with length right down to the ankle their high waisted fit above my belly button which is important for me because I have a tummy and it eliminates muffin top. For me they are very true to size. They are a nice fitted look. The only reason I did not give them five stars as they have a slight baggy crotch with cameltoe so you can't tuck in a shirt with them. For the price they're really great. They are a very light material so great for spring and summer. I will definitely be ordering more of these. UPDATE returning these beacause of the camel toe issue. My shirts are not long enough to cover it. Otherwise love these

Joggers for tall women These Womens joggers were perfect for me. I bought them to go with a bright 80’s jacket for a costume and fell in love with them. They are buttery soft and the pink color is very vibrant. I am 5’10” and I always struggle to find bottoms that are long enough. These hit right at the ankle and are super stretchy. They have an elastic waist but also have tie strings for adjusting. Bonus, they have pockets!

Love these! I’m 5’3 and 140 lbs and the medium fits perfectly! Fabric is super soft and love the pockets. Some reviews say the fabric is thin, but that isn't the case with the polka dotted pattern. I couldn’t see through them when held up to the light. I was worried that the elastic would be tight on my stomach but it’s super comfortable.

Too Thin material. Fit like sweatpant drawstring but leggings stretch with pockets. linty. problem obviously with the tag being sewn in two places that caused the butt seam and the waist seam to be connected and stretched weird. It wouldnt have been a major issue and i wouldnt have minded figuring out cutting the tag off if the pants werent also see thru. The fabric feels like the kind that would quickly fuzz up after washings and catches lots of lint. Super thin stretchy material. Strange combination of thin legging material and feel but sweatpant look?? Awkward. it is a rather a bit see thru for being leggings maybe the color has something to do with it besides the material also being very thin. Got the marigold yellowish color, which is nice. After checking carefully the size chart from the seller, I bought a one size up because i wanted the pants more flowy instead of leggings skin tight, and it still fit a way tad snug on the hips. They would be okay as leggings if worn with a longer shirt to cover bum. They fit like a sweatpants but unfortunately the material is way thin. So thin that you can read a nature valley granola bar thru them. I don’t think they look right on me plus the issue with the same one tag opposite edges, being sewn in to two different seams as of they’re trying to make you choose to cut the tag off based on where you prefer the tag to stay. Weird. ! so im returning. For ref I bought 1xl and my hips are 44, waist 37 and im a bit under 5’2short. Other items I got from leggings depot were great. This one missed the mark.
Soft and long length I am 5’11” 185lbs, size 12. Ordered XL and fits great. A little loose but makes them comfortable. Material very soft and longer length! Would recommend for taller women.

Really great for the price! So comfy! For size reference, I’m 5’9” and 130 lbs. I orderEd the medium and that’s a good fit for me. These are pretty cute!!! And super comfortable. So buttery soft!!! And I also love the giant pockets. My only complaints would be 1. The top is a lot looser than the bottom. I realize that is now joggers are supposed to be, but if there could be just a tadddd less fabric in the top, then I think these would be absolutely perfect. And 2. I had hoped I would be able to dress these up for work (I’m a teacher). But I’m not sure if that is doable. The fabric is so soft and loose that I’m just not sure I can pull it off! They do resemble more like sweatpants. But they are PERFECT for running errands or wearing around the house or throwing on after work. Very casual. I’m going to order some more colors for fall (wine and olive) because you seriously cannot beat this price! And I have hopes that I’ll somehow be able to dress those up a little more? We will see!

Wrong color, panty lines, SOFT material They are super soft. This specific color does not seem see through or transparent as some have said. They’re thin but opaque. I’m 5’2, 119 lbs and I got the small, happy with the size. I have a short torso so they ride sort of high, right under my belly button. I wish the didn’t ride so high but on a person with medium torso-length, it would probably sit a couple inches under the belly button. I’m really disappointed with the order because as you can see in my picture, the color isn’t quite as expected. I bought these for the off pink shade but it has a strong purple undertone. I took it under a light that gives a little more of an orange-y yellow hue so it cancels the purple a little, but it just doesn’t look that pink, it’s too dark. The label says “mauve” which is more or less correct. The draw string was also supposed to be white which would have been cute but you’ll notice it’s the mauve color instead. It’s probably a hassle to return them so I won’t bother because they’re at least soft. Another bit you’d want to know is that the smooth material tends to cling in the butt, so I got panty lines wearing these. It would be better with a thong or thin, seamless underwear.

They fit a little loose but these things are so dang comfortable I can’t even be mad about it. When they say they’re smooth like butter they are not lying. Besides them being a bit loose I love them. *Update* I’ve had these for a whole 2 years now, they’re still in great condition, haven’t gone see through or warn down in any way and still smooth like butter, I wear them consistently so they’re WELL used. Like I stated previously they were too big when I ordered, but I have gained weight and they fit perfectly now so I would *still* order a size down, Im still 5’4 and weigh 240 with a 40’’ waist and 56’’ hips and 2xl fits me perfectly. Tall fat girl says BUY THESE I bought these bc the design was super cute and I've been trying to find replacement joggers for an old pair that's falling apart. As a gal with a gut, if you're trying to hide your stomach or look for compression then these probably aren't what you're looking for. The elastic might also be too tight for some people who wear a 3X like me, depending on how you carry your weight, but that could prob be fixed by taking out the drawstring. The pockets are also comically tiny, but will (barely) hold my phone when I am moving from one room to another (wouldn't recommend anywhere else bc...it barely fits). HOWEVER!!! That said, these are so soft and so comfortable that I'll basically live in them anyway. I'm already trying to decide which design to get next, I might collect them all! They keep me warm but they're not hot, perfect for spring/summer. I'm 5'11" and the length is still great on me which is usually not the case for any pants I buy online. Highly, highly recommend these.

Well made but... Hey Amazon. This is an entirely unbiased and unpaid for product review. I got these for a cosplay I was doing (Pearl from Steven Universe). I originally meant to cut these into shorts however I kept them long because of weather. These came within a day with my student Prime which was excellent. The product was very well packaged and immediately I noticed how soft these were. They were the texture of a very thin velvet almost and the color stayed saturated even at my joints. Trying them on they fit all the way down to NY ankles. I'm 5'2" so I didnt really have any concerns, however, I believe a taller person would have problems with leg length because even for a short person these didn't go awfully long. Now although the material was very very soft it was also very thin. My butt was covered in my costume however I wouldn't recommend wearing these (at least the light colors) by themselves. Wearing standard underwear the pattern and crease of the underwear was very obvious and looked pretty bad. I think even with a thong it'd be way too obvious. So if you plan to wear these by themselves get a darker pair because the lighter pair won't satisfy your needs. With them being so thin they didn't keep in heat very well either and I was pretty cold even inside. Also, I developed a small hole near my crotch after dancing in them for a while, probably due to constant stretching. Overall I believe these are decent quality. I would consider buying a black pair as leggings if the material would be made a little thicker. If you need something for under a skirt or shorts then these are absolutely perfect. TDLR; Soft and well made but too thin

Great fit for a pregnant belly! :) To say I love leggings is an understatement. I mean, let’s be honest: what woman doesn’t like being comfortable? I have an active 2 year old boy, and am 27 weeks pregnant with my second child on the way! I bought these and started wearing them early on in pregnancy (when the belly wasn’t really showing) and they fit well. I didn’t buy them with pregnancy in mind, just for general comfort/ looking for a style that was a good midway point from a super loose jogger and a legging. BUT they have surprising been phenomenal during pregnancy as well. So many of my other leggings and shorts have been stowed away but I honestly can see these lasting my whole pregnancy! The band is a TRUE high rise on me- which I really, really appreciate. The material is true work out/ sweat wicking and thick enough to be squat proof. I’ve been very active in these and they’ve held up well so far! I am 5’1 and have gained about 16 pounds in pregnancy so far(staring weigh: 114lbs, currently ~130lbs). I purchase the small (which was the smallest size I saw available when I went to purchase it) and they fit great. Since I’m short they are just at/above my ankles length wise which I love. They basically fit me like a normal jogger should with no bunching. I personally don’t anticipate the belly band portion being stretched out too much/ to the point where it won’t fit well post pregnancy and therefore think these will last me well till the very end, and then after baby arrives! But at this price point I’d be happy to buy another pair if need be! I’m a staunch believer in not keeping ANY clothing piece that you don’t LOVE how they fit. And I wouldn’t be keeping these unless they checked all the boxes. They do! If this helped you out in any way, let me know with the “ ” button below my comment. Happy shopping, lovely ladies. :)

Great Jeans, Wrong Size. Beware “Amazon Size Recommendation!” Update: Got the right size (25), attached a new image of the fit above. Original review: I couldn’t decide between getting a size 27 or 28, and Amazon’s recommended size was 28. I got the 27 because it would arrive in time for my outing, and it still ended up being way too big (I prefer skinny/bootcut jeans to fit tightly), so the Amazon recc would’ve been way off. The Amazon size recc is based on just your height and weight, not all your measurements, and it messed up another order of Levi’s jeans I got, so I would trust the size guide instead! Other than that, the jeans are amazing. They’re comfortable levels of stretch while still being thick and giving quality, the deep blue color in combination with the contrasting thread in the “Casting Shadows” color is just 🧑‍🍳💋 chefs kiss perfect. Also, in the last image you can see it has the Levis stamp and tag so it’s definitely official, at least my variant! It also comes from the official Levis store and Amazon is strict against impersonators so I don’t think this is fake like that one reviewer claimed. Some Levi’s jeans have a tag that’s just red, without the logo, I own some of those. So yes! Buy it, but make sure to order the right size! My measurements: Height: 5’7 Weight: 120lbs Natural waist: 27 in Belly button: 29 in Hip: 33 in Butt: 37 in

Reliable Levi's, though the Amazon size listing is confusing I’m 5’3”, 118 lbs and ordered 28 reg based on another pair of Skinny Levi’s I have. Based on the confusing chart for ordering, I wasn’t sure what I would end up with. I got a 28/32 that also says 6 Medium ( guess that translates to the “reg”). I would say the fit in this Vintage pair is comfortable, if not a bit roomy, but at least not tight or restrictive. Length is perfect. I ordered color StayPut, and the actual version is not as faded looking as the pic online, but I like what I got just as much if not better. I really like the flare (ready for a change from the skinny). I also ended up with the actual “vintage” version, which has really cute 3 row stitching on back and front pocket. I noticed at least one review pic had the traditional 2 row Levi stitching, so odd that you don’t know what you’re getting from this particular listing. Anyway, I am very satisfied with my purchase, with a very fair price for new Levi’s.

Garment Shrank 4 inches after washing! These are Misses size 16 Short. I am 5'6" 170lbs. I ordered two pair. I tried one of them on and they fit, except were too big in the back waist (as expected). I usually order short, since that is exactly the right length because I never wear heels with jeans. These were the right length and fit in the leg and all around. I planned to have the waist taken in, but decided to wash one pair, to see what the shrinkage was and whether or not I should wash the other pair before having the alteration done. I thought they might shrink some, but was really floored to see how much they shrank! Now, they are way too short and too tight in the legs and too small all around. I have never had this problem before. I am returning them.

A 33” jean is 38” circumference? Wtf is with this sizing?! So, my waist is 33”. Since I didn’t have any option to choose a size, I chose 33” waist. I am usually a size 9. These jeans are a 16. I am beyond confused. So fyi, 33” jeans mean 38” waist.

Do everything jean I'll write a review because I know it can be for other ladies on a quest for good jeans. I've probably ordered 10+ pairs on Amazon and these are the winners. My measurements: 28" waist. 37" hip. 30" inseam. About 5'3". 133lbs. Athletic build. 30 yr old mom of a toddler. These jeans stay up without a belt. They are mid rise just below my belly button. I gave up on low rise cut jeans awhile ago because I hate having to yank them up every hour. The Levis website says these are relaxed through the hip and thigh. But if you are an athletic build and occasionally do outdoor chores in these pants, I found that they fit great and still flatter. I dislike skinny jeans because I prefer to wear my pants over my winter/fall boots without looking awkward. These bootcut jeans are a rare find in the skinny jean saturated market. Material is medium weight cotton blend, not paper thin. Decent cold weather jeans. I may wear leggings underneath if the temps drop lower in NH. Hope this helps another lady who needs "do everything" jeans.

SHORT, BIGGER GIRLS—buy these jeans! My body type: 5’ 5” and 242# with short, thick legs and a belly. I’m not familiar with this type of sizing, so I went with a 38 Short (I usually wear a 20 short in “regular” sizes). These jeans feel like they were made just for me! The material is very soft and stretchy, and the bootcut isn’t too flared at the bottom. The front of the pants can be pulled up over my belly without riding too far up in the “front” (if you know what I mean). Also, the back of the jeans doesn’t gap out if you pull them up in the front which I LOVE! I have washed and dried these jeans which made them shrink a bit, but the stretchy fabric allows them to still fit and loosen up after a bit of wearing. Highly recommended for my shorter, bigger girls out there!!!

I’ll most likely need to go up to, at least a 34. Very unfortunate. Although, returns are super easy, it’s still inconvenient!

Freaking Jackpot! Normally when I buy jeans, I spend 20-30 minutes crying in front of the mirror in a self loathing kind of way. I’ve suffered from BDD since I can remember and pants, jeans especially, have always been my biggest nemesis. All that being said… sweet baby Jesus these jeans are the freaking epitome of comfort. For reference, I’m 5’4”, 130lbs and haven’t seen the inside of a gym in probably 10 years and my ass is clearly nonexistent — I purchased the Maui Views (Waterless) in a size (4) 27 Short. Unpopular opinion but I detest high waisted jeans. I’m “short torso - long leg” so hard pass on anything that even gets near my belly button. I was nervous about the rise of these but am so glad I took the chance because they truly sit just perfectly under my belly button and at my waist. I’ve never felt so comfortable and honestly, like myself, in a pair of jeans. I could not recommend a pair more than these. *only hiccup in my purchase was that the original pair claimed to have been delivered but was in fact not. Amazon customer service quickly rectified that and sent a replacement pair overnight.

My favorite pair of jeans So I decided to get these 311 shaping skinny jeans because I have a pair of light blue 311s. I really like the way this style of Levi’s fits my figure. I really appreciate that the fabric is soft and stretchy . It may seem as if they are tight but I just ate. The only weird thing to me was the Levi’s label in the back of the pants. It just seemed like it was going to easily tear off.

Solid pair of skinny jeans! Bought these on sale, would buy again (on sale). I’m 5’6”/158lbs, typically a size 8-10, I bought the 29 Regular and they fit well! Fit perfectly! I have very long skinny legs and have a really hard time finding stretch jeans that fit my legs and small butt. These jeans are PERFECT!! I haven’t bought jeans in years, been wearing the same ones, but it was time to get some new ones as my old ones were showing their wear :( I’ve always worn American Eagle jeans, but I don’t have a store close to me so decided to go to the lovely Amazon and browse jeans. I HATE jeans shopping—HATE it. I’m extremely picky how jeans should fit me. I am tall, so they need to be long. And also I like them to be stretchy so I can move around. After 30-45 minute of browsing, I narrowed it down to these Levi’s! After reading the reviews I decided to give them a try. OMG am I glad I did. I got size 8L and these things fit me perfectly! They are comfortable, they are long enough, these are now the BEST jeans I own! I want to get more, but there aren’t many colors available in my size, so waiting for a restock. For reference I am 5’8 and weigh about 135ish. GET THE JEANS :)

Jeans labeled wrong Jeans would of been great if not labeled wrong. As you see I ordered a 31 waist. Well it for my daughter perfectly. She is a 26 Waist. So unfortunately she wants to keep them. So I'm out of a pair of jeans and money!

Perfect fit and amazing quality As someone who is considered “midsize” and not quite plus, it’s often difficult to find jeans and even harder to find good reliable reviews to help gauge fit when shopping online. Decided to try these out when I saw them on sale… I didn’t own any Levi’s jeans before this. I read the reviews and based on that, I sized up… I’m 5’5” and typically wear a 14 or 16 regular, depending on the brand. They didn’t have any regular length left, so I thought I’d try a 34short and return if it didn’t work out. Shipping was extremely fast and I received them the next day. I immediately tried them on and the fit is PERFECT 🙌🏻 Just enough stretch to feel comfy and perfect length. Glad I sized up too because they fit like a glove 👍🏻 I prefer high rise jeans and these hit at a comfortable spot for me. Highly recommend. Great quality and the perfect staple for any wardrobe. I agree with the recommendation to size up so keep that in mind when ordering.

Soft black jeans have a decent amount of stretch I purchased these in Soft Black in 29x30 (FYI I'm 5'6", 155lbs). I wasn't sure if I should buy these in 30x30 since I read some reviewers say these have no stretch, but I others said these were as stretchy as jeggings. Maybe it depends on which color jeans, and the dye that's used. I am between sizes since I lost 10lbs & most of my 30's are now too big on me, except 1 pair of corduroys from Levi's. By comparison, these have a decent amount of stretch, but they're not as stretchy as my Lucky brand or Liverpools. Still, I used my best judgment purchased 29's and I'm glad I did!

These are to die for! I'm roughly 6ft 175lbs waist is 34"-35" and hip is 44"-45" I bought the 33 Long. It's nearly impossible to find jeans that fit without paying upwards of a hundred dollars. Especially considering there's a 10" margin between my waist and hips. I'm genuinely impressed at how well these fit! The material is stretchy yet durable. These jeans are super comfy and flattering which is very rare! You will absolutely love these jeans! They aren't super skinny so you can walk instead of waddling! You could always size down if you wanted the super skinny leg. Take your measurements and invest, you won't be disappointed!

Loooove these! So! These pants are perfect, they fit amazing and they are flattering. I am 5’4, 120 lbs, waist is 26” and hips 35”, I got a size 4 and 32” long. I am weird and like my skinny jeans long! They wrinkle a bit at the bottom, just how I like! Amazon suggested I get a size 6 but I knew a 4 would be right! These are great pants and I love them! The only thing is the pockets are small, but hey that’s women’s jeans for you lol. Would definitely recommend!

Just OK "jeans" I was really excited to get these pants but was a little less than wowed when I tried them on. Here's why: These pants comfortably fit right at the belly button. I got size 26 short (I am 5'3 & 119lbs) I think I could have gotten a size smaller and it would have been a better fit, because when you sit down in them you get this weird bag in the crotch, but I'm sure they'll be nice after a wash or two. They didn't really feel like denim, though, more like a "jegging" mix. The exact blend is: 68% cotton, 23% polyester, 8% viscose, 1% elastane (compared to a pair of older Levi's 524 blend: 99% cotton, 1% elastane) Another thing to note- they pick up every piece of lint and dog hair around, but that's kind of expected since they are black pants, after all.

Steady Rock & Blue Story I’m 5’6”, 170 pounds and gain weight in my post menopausal belly and the size 31 fit great. I bought these because I love Levis and these don’t disappoint. The material has stretch and the Rise long, going all the way to my belly button. The rise with the stretch is so effective that it sucks my belly in and helps give me shape. The stretch is also comfortable. The leg is definitely skinny, almost like a legging. Colors in my pics are blue story- a medium dark blue and steady rock- gray with Lighter faded areas on legs Definitely recommend

Comfy & Stretchy These are surprisingly comfy jeans that fit me. They have really good stretch and I think the color is similar to the product picture. I’m 5’2 and the length is perfect. My only complaint is that the front pockets are not deep. Can only really fit a chapstick in them.

Cobalt Overboard goes all the way to ankle unlike description photo Im 5’9 169 postpartum and these are my first pair of postpartum pants/jeans and after just trying them on Im SO HAPPY (size 31 regular). Even though I ordered Cobalt Overboard the packaging says Marine Overboard but it’s fine because they fit beautifully and go all the way to my ankle which was a pleasant surprise because the photo makes it look like they’re shorter and have more of a capri look. I prefer the longer look actually and can just roll them if I need/want to.

I finally found some black levis that fit. These are the blackest black levis I have ever seen. They are soft and stretchy and they fit great (My butt is lifted up). As I mentioned in another review (Levis 311 skinny plus, kabekona camo), I currently wear an 18 in Womens Levis 505 which run larger than most levis so I sized up to a size 22 to be safe. I probably could have gotten away with a size 20 with these but they are so comfortable (I don't want to take them off). However, I did not have to hold my breath to button them or wiggle into them, they just slid on, so I'm happy. I will also have to wear a belt with these which is always the case for me. For reference: I am 5"5 with thick hips, butt, and thighs and currently weigh around 260 and my stomach is not flat but it is smaller in proportion to the rest of my body.

The last picture shows how the waist fit on me- I dont think they're too tight not too loose. I am often between a 24 and 25" so this skinny mid to high waist I chose the 25. They are a little long but or reference I'm only about 5'1" and 100lbs si it is expected I'll wear heels or get them altered.

Much Longer cut than 721 (High Rise Skinny) I have a pair of 721's (High Rise Skinny) in Soft Black that I wear all the time, and the fit is perfect, so I figured the same size in 724 (also Soft Black) would be a slam dunk! But they are at least 3-4 inches longer! Why, Levi's? Why? The material is exactly the same, the cut is just waaaay longer. For reference, I'm 5'6 145 lbs and size 30 fits great- snug, but stretchy! In Soft Black at least. I've found every wash is a different amount of stretch so this review is for Soft Black only! Update- Chelsea Hour Dark Indigo had a similar fit to Soft Black, although a tad shorter. Even though they're cropped, they're way too long. Even the 10S hit the top of my foot and bunched slightly. They might shrink a bit after washing. Verdict: returning. Update 2: Chelsea Pier Dark Indigo - pics in the ad are super misleading. These are a thin extra stretchy denim - like super stretchy jegging material - and the 10S/30S brushed the top of my feet. The model for this style must be 12' tall bc they look like crops of her! The color is a very cute blue but only shoe I could wear these with is a flip flops or they'll bunch up
Love these!! These are super cute and stretchy. I’m 5’2, 155lbs and a 30 fits me perfect. Love these, I will definitely be getting more

I was hoping that they’d have pockets that I can fit at least my phone in but alas not. I posted one pic with my hand in the pocket and it reaches the end.

I like the 27 or 29 inch inseam option High waisted fit, elastic and strong, Drawstring tight, you can’t go wrong, Flowy wide leg, with a side slit game, Fashion and movement, you’ll never be the same. Two deep pockets, hold what you need, Phone, keys, cards, stash with speed, Easy storage, on the go, These pants got style, let it show. Two lengths to choose, check the size chart, 27 or 29, pick it smart, Comfort and flow, all wrapped tight, These wide-leg pants, they hit just right.

Poorly made. Faux zipper off centered to the right. These pants are a cheap quality version of slacksweats. They fit pretty well, and are comfortable on, but as soon as I put them on, I immediately noticed that the waist is not centered. You can see in my pictures that it’s off to the right, which is even more noticeable when they’re on. It’s a definite no for me because that’s the only thing I can focus on now; just how much off center they look. So for them to look right with the faux zipper centered on the tummy, I’d have to twist them to one side, which throws off the whole fit of the pants due to the other seams and pockets. Looking back at the other reviews, I can see that many of the other colors are cut/sewn the same way, but may not be as noticeable in a darker color. Bottom line, just not worth it when there are so many other better options with more flowy material that’s not this weird rough waffle weave anyway. I prefer a more sophisticated look, but wanted a pull-on comfortable dress slack. This is definitely not it, even if the seams did line up. FYI, I am 5’6, 190lbs, wear a size medium pant, and these were true to size in a medium.

These pants are great for the office or a casual night out. They are a little snug at the waist, and a bit short in length, but overall they look pretty cute. For the most part they are comfortable to wear. The fabric feels like it's a good quality, and I really like the width of the pant legs. I'm 5'1, 130lbs, and I ordered a size medium.

Lightweight, Comfortable, Breathable & Versatile Palazzo Pants I ordered these because I’ve got about 10 pairs of Libin’s cargo joggers, which I love, and these palazzos looked fun. (For reference, I’m 5’2”, 36 inches at the waist, 41” bum). After trying them on here’s my impression: PROS: 1. 100% polyester, so they won’t shrink. The outside is a textured waffle weave that is 100% breathable, but the inside is smooth and soft. 2. SIzing is Spot on. I went by both my usual Libin size (XL) and their sizing chart (36 in waist), and super happy I did. These don’t pinch at the waist, but the top part of the pants aren’t excessively baggy. 3. Versatile and cute. I was just going to try them on this morning, thinking that they’d be great for church, but then I ended up wearing them on today’s errands. Considerations: 1. These are Palazzo pants, meaning there’s a lot of material in the leg. If you’re like me (a bit chunky, significant bum) you’ll want to wear these with a fitted top, to maintain a feminine silhouette. 2. The 27 inch inseam just works on my 5”2” frame (but I have a short waist and long legs for my height). Shoes keep them just off the ground. Anyway, I’m a fan.

Waffle knit, grey, comfortable pants I’m 5’5” and these are a great length for me. They feel like wearing pajamas for sweatpants but look very nice and business professional. I was afraid they would snag easily but they don’t. The grey is neutral but pretty. I wore a wide belt and it fit the holes well. Pleats on me aren’t the most flattering, but that’s what made them so comfy and I didn’t feel they were unattractive on me.
